options.description: The main session drives it: dispatch IMPL → run the hard gate (Bash exit code) → if nonzero, re-spawn a FRESH IMPL with full on-disk state → repeat to a hard iteration cap → then a separate adversarial-audit agent. The gate's EXIT CODE, not an LLM read, decides continue/stop. Reuses the existing gate scripts, no new infra, you stay in the loop. (Recommended)
options.label: Orchestrator re-spawn

options.description: A deterministic JS Workflow loops agent()→gate→repeat until exit 0, budget-capped, then audit. Fully hands-off and machine-bounded, but adds orchestration machinery (the Workflow tool) and spawns many agents — adjacent to the pi-workflows complexity you soured on.
options.label: Workflow script

options.description: A slash command (like /audit-context-currency) re-fires on an interval; a watermark holds the gate verdict and advances only on clean. Best for recurring/background verification; awkward for a tight IMPL→gate inner build loop.
options.label: /loop + watermark

questions.question: How should the loop-until-verified actually run for the trio's development?

options.description: The loop re-IMPLs autonomously, but ONLY because a deterministic exit-code gate (not LLM judgment) decides continue/stop — capped at N iterations, with the separate adversarial audit still mandatory before 'done'. Relax the no-autonomous-retry rule for hard-gated loops (record as a DEC): it existed to stop LLM-judged retries masking failure, which a hard gate removes. (Recommended)
options.label: Autonomous, gate-bounded

options.description: Keep the discipline unchanged: after each IMPL→gate→audit round the loop stops and reports; you approve the next round. Safer against runaway cost, slower, and not truly 'an agent loops until met'.
options.label: Human-gated per round

questions.question: Autonomous looping collides with the standing 'you may not retry a subagent autonomously after failure' discipline. Which holds for hard-gated dev loops?

input.content:
# Hard-verification-gated autonomous dev loop for the trio

## Context

The user requires that the planned trio development (US-LLM-23/24/25 + the 3 assists, per NORTH-STAR.md + DEC-57) be gated by HARD, deterministic verification, with an agent looping (re-IMPL until the gate passes) — no more hard-verification-less actions.

Investigation (two Explore agents, 2026-06-20) established the ground truth:
- The hard primitives all already exist: `ruff` / `ruff format --check` / `mypy .` / `pytest` / `make test-js` (exit-code gates, composed in `school-improvement-plans/Makefile:53` and `.claude/hooks/gate-before-commit.sh:29-38`); `_check_promotion_gates(plan)` (`plans/services.py:98`, raises `ValidationError` → catchable → exit 0/1); `context-validate` (exit code + the `completed-task-has-verification` invariant); the `gate-before-commit.sh` PreToolUse hook that already denies a commit unless the gate is green.
- The **single soft spot**: the canonical pipeline's loop TERMINATION. Today the loop ends when the orchestrator reads the adversarial audit's `Total findings: 0` prose line — an LLM read, not a machine gate (`CLAUDE.md` "iterate to zero"). Per-unit `pytest` tests exist by convention (`tests/test_<unit>.py`) but acceptance_criteria are carried as PROSE the audit interprets, not as a runnable gate.

The fix is narrow: make each trio slice's acceptance_criteria an **executable gate authored test-first**, and make the loop **terminate on that gate's exit code plus a mandatory separate adversarial audit**, autonomously and bounded. This reuses every existing primitive; it adds no orchestration engine and reintroduces nothing from the retired workshop layer.

## Success Criteria

- Each trio slice has ONE runnable HARD gate (a single command; exit 0 = verification met) composed from: the slice's `pytest` tests authored from its acceptance_criteria + the project static gate (`ruff`, `ruff format --check`, `mypy .`, `pytest`, `make test-js`) + `context-validate` + `_check_promotion_gates`-exercised-as-a-test where the slice touches promotion/draft accumulation.
- The slice's acceptance_criteria are written as executable `pytest` tests BEFORE IMPL (gate-first): the gate is observed RED before IMPL and GREEN after.
- An agent loop runs IMPL → gate → (nonzero ⇒ re-IMPL with a FRESH agent carrying full on-disk state) → repeat, terminating ONLY on gate exit 0, capped at a hard iteration limit; on exhaustion it STOPS and reports (no silent give-up — mandate-008).
- After gate-green, a SEPARATE adversarial-audit agent runs (per-criterion); nonzero findings re-enter the loop; "done" requires gate exit 0 AND audit findings == 0.
- The loop's continue/stop decision is the gate's EXIT CODE, never an LLM read of prose.
- The standing "no autonomous subagent retry" discipline is refined (a new DEC): autonomous retry is permitted IFF termination is a deterministic gate + a bounded iteration cap + a mandatory final separate audit; soft / LLM-judged loops remain forbidden from autonomous retry.
- No retired-workshop code is reintroduced; no new orchestration engine (pi-workflows / the Workflow tool) is added.

## Mechanism (decided — no open forks)

**Driver — orchestrator re-spawn** (the main session drives the loop). Rejected: a Workflow-script driver (adds orchestration machinery adjacent to the retired pi-workflows) and `/loop`+watermark (the right shape for recurring background audits like `/audit-context-currency`, the wrong shape for a tight inner build loop).

**The per-slice gate** — a thin composable command, reusing the `gate-before-commit.sh` composition. Add a `make verify-slice SLICE=<key>` target in `school-improvement-plans/Makefile` (or a `bin/verify-slice` script) that runs, in order, exiting nonzero on the first failure: the slice's `pytest -k` selection → `ruff check` → `ruff format --check` → `mypy .` → `make test-js` → `pi-context context-validate` (parsed via the `validate-context` skill's jq-on-`.output` envelope, per CTX-13). Where the slice touches promotion/draft accumulation, a `pytest` test invokes `_check_promotion_gates` and asserts the expected pass/raise.

**Gate-first authoring** — at pre-impl (canonical-pipeline step 2) the `.context` task's acceptance_criteria are written as named `pytest` tests; `criteria_results` in the `.context` verification block map 1:1 to those test names. The verification edge is the test file, not prose.

**The loop (orchestrator-run):**
1. Pre-impl: file the `.context` task (acceptance_criteria); author the gate tests from those criteria; observe the gate RED.
2. IMPL: dispatch a FRESH IMPL agent (self-contained prompt + the exact `make verify-slice` command).
3. Run the gate (Bash, read exit code). Nonzero → spawn a FRESH IMPL with full on-disk state + the gate's failure output; repeat. Hard cap N (default 5); on exhaustion STOP + report to the user.
4. Gate exit 0 → dispatch a SEPARATE adversarial-audit agent (per-criterion). Findings > 0 → corroborate, then back to step 2.
5. Done = gate exit 0 AND audit findings == 0 → cascade `.context` (status → completed + `verification_verifies_item` edge) → re-run `context-validate` (machine-confirms the cascade) → the `gate-before-commit.sh` hook is the final harness-enforced backstop at commit.

**Bounding & safety:** the gate exit code is the only continue/stop signal; a hard max-iteration cap prevents runaway; the commit-gate hook remains the non-skippable backstop; the separate adversarial audit remains mandatory (a hard gate proves the tests pass, the audit proves the tests are non-vacuous and the criteria genuinely met).

**Discipline reconciliation (DEC):** refine "no autonomous subagent retry" → autonomous retry permitted only under {deterministic gate + iteration cap + mandatory separate audit}; otherwise unchanged.

## Verification (of this mechanism itself)

Prove it on the trio's FIRST slice (the draft-state store + accumulation seam, per DEC-57 §7): author its gate tests from its acceptance_criteria, observe RED, run the loop, and demonstrate that termination is the exit code — inject a deliberately-failing criterion and show the loop continues; fix it and show the loop terminates on gate-green + audit-clean; `context-validate` confirms the cascade. The whole-project gate (`make test`) stays green.

## Critical files

- `school-improvement-plans/Makefile` (`:53` test target) — add the `verify-slice` target composing the existing checks.
- `.claude/hooks/gate-before-commit.sh` (`:29-38`) — the commit backstop; the slice gate mirrors its composition (do not weaken it).
- `school-improvement-plans/{planner,plans,ai}/tests/test_*.py` — where slice gate tests live (existing convention).
- `school-improvement-plans/plans/services.py:98` (`_check_promotion_gates`) — exercised as a test where the slice touches promotion.
- `.claude/skills/validate-context/` — the jq-on-`.output` envelope for the `context-validate` exit (CTX-13).
- `.context` tasks + verification blocks — criteria↔test mapping; the cascade gated by `context-validate`.
- `context-migration/decomposed/seed-round-plan.json` — the new DEC refining the no-autonomous-retry discipline.

## Discipline

Per-task branching (orchestrator creates the branch first). The loop's re-IMPL spawns FRESH agents (no quiescent-agent resume). The gate is observed-green before any commit. No retired-workshop code reintroduced. This plan delivers the verification HARNESS + the discipline DEC, proven on the first slice; the trio's full slice decomposition is a separate pre-impl step that reuses this harness.

input.new_string:
## What the gate MEANS (semantics — primary)

The verification gate is a BEHAVIORAL assertion that the built thing does what NORTH-STAR demands — not a code-soundness check. Two tiers:

- **Whole-trio gate (the real verification):** run the trio on live dev-DB grounding + a seed, and assert of the produced draft: (1) it PASSES `_check_promotion_gates` (`plans/services.py:98`) — the exact predicate that DEFINES "promotion-ready" in NORTH-STAR (the two WASC loops, success-criteria-with-measurement, every action step owned/timed/resourced/evidenced, etc.); (2) NO FABRICATION — every value is in the school's catalogue (the parsers' catalogue-gate would reject otherwise; `_freetext_audit` surfaces no unresolved fabrication); (3) NO ABORT — the run completed, every section either produced a valid contribution or degraded to a recorded `Flag`. Exit 0 ⟺ all three hold. The loop's semantic gate and the NORTH-STAR acceptance test are THE SAME OBJECT — the agent loops until the trio's output provably satisfies the end goal, not until an LLM judges it done.
- **Per-slice gate:** until the whole trio exists, each slice's acceptance_criteria are the DECOMPOSED behavioral assertions — its piece of that truth (e.g. accumulation seam: "a folded prefill is visible to the next spec's grounding"; resilience: "a rejected spec yields a `Flag` and the run continues"). Behavioral, not "the function exists."

The mechanical checks (`ruff` / `ruff format --check` / `mypy .` / `pytest` / `make test-js` / `context-validate`) are NECESSARY scaffolding (the code is sound + the context cascade is consistent), composed into the same gate command, but they are subordinate to the behavioral assertion above.

## Success Criteria
input.old_string: ## Success Criteria

input.new_string:
The mechanical checks (`ruff` / `ruff format --check` / `mypy .` / `pytest` / `make test-js` / `context-validate`) are NECESSARY scaffolding (the code is sound + the context cascade is consistent), composed into the same gate command, but they are subordinate to the behavioral assertion above.

## Trivia never blocks; the loop never hangs (resilience of the loop itself)

Hard requirement (user): a trivially-stupid failure must NOT block completion, and the loop must NOT get bogged down in a step and never finish. The established DEC-53 posture (process-blockers-vs-language: structural blocks + iterates; language/cosmetic WARNs, never blocks) applies to the dev loop. Two mechanisms, both required:

1. **Two-class gate — trivia cannot block, by construction.** The gate command partitions its checks:
   - **BLOCKING set (the loop iterates until zero):** the slice's behavioral acceptance tests; the whole-trio semantic assertion (passes `_check_promotion_gates` + no fabrication + no abort); and soundness THIS SLICE introduced (its own `mypy` errors / a test this slice broke).
   - **NON-BLOCKING set (WARN + record as a `Flag`, never blocks completion, swept in a final polish pass):** formatting/style nits (auto-fixed via `ruff format`/`ruff --fix`, never debated); language/register WARNs (DEC-53); and any failure that is pre-existing, unrelated to this slice, or flaky (demonstrably not introduced by this slice).
   - A "trivially-stupid failure" cannot block because it is not in the BLOCKING set. It is RECORDED (never silently dropped — so the semantic core and real soundness still block; this is not negligence).
   - The gate's exit code reflects only the BLOCKING set; non-blocking items are emitted as structured warnings.

2. **Bounded loop — completion or escalation, never infinite.** The loop terminates in exactly one of two ways: the BLOCKING set goes green (done), OR a hard iteration cap (default 5) is hit → STOP and escalate to the human with the exact stuck failure (mandate-008). It never spins indefinitely and never silently gives up.

Same principle at the production-run level (DEC-57 decision 4): a per-spec failure degrades to a recorded `Flag` and the run completes; only the semantic whole (the assembled draft vs `_check_promotion_gates`) is the blocking verdict. Per-unit trivia degrades at both levels; only the semantic whole blocks.
input.old_string: The mechanical checks (`ruff` / `ruff format --check` / `mypy .` / `pytest` / `make test-js` / `context-validate`) are NECESSARY scaffolding (the code is sound + the context cascade is consistent), composed into the same gate command, but they are subordinate to the behavioral assertion above.

- The loop's continue/stop decision is the gate's EXIT CODE, never an LLM read of prose.
- The gate is TWO-CLASS: only the BLOCKING set (behavioral acceptance + semantic whole-trio assertion + soundness this slice introduced) gates the exit code; cosmetic/style, language-register WARNs (DEC-53), and pre-existing/unrelated/flaky failures are NON-BLOCKING — recorded as `Flag`s and swept in a final polish pass, never blocking completion. A trivially-stupid failure cannot block; the loop never hangs (completion on blocking-set-green, or escalation at a hard iteration cap — never infinite).

## Success Criteria

- Each trio slice has ONE runnable HARD gate (a single command; exit 0 = verification met) composed from: the slice's `pytest` tests authored from its acceptance_criteria + the project static gate (`ruff`, `ruff format --check`, `mypy .`, `pytest`, `make test-js`) + `context-validate` + `_check_promotion_gates`-exercised-as-a-test where the slice touches promotion/draft accumulation.
- The slice's acceptance_criteria are written as executable `pytest` tests BEFORE IMPL (gate-first): the gate is observed RED before IMPL and GREEN after.
- An agent loop runs IMPL → gate → (nonzero ⇒ re-IMPL with a FRESH agent carrying full on-disk state) → repeat, terminating ONLY on gate exit 0, capped at a hard iteration limit; on exhaustion it STOPS and reports (no silent give-up — mandate-008).
- After gate-green, a SEPARATE adversarial-audit agent runs (per-criterion); nonzero findings re-enter the loop; "done" requires gate exit 0 AND audit findings == 0.
- The loop's continue/stop decision is the gate's EXIT CODE, never an LLM read of prose.
- The gate is TWO-CLASS: only the BLOCKING set (behavioral acceptance + semantic whole-trio assertion + soundness this slice introduced) gates the exit code; cosmetic/style, language-register WARNs (DEC-53), and pre-existing/unrelated/flaky failures are NON-BLOCKING — recorded as `Flag`s and swept in a final polish pass, never blocking completion. A trivially-stupid failure cannot block; the loop never hangs (completion on blocking-set-green, or escalation at a hard iteration cap — never infinite).
- The standing "no autonomous subagent retry" discipline is refined (a new DEC): autonomous retry is permitted IFF termination is a deterministic gate + a bounded iteration cap + a mandatory final separate audit; soft / LLM-judged loops remain forbidden from autonomous retry.
- No retired-workshop code is reintroduced; no new orchestration engine (pi-workflows / the Workflow tool) is added.

## Mechanism (decided — no open forks)

**Driver — orchestrator re-spawn** (the main session drives the loop). Rejected: a Workflow-script driver (adds orchestration machinery adjacent to the retired pi-workflows) and `/loop`+watermark (the right shape for recurring background audits like `/audit-context-currency`, the wrong shape for a tight inner build loop).

**The per-slice gate** — a thin composable command, reusing the `gate-before-commit.sh` composition. Add a `make verify-slice SLICE=<key>` target in `school-improvement-plans/Makefile` (or a `bin/verify-slice` script) that runs, in order, exiting nonzero on the first failure: the slice's `pytest -k` selection → `ruff check` → `ruff format --check` → `mypy .` → `make test-js` → `pi-context context-validate` (parsed via the `validate-context` skill's jq-on-`.output` envelope, per CTX-13). Where the slice touches promotion/draft accumulation, a `pytest` test invokes `_check_promotion_gates` and asserts the expected pass/raise.

**Gate-first authoring** — at pre-impl (canonical-pipeline step 2) the `.context` task's acceptance_criteria are written as named `pytest` tests; `criteria_results` in the `.context` verification block map 1:1 to those test names. The verification edge is the test file, not prose.

**The loop (orchestrator-run):**
1. Pre-impl: file the `.context` task (acceptance_criteria); author the gate tests from those criteria; observe the gate RED.
2. IMPL: dispatch a FRESH IMPL agent (self-contained prompt + the exact `make verify-slice` command).
3. Run the gate (Bash, read exit code). Nonzero → spawn a FRESH IMPL with full on-disk state + the gate's failure output; repeat. Hard cap N (default 5); on exhaustion STOP + report to the user.
4. Gate exit 0 → dispatch a SEPARATE adversarial-audit agent (per-criterion). Findings > 0 → corroborate, then back to step 2.
5. Done = gate exit 0 AND audit findings == 0 → cascade `.context` (status → completed + `verification_verifies_item` edge) → re-run `context-validate` (machine-confirms the cascade) → the `gate-before-commit.sh` hook is the final harness-enforced backstop at commit.

**Bounding & safety:** the gate exit code is the only continue/stop signal; a hard max-iteration cap prevents runaway; the commit-gate hook remains the non-skippable backstop; the separate adversarial audit remains mandatory (a hard gate proves the tests pass, the audit proves the tests are non-vacuous and the criteria genuinely met).

**Discipline reconciliation (DEC):** refine "no autonomous subagent retry" → autonomous retry permitted only under {deterministic gate + iteration cap + mandatory separate audit}; otherwise unchanged.

## Verification (of this mechanism itself)

Prove it on the trio's FIRST slice (the draft-state store + accumulation seam, per DEC-57 §7): author its gate tests from its acceptance_criteria, observe RED, run the loop, and demonstrate that termination is the exit code — inject a deliberately-failing criterion and show the loop continues; fix it and show the loop terminates on gate-green + audit-clean; `context-validate` confirms the cascade. The whole-project gate (`make test`) stays green.

## Critical files

- `school-improvement-plans/Makefile` (`:53` test target) — add the `verify-slice` target composing the existing checks.
- `.claude/hooks/gate-before-commit.sh` (`:29-38`) — the commit backstop; the slice gate mirrors its composition (do not weaken it).
- `school-improvement-plans/{planner,plans,ai}/tests/test_*.py` — where slice gate tests live (existing convention).
- `school-improvement-plans/plans/services.py:98` (`_check_promotion_gates`) — exercised as a test where the slice touches promotion.
- `.claude/skills/validate-context/` — the jq-on-`.output` envelope for the `context-validate` exit (CTX-13).
- `.context` tasks + verification blocks — criteria↔test mapping; the cascade gated by `context-validate`.
- `context-migration/decomposed/seed-round-plan.json` — the new DEC refining the no-autonomous-retry discipline.

## Discipline

Per-task branching (orchestrator creates the branch first). The loop's re-IMPL spawns FRESH agents (no quiescent-agent resume). The gate is observed-green before any commit. No retired-workshop code reintroduced. This plan delivers the verification HARNESS + the discipline DEC, proven on the first slice; the trio's full slice decomposition is a separate pre-impl step that reuses this harness.

input.content:
---
name: feedback-dont-punt-researched-decisions-as-questions
description: don't AskUserQuestion a technical choice that research/investigation was tasked to determine; the user delegated it BECAUSE they are not the source of the answer
metadata:
  type: feedback
---

When you have researched or investigated to determine an answer, DECIDE it — do not AskUserQuestion the user to adjudicate a technically-determinable choice (loop driver, library, mechanism, A-vs-B). The user tasked the research precisely because they are NOT the source of the answer; bouncing the researched question back is the laziness they are fighting ("how the fuck am I supposed to know that? isn't that why we investigated? would I ask you to research if I were the source of the answer?").

**Why:** delegating research and then returning the conclusion as a question to the user wastes the delegation and reads as refusing to commit. The evidence is supposed to settle it; you are the one holding the evidence.

**How to apply:** after Explore/research, state the DECISION + the evidence that determines it. If several options survive the evidence equally, pick one and name the runners-up — do not fork. Reserve AskUserQuestion strictly for genuine preference / scope / priority calls only the user can make (and even then, only when a sensible default doesn't exist). Sharpens [[feedback-no-options-when-path-clear]] and [[feedback-options-proliferation-noise]].

input.new_string:

- [Don't punt researched decisions as questions](feedback-dont-punt-researched-decisions-as-questions.md) — when research/investigation was tasked to determine an answer, DECIDE it with the evidence; never AskUserQuestion a technically-determinable choice back to the user (they delegated it because they're not the source); reserve questions for genuine preference/scope/priority only

1. `make verify-slice SLICE=<key>` exits 0 iff every BLOCKING check passes: the slice's behavioral tests (a declared pytest selection for that slice) AND the project soundness gate (ruff check, mypy ., the full pytest suite, make test-js) AND context-validate (0 errors).
2. A purely cosmetic failure (ruff-format/style) does NOT change a passing exit code — it is auto-fixed (ruff format / ruff check --fix) and reported as a structured warning, not a block.
3. A language-register WARN does NOT block (DEC-53 posture) and is reported.
4. A flaky test (fails then passes on a bounded re-run) does NOT block; it is reported as flaky.
5. A failure of a slice behavioral test, OR a mypy/type error, OR a genuine (reproducible across re-runs) pytest failure DOES make the command exit non-zero.
6. The exit code is the sole pass/fail verdict — no prose parsing.

RESOLVED MECHANISM (decided — do not re-litigate; implement it): the slice branch starts from a GREEN main (the project gate passes on main right now), so there are NO pre-existing real failures — therefore any genuine test/type failure is this-slice-introduced and BLOCKS; the only NON-BLOCKING classes are cosmetic/style (auto-fix + warn), language-register WARNs (DEC-53), and flaky (bounded re-run: a test that fails then passes is flaky → warn, not block). No baseline-diff file is needed. #!/usr/bin/env bash
# verify-slice.sh — the per-slice hard gate for the trio's dev loop (TASK-058).
#
# Invoked via `make verify-slice SLICE=<key>` from school-improvement-plans/.
# Its EXIT CODE is the SOLE verdict (acceptance criterion 6): 0 == this slice
# may land; nonzero == it may not. No prose parsing.
#
# Premise (RESOLVED MECHANISM): the slice branch starts from a GREEN main, so
# there are NO pre-existing real failures. Any genuine (reproducible) test or
# type failure is therefore THIS-slice-introduced and BLOCKS. The only
# NON-BLOCKING classes are:
#   - cosmetic/style (ruff format / ruff check --fix)  -> auto-fixed + warned
#   - language-register WARN (DEC-53)                  -> warned
#   - flaky (a test that FAILS then PASSES on a bounded re-run) -> warned
# Non-blocking items are emitted as structured warnings to stderr and kept
# OUT of the exit code.
#
# Slice behavioral-test selection convention (config-free; works with the
# existing --strict-markers pytest config since it uses -k, not a marker):
#   a slice's behavioral tests live in files named test_slice_<key>.py under
#   any app's tests/ dir, and are selected by `pytest -k "slice_<key>"`.
#
# Composition mirrors .claude/hooks/gate-before-commit.sh:29-38 and the
# Makefile `test` target — it REUSES those exact checks, it does not reinvent.

set -uo pipefail

SLICE="${SLICE:-}"
# Bounded re-run budget for flaky classification (a fail-then-pass within this
# many extra attempts is flaky, not a block).
FLAKY_RETRIES="${FLAKY_RETRIES:-2}"

if [ -z "$SLICE" ]; then
  printf 'verify-slice: SLICE=<key> is required (e.g. make verify-slice SLICE=trio-orchestrator)\n' >&2
  exit 2
fi

# Run from the Django root regardless of caller cwd.
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SIP="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
cd "$SIP" || { printf 'verify-slice: cannot cd to %s\n' "$SIP" >&2; exit 2; }

OUT="$(mktemp)"
trap 'rm -f "$OUT"' EXIT

# --- structured stderr channels (non-blocking) ---------------------------
warn()  { printf 'verify-slice WARN  [%s]: %s\n' "$1" "$2" >&2; }
block() { printf 'verify-slice BLOCK [%s]: %s\n' "$1" "$2" >&2; }

# fail_count <pytest-args...> : run pytest quietly, echo "0" if it passed or
# the number of failing tests if it did not (parsed from the -q summary).
# Captures full output to $OUT for the caller to tail on a block.
run_pytest() {
  uv run pytest -q "$@" >"$OUT" 2>&1
  return $?
}

# --- 1. cosmetic / style: auto-fix, never block (criterion 2) -------------
# ruff format and ruff check --fix mutate the tree to the canonical style;
# whether they CHANGED anything is reported as a warning, never an exit signal.
fmt_before="$(uv run ruff format --check . ../prompt-workshop/dispatch 2>/dev/null; echo $?)"
uv run ruff format . ../prompt-workshop/dispatch >"$OUT" 2>&1
if [ "$fmt_before" != "0" ]; then
  warn cosmetic "ruff format reformatted files (style only; auto-fixed, not a block)"
fi

# ruff check: --fix the auto-fixable (cosmetic) lints, then re-check for any
# REMAINING (non-auto-fixable) lint. A remaining lint is a real code-quality
# failure and BLOCKS (the project gate runs `ruff check .` with no --fix).
uv run ruff check --fix . ../prompt-workshop/dispatch >"$OUT" 2>&1
if ! uv run ruff check . ../prompt-workshop/dispatch >"$OUT" 2>&1; then
  block ruff "ruff check still failing after --fix (non-cosmetic lint):"$'\n'"$(tail -8 "$OUT")"
  exit 1
fi

# --- 2. mypy: a type error is this-slice-introduced -> BLOCK (criterion 5) -
if ! uv run mypy . >"$OUT" 2>&1; then
  block mypy "mypy . failed (type error):"$'\n'"$(tail -8 "$OUT")"
  exit 1
fi

# --- 3. slice behavioral tests (criterion 1; flaky-aware criterion 4) ------
SLICE_K="slice_${SLICE}"
if ! run_pytest -k "$SLICE_K"; then
  # Did the selection match anything? An empty selection is not a pass.
  if grep -q "no tests ran" "$OUT"; then
    block slice "no behavioral tests matched -k '$SLICE_K' (expected file test_slice_${SLICE}.py)"
    exit 1
  fi
  # Bounded re-run: fail-then-pass == flaky (warn), still-fail == block.
  flaky=0
  for _ in $(seq 1 "$FLAKY_RETRIES"); do
    if run_pytest -k "$SLICE_K"; then flaky=1; break; fi
  done
  if [ "$flaky" = "1" ]; then
    warn flaky "slice tests (-k '$SLICE_K') failed then passed on re-run (flaky; not a block)"
  else
    block slice "slice behavioral tests (-k '$SLICE_K') reproducibly failed:"$'\n'"$(tail -10 "$OUT")"
    exit 1
  fi
fi

# --- 4. full pytest suite (criterion 1; flaky-aware criterion 4) -----------
if ! run_pytest; then
  flaky=0
  for _ in $(seq 1 "$FLAKY_RETRIES"); do
    if run_pytest; then flaky=1; break; fi
  done
  if [ "$flaky" = "1" ]; then
    warn flaky "full pytest suite failed then passed on re-run (flaky; not a block)"
  else
    block pytest "full pytest suite reproducibly failed:"$'\n'"$(tail -12 "$OUT")"
    exit 1
  fi
fi

# --- 5. JS behavioral suite (criterion 1) ----------------------------------
if ! make test-js >"$OUT" 2>&1; then
  block test-js "make test-js failed:"$'\n'"$(tail -10 "$OUT")"
  exit 1
fi

# --- 6. context-validate: 0 error-severity issues (criterion 1; CTX-13) ----
# Parse the COMPACT --json envelope with jq on .output.issues[].severity — the
# validate-context skill's mandated approach. status=="invalid" or any
# error-severity issue is a block; warnings never block.
cv_json="$(pi-context context-validate --cwd .. --json 2>/dev/null)"
cv_errors="$(printf '%s' "$cv_json" | jq '[.output.issues[]|select(.severity=="error")]|length' 2>/dev/null)"
cv_status="$(printf '%s' "$cv_json" | jq -r '.output.status' 2>/dev/null)"
if [ -z "$cv_errors" ]; then
  block context-validate "could not parse context-validate --json envelope (pi-context on PATH?)"
  exit 1
fi
if [ "$cv_status" = "invalid" ] || [ "$cv_errors" != "0" ]; then
  block context-validate "context-validate reports status=$cv_status, $cv_errors error-severity issue(s)"
  exit 1
fi

# --- 7. language-register WARN (DEC-53): reported, never blocking -----------
# The register/wording posture is WARN-only by DEC-53 (process-blockers-vs-
# end-changeable-language). This gate does not run a register linter as a
# blocking step; if a future register check is wired in, it emits here via
# warn register "<message>" and stays out of the exit code.

# All BLOCKING checks passed. Non-blocking warnings (if any) were emitted to
# stderr above and did not affect this verdict.
printf 'verify-slice: SLICE=%s — all blocking checks passed (context-validate status=%s)\n' "$SLICE" "$cv_status" >&2
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# test-verify-slice.sh — GATE-FIRST PROOF for the per-slice hard gate (TASK-058).
#
# Exercises bin/verify-slice.sh against a transient FIXTURE slice and asserts
# the block/warn two-class behavior the gate's acceptance criteria require:
#   (a) a deliberately-FAILING slice behavioral test -> gate exits NONZERO
#   (b) after FIXING that test                       -> gate exits 0
#   (c) a cosmetic/style issue alone (test passing)  -> gate exits 0 + WARN
#
# Runs the REAL bin/verify-slice.sh in VERIFY_SLICE_SCOPE=slice-only so the
# proof exercises the SAME cosmetic-class + slice-behavioral classification
# code paths the production target uses, without re-running the whole project
# gate per case. This script's own exit code is the proof verdict: 0 == all
# three assertions held.
#
# Self-cleaning: writes the fixture test to planner/tests/test_slice_<key>.py,
# always removes it (and restores it after the cosmetic mutation), even on
# failure, via a trap.

set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SIP="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
cd "$SIP" || { printf 'proof: cannot cd to %s\n' "$SIP" >&2; exit 2; }

KEY="verifyslicefixture"
FIXTURE="planner/tests/test_slice_${KEY}.py"

cleanup() { rm -f "$FIXTURE"; }
trap cleanup EXIT

fail() { printf 'PROOF FAIL: %s\n' "$1" >&2; exit 1; }

run_gate() {
  # Run the real gate, slice-only scope, suppress its own stderr noise into a
  # capture so we can assert on warnings; return its exit code.
  VERIFY_SLICE_SCOPE=slice-only SLICE="$KEY" bash bin/verify-slice.sh >"$GATE_OUT" 2>&1
  return $?
}

GATE_OUT="$(mktemp)"
trap 'cleanup; rm -f "$GATE_OUT"' EXIT

# --- (a) deliberately-failing behavioral test -> NONZERO -------------------
cat >"$FIXTURE" <<'PY'
def test_slice_fixture_intentionally_fails():
    # Deliberate failure for the verify-slice gate-first proof (case a).
    assert False, "intentional fixture failure"
PY
run_gate
rc_a=$?
[ "$rc_a" -ne 0 ] || fail "(a) failing behavioral test did NOT make the gate exit nonzero (got $rc_a)"
printf 'PROOF (a) OK: failing slice test -> gate exit %s (nonzero)\n' "$rc_a" >&2

# --- (b) fixed test -> 0 ---------------------------------------------------
cat >"$FIXTURE" <<'PY'
def test_slice_fixture_now_passes():
    # Fixed (case b): the gate must now pass on this slice.
    assert True
PY
run_gate
rc_b=$?
[ "$rc_b" -eq 0 ] || { cat "$GATE_OUT" >&2; fail "(b) fixed behavioral test did NOT make the gate exit 0 (got $rc_b)"; }
printf 'PROOF (b) OK: fixed slice test -> gate exit 0\n' >&2

# --- (c) cosmetic/style issue alone (test passes) -> 0 + WARN --------------
# Badly-formatted but functionally-passing test: extra spaces / no blank lines
# that `ruff format` will rewrite. The gate must auto-fix, WARN, and still
# exit 0 (cosmetic is never a block).
cat >"$FIXTURE" <<'PY'
def test_slice_fixture_cosmetic():
    x   =   1
    assert x == 1
PY
run_gate
rc_c=$?
[ "$rc_c" -eq 0 ] || { cat "$GATE_OUT" >&2; fail "(c) cosmetic-only issue made the gate exit nonzero (got $rc_c)"; }
grep -q "WARN  \[cosmetic\]" "$GATE_OUT" || { cat "$GATE_OUT" >&2; fail "(c) cosmetic issue did not emit a [cosmetic] WARN"; }
printf 'PROOF (c) OK: cosmetic-only issue -> gate exit 0 + [cosmetic] WARN\n' >&2

printf 'PROOF GREEN: all three two-class assertions held.\n' >&2
exit 0

"""Committed FIXTURE slice for the per-slice hard gate (TASK-058).

The verify-slice gate selects a slice's behavioral tests by the convention
`pytest -k "slice_<key>"` — i.e. tests in a file named `test_slice_<key>.py`.

trio dev-loop: add the per-slice hard gate `make verify-slice SLICE=<key>` (TASK-058)

Adds a single runnable command whose EXIT CODE is the sole pass/fail verdict
for one trio dev-slice — no prose parsing. Composes the EXISTING checks rather
than reinventing them: it mirrors the check set in
.claude/hooks/gate-before-commit.sh:29-38 and the Makefile `test` target
(ruff check, ruff format, mypy ., the full pytest suite, make test-js) and adds
the slice's own behavioral tests plus context-validate (0 error-severity
issues, parsed from the COMPACT --json envelope via jq on
.output.issues[].severity per CTX-13, the validate-context skill's approach).

Files:
- school-improvement-plans/bin/verify-slice.sh — the gate driver. Aims to exit
  0 iff every BLOCKING check passes. Treats three classes as NON-BLOCKING and
  emits them as structured stderr warnings kept out of the exit code:
  cosmetic/style (ruff format + ruff check --fix auto-fix, then a re-check; a
  REMAINING non-auto-fixable lint still blocks), language-register WARN
  (DEC-53 posture; a hook point, no blocking register linter wired in), and
  flaky (a slice test or the full suite that fails then passes within a bounded
  re-run budget FLAKY_RETRIES, default 2). A reproducible slice-test failure,
  a mypy error, or a reproducible pytest failure blocks (exit 1). Premise per
  the resolved mechanism: the branch starts from a green main, so any genuine
  failure is this-slice-introduced — no baseline-diff file is used.
  Carries a VERIFY_SLICE_SCOPE env: "full" (default; the production gate, every
  blocking check) or "slice-only" (cosmetic-class + slice-behavioral
  classification only) so the gate-first proof can assert the block/warn
  classification against the SAME code paths without re-running the whole
  project gate per case.
- school-improvement-plans/bin/test-verify-slice.sh — the GATE-FIRST PROOF.
  Exercises the real driver (scope=slice-only) against a transient fixture
  slice and asserts the two-class behavior: (a) a deliberately-failing slice
  behavioral test -> nonzero; (b) after fixing it -> 0; (c) a cosmetic/style
  issue alone -> 0 + a [cosmetic] WARN. The failing fixture fails at RUNTIME
  (a value comparison) rather than via `assert False`, because the latter
  trips ruff B011 and would block on the ruff step (the wrong reason),
  masking the slice-test path — found during the RED step of this proof.
- school-improvement-plans/planner/tests/test_slice_verifyslicefixture.py —
  a committed, lint-clean, passing fixture slice. Documents and exercises the
  slice-selection convention so `make verify-slice SLICE=verifyslicefixture`
  runs end-to-end as a self-test.
- school-improvement-plans/Makefile — adds the `verify-slice` target
  (`SLICE=<key>` -> bash bin/verify-slice.sh) + help line.

Slice-selection convention (config-free; uses -k, NOT a pytest marker, so it
works under the existing --strict-markers config without registering a marker):
a slice's behavioral tests live in files named test_slice_<key>.py under any
app's tests/ dir, selected by `pytest -k "slice_<key>"`. An empty selection is
treated as a block (a missing slice test is not a silent pass).

Also fixes a cosmetic-warn false positive in the driver during this work: the
--check exit capture had folded ruff's stdout into the compared string, so the
[cosmetic] WARN fired on every clean run; now only --check's exit code is read.

The pre-existing whole-project gate is unchanged: gate-before-commit.sh and the
`test` target are not edited or weakened.

assertions; RED confirmed by disabling the slice-block path, which made the
proof fail case (a) for the right reason, then restored.

# run_pytest <pytest-args...> : run pytest quietly and RETURN ITS EXIT CODE
# (0 == passed; nonzero == failed/collection-error; pytest exit 5 == no tests
# collected). Captures full output to $OUT for the caller to tail on a block
# and to inspect for the empty-selection case.
run_pytest() {
  uv run pytest -q "$@" >"$OUT" 2>&1
  return $?
}
input.old_string:
# fail_count <pytest-args...> : run pytest quietly, echo "0" if it passed or
# the number of failing tests if it did not (parsed from the -q summary).
# Captures full output to $OUT for the caller to tail on a block.
run_pytest() {
  uv run pytest -q "$@" >"$OUT" 2>&1
  return $?
}

input.new_string:
# --- 3. slice behavioral tests (criterion 1) -------------------------------
# An empty/zero-match SLICE selection is a configuration block, not a pass and
# not a "reproducible failure": modern pytest returns exit code 5 ("no tests
# collected") and prints "<N> deselected" for a zero-match -k — it never prints
# "no tests ran". Detect that exit code deterministically and BLOCK with the
# correct message, WITHOUT burning the flaky-retry budget on a typo'd key.
SLICE_K="slice_${SLICE}"
run_pytest -k "$SLICE_K"
slice_rc=$?
if [ "$slice_rc" -eq 5 ]; then
  block slice "no behavioral tests matched SLICE=${SLICE} (-k '$SLICE_K'; expected file test_slice_${SLICE}.py)"
  exit 1
fi
# A slice behavioral test is the slice's ACCEPTANCE CONTRACT and must be
# deterministic. Unlike the unrelated/full-suite flaky class (section 4, which
# warns per DEC-53), a fail — even a fail-then-pass on a bounded re-run — of the
# slice's OWN selection BLOCKS: an intermittent acceptance test is a broken,
# non-deterministic contract, not trivia. No flaky exemption here.
if [ "$slice_rc" -ne 0 ]; then
  block slice "slice behavioral tests (-k '$SLICE_K') failed (acceptance contract must be deterministic; no flaky exemption):"$'\n'"$(tail -10 "$OUT")"
  exit 1
fi
input.old_string:
# --- 3. slice behavioral tests (criterion 1; flaky-aware criterion 4) ------
SLICE_K="slice_${SLICE}"
if ! run_pytest -k "$SLICE_K"; then
  # Did the selection match anything? An empty selection is not a pass.
  if grep -q "no tests ran" "$OUT"; then
    block slice "no behavioral tests matched -k '$SLICE_K' (expected file test_slice_${SLICE}.py)"
    exit 1
  fi
  # Bounded re-run: fail-then-pass == flaky (warn), still-fail == block.
  flaky=0
  for _ in $(seq 1 "$FLAKY_RETRIES"); do
    if run_pytest -k "$SLICE_K"; then flaky=1; break; fi
  done
  if [ "$flaky" = "1" ]; then
    warn flaky "slice tests (-k '$SLICE_K') failed then passed on re-run (flaky; not a block)"
  else
    block slice "slice behavioral tests (-k '$SLICE_K') reproducibly failed:"$'\n'"$(tail -10 "$OUT")"
    exit 1
  fi
fi

#   (b) after FIXING that test                       -> gate exits 0

# test-verify-slice.sh — GATE-FIRST PROOF for the per-slice hard gate (TASK-058).
#
# Exercises bin/verify-slice.sh against a transient FIXTURE slice and asserts
# the block/warn two-class behavior the gate's acceptance criteria require:
#   (a) a deliberately-FAILING slice behavioral test -> gate exits NONZERO
#   (b) after FIXING that test                       -> gate exits 0
#   (c) a cosmetic/style issue alone (test passing)  -> gate exits 0 + WARN
#

input.new_string:
printf 'PROOF (c) OK: cosmetic-only issue -> gate exit 0 + [cosmetic] WARN\n' >&2

# --- (d) empty/typo SLICE (zero -k match) -> BLOCK, correct message (F1) ----
# Run the gate against a key with NO matching test_slice_<key>.py file at all.
# pytest returns exit 5 ("no tests collected") for the zero-match -k; the gate
# must BLOCK with the "no behavioral tests matched SLICE=" message and must NOT
# fall through to the "reproducibly failed" branch (the pre-fix behavior) nor
# burn the flaky-retry budget.
EMPTY_KEY="verifyslicenosuchkeytransient"
VERIFY_SLICE_SCOPE=slice-only SLICE="$EMPTY_KEY" bash bin/verify-slice.sh >"$GATE_OUT" 2>&1
rc_d=$?
[ "$rc_d" -ne 0 ] || { cat "$GATE_OUT" >&2; fail "(d) empty/typo SLICE did NOT block (got $rc_d)"; }
grep -q "no behavioral tests matched SLICE=${EMPTY_KEY}" "$GATE_OUT" \
  || { cat "$GATE_OUT" >&2; fail "(d) empty SLICE blocked, but not with the 'no behavioral tests matched SLICE=' message"; }
grep -q "reproducibly failed" "$GATE_OUT" \
  && { cat "$GATE_OUT" >&2; fail "(d) empty SLICE wrongly fell through to the 'reproducibly failed' branch"; }
printf 'PROOF (d) OK: empty/typo SLICE -> BLOCK "no behavioral tests matched SLICE=%s"\n' "$EMPTY_KEY" >&2

# --- (e) flaky slice behavioral test -> BLOCK (F3) -------------------------
# A slice test that FAILS on the first run then PASSES on re-run. The unrelated/
# full-suite flaky class warns (DEC-53), but a flaky SLICE acceptance test must
# BLOCK. Drive the fail-then-pass via a persistent counter file that the test
# increments+checks: process-1 (first run) fails, process-2 (the gate's bounded
# re-run, were one to occur) would pass. The gate must NOT re-run/exempt it.
FLAKE_STATE="$(mktemp)"
rm -f "$FLAKE_STATE"   # absent on first read == first attempt
cat >"$FIXTURE" <<PY
import os

_STATE = "${FLAKE_STATE}"


def test_slice_fixture_is_flaky():
    # Fail on the FIRST invocation, pass on every subsequent one — the classic
    # fail-then-pass flaky shape. A flaky SLICE acceptance test must BLOCK.
    first = not os.path.exists(_STATE)
    with open(_STATE, "w") as fh:
        fh.write("seen")
    assert not first, "intermittent slice acceptance failure (must block, not warn)"
PY

grep -q "WARN  \[flaky\]" "$GATE_OUT" \
  && { cat "$GATE_OUT" >&2; fail "(e) flaky SLICE test was warn-exempted as flaky; it must BLOCK"; }
printf 'PROOF (e) OK: flaky slice behavioral test -> BLOCK (no flaky exemption)\n' >&2

printf 'PROOF GREEN: all five block/warn assertions held.\n' >&2
exit 0

input.content:
prompt-workshop: clear the 4 TASK-058 round-2 audit findings on verify-slice (F1/F2/F3/F4)

Round-2 adversarial-audit findings on the per-slice hard gate (bin/verify-slice.sh)
and its gate-first proof (bin/test-verify-slice.sh). Each was reproduced against
current source before fixing, and the fix proven RED->GREEN.

F1 — dead empty-selection guard (verify-slice.sh §3).
  Reproduced: `uv run pytest -q -k slice_nonexistenttypokey` -> exit 5,
  "3215 deselected", NEVER "no tests ran". The prior guard gated empty selection
  on `grep -q "no tests ran"`, which modern pytest does not emit for a zero-match
  -k; so a typo'd/empty SLICE fell through to the flaky-retry loop and then the
  "reproducibly failed" block (wrong message + wasted FLAKY_RETRIES re-runs — the
  pre-fix run showed "3216 deselected", evidence of the wasted re-run).
  Fix: detect the empty selection deterministically by pytest exit code 5
  ("no tests collected"), capturing run_pytest's rc once and branching on it;
  BLOCK with the correct message ("no behavioral tests matched SLICE=<key>")
  before any retry budget is spent. Aims to make a misconfigured SLICE fail fast
  with an accurate diagnosis rather than masquerade as a reproducible failure.

F2 — stale run_pytest docstring (verify-slice.sh §run_pytest).
  The comment described the function as echoing a fail-count parsed from the -q
  summary; the body does `return $?` (the exit code). Corrected the comment to
  describe the actual contract (returns the exit code; 0==pass, nonzero==fail,
  5==no tests collected) — documentation-only, no behavior change.

F3 — flaky-retry masked an intermittent SLICE acceptance regression
  (verify-slice.sh §3).
  The flaky class (fail-then-pass on bounded re-run -> warn) is the intended
  DEC-53 posture for UNRELATED/full-suite flakes. A flaky SLICE BEHAVIORAL test,
  however, is a non-deterministic acceptance contract, not trivia. Removed the
  flaky exemption from the slice's OWN selection (`-k slice_<key>`): any failure
  of that selection now BLOCKS. The flaky-warn class is retained ONLY for the
  broader/unrelated suite (§4 full pytest), preserving the user's directive that
  cosmetic/language WARNs and unrelated/full-suite flakes still warn and never
  block.

F4 — context-validate trusted an empty/unfound substrate as "clean"
  (verify-slice.sh §6).
  Reproduced: `pi-context context-validate --cwd /tmp` (a dir with no substrate)
  returns {"status":"clean","issues":[]} exit 0 — a false "clean" reported on
  nothing, which the prior status=="invalid"/errors!=0 checks both passed. Added
  a positive-presence guard asserting the substrate (.pi-context.json + .context/)
  actually resolves at the validated cwd ($SIP/..) BEFORE trusting any verdict;
  BLOCK if it cannot be located. Aims to ensure context-soundness is never
  reported on an absent substrate. Verified the predicate fires on /tmp and
  passes on the repo root.

Proof harness (bin/test-verify-slice.sh): added two assertions to the existing
three —
  (d) an empty/typo SLICE BLOCKS with the "no behavioral tests matched SLICE="
      message and does NOT reach the "reproducibly failed" branch (F1);
  (e) a flaky (fail-then-pass) SLICE behavioral test BLOCKS and is NOT warn-
      exempted as flaky (F3), driven by a persistent-counter fixture test.
Observed RED against the pre-fix gate (assertion (d) failed: empty SLICE hit
"reproducibly failed" after burning the retry budget) and GREEN against the
fixed gate (all five assertions held).

Whole-project gate composition (.claude/hooks/gate-before-commit.sh, Makefile
`test` target) left unchanged; the gate continues to reuse those checks.

Observed-green before commit (from school-improvement-plans/ under uv + dev DSN):
ruff check (All checks passed!), ruff format --check (485 files formatted),
mypy . (Success: no issues found in 326 source files), full pytest (3215 passed),
make test-js (exit 0), make verify-slice SLICE=verifyslicefixture (exit 0),
bin/test-verify-slice.sh (PROOF GREEN, exit 0), context-validate (0 errors,
5 pre-existing warnings).

input.prompt:
You are an adversarial auditor. You did NOT do this work. Verify the round-2 fixes resolved the 4 prior findings WITHOUT introducing new ones. Try to find it WRONG. Report findings with file:line evidence; propose no fixes; end with `Total findings: N`.

REPO: /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan ; Django root school-improvement-plans/ (run uv/make from there; dev DB DSN postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans). Branch verify-gated-dev-loop @ commit 4ac6ea6. Gate: bin/verify-slice.sh ; proof: bin/test-verify-slice.sh.

The 4 prior findings the round-2 commit claims to fix:
- F1: empty/typo SLICE selection must BLOCK with message "no behavioral tests matched SLICE=<key>" (deterministically, via pytest exit code 5 / collect-only count 0), with no wasted flaky retries.
- F2: the `run_pytest` docstring must correctly describe that it returns an exit code (not a parsed fail-count).
- F3: a fail-then-pass on a SLICE behavioral test (the `-k slice_<key>` selection) must BLOCK (no flaky exemption for the slice's own acceptance tests); flaky-warn remains ONLY for the unrelated/full-suite.
- F4: an absent/empty `.context` substrate must BLOCK (positive presence assertion of `.pi-context.json` + `.context/`), not pass as false-"clean".

ADVERSARIALLY VERIFY (cite file:line, RUN to confirm — don't trust the diff):
1. F1 fixed + non-vacuous: confirm a typo'd SLICE now blocks with the right message AND spends no extra retries (instrument or inspect). Confirm a VALID slice still passes (the exit-5 detection didn't over-trigger on a real selection).
2. F2: read the corrected docstring vs the function body — accurate now.
3. F3 fixed + the user's directive intact: confirm a flaky SLICE behavioral test BLOCKS, AND confirm cosmetic/style + language WARNs + UNRELATED/full-suite flakes STILL do NOT block (the fix didn't over-correct into blocking trivia — that would violate the user's "trivia never blocks"). Examine how the slice selection is distinguished from the full suite.
4. F4 fixed + non-vacuous: confirm an absent substrate blocks (e.g. point it at a dir with no substrate) AND the real repo substrate still passes (the presence assertion didn't break the normal path).
5. NO NEW DEFECTS: re-trace every exit path of verify-slice.sh — is the exit code still solely the blocking set? Did the new guards introduce a `set -e` gap, a swallowed $?, an over-block, or a path that exits 0 while a blocking check failed? Run the gate-first proof (bin/test-verify-slice.sh) and the full project gate (ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js, context-validate) yourself and confirm green. Confirm gate-before-commit.sh + Makefile `test` target still unweakened (git diff main..HEAD).

Run the actual commands. Report only substantiated findings. End with `Total findings: N`.

Goal: nail the EXACT current-source facts the first trio slice depends on — the persisted draft_state store + the server-side accumulation seam (fold a parsed assist prefill into the flat draft_state). Verify against live code, not assumptions.

Establish, with file:line:

1. THE FLAT draft_state KEY GRAMMAR. Read planner/static/wizard/js/ai-assist.js `collectDraftState` (~line 143) — the exact key convention it produces (bare form-field names + `{prefix}-{i}-{field}` for formset rows; the nested step-child prefixes). And `applyFormsetPrefill` (~line 200) — exactly how it folds a prefill back into form/formset DOM (this is the inverse the server-side seam must replicate). List the formset prefixes (criteria, feedback, milestones, phases, steps, review) and the step-child nesting (assignment/timeline/resource/substep/responsibility/position_responsibility/evidence).

2. WHAT build_grounding EXPECTS. ai/services/grounding.py `build_grounding(school, draft_state, include)` (~line 385) — confirm it reads the flat draft_state dict verbatim under `include` containing "draft_state"; and any helpers that PARSE that flat shape (planner/specs.py `_draft_state_*` helpers, ~line 609-662) — the exact key patterns they read (so the folded draft_state stays consumable).

3. THE PREFILL SHAPES. planner/assist.py `AssistResult.prefill` — its type (dict for a form step vs list[dict] for a formset step). For 2-3 representative specs (narrative-draft = form/basics; propose-milestones = formset; propose-responsibilities = the responsibilities shape), what prefill shape does each parser return, and which target_step/formset prefix it lands in. This tells the seam how to fold each.

4. MODEL + MIGRATION + SCOPING CONVENTIONS for a NEW store. Where plans models live (plans/models/), the file/registration pattern (plans/models/__init__.py), how a model is school-scoped (FK to school / a tenant manager), the migration convention, and whether a JSONField is already used anywhere (so PlanDraftState can hold the flat draft_state as JSON). Confirm there is still NO existing draft/version/Proposal model (the earlier finding) by grepping plans/ + planner/.

5. THE PER-SLICE TEST CONVENTION for this harness: confirm `test_slice_<key>.py` selected by `pytest -k slice_<key>` (per TASK-058's verify-slice). Where would test_slice_<draftstate-key>.py live (which app's tests/).

DELIVER: the exact flat-key grammar + the fold rules per prefill shape (the seam's spec), what build_grounding/specs read (the consumability contract the folded draft_state must satisfy), the model/migration/scoping pattern for a new PlanDraftState-style store, and the test-slice location. Flag any mismatch with the earlier findings. Establish facts; do not design or recommend.

input.content:
# Trio slice 1 — the persisted, versioned draft_state store

## Context

First real slice of the production orchestration trio (NORTH-STAR.md + DEC-57), built through the now-merged verify-gated harness (FEAT-009 / `make verify-slice`, DEC-58). DEC-57 §7 named "the draft-state store + accumulation seam" as the first slice; exploration (corroborated against live source) shows the accumulation seam spans ~14 distinct prefill shapes (form-dict, simple-formset-list, multi-section A3/G1, per-step children F2–F5/US-LLM-27, nested B2) — too large for one slice. Per maximal decomposition this slice is the STORE ALONE; the fold/seam is the next slice.

Why the store is needed (DEC-57): the trio accumulates a draft across DEC-33's staged admin review, so the draft must persist between stages. Production today has NO such store — the browser holds the flat `draft_state` in the form DOM (`collectDraftState`, ai-assist.js:143) and persists only once via the atomic `save_nested`. The trio needs a server-side, versioned (walkable-back, US-DRAFT-2/3) home for the in-progress flat draft_state, keyed to the author BEFORE any Plan exists.

## Success Criteria

- Saving a draft_state persists a row scoped to the author + school, identified by a draft id; a second save of the same draft APPENDS a new version (walkable-back history) — it does not overwrite the prior version.
- Loading the latest version of a draft returns the exact flat `draft_state` dict that was saved (round-trip equal, every `{prefix}-{i}-{field}` key and value preserved).
- The stored flat draft_state, passed to `build_grounding(school, draft_state=<loaded>, include=["draft_state"])`, is consumable unchanged: the spec helpers `_draft_state_row_count` and `_draft_state_milestone_improvement_types` read the same row counts / improvement-type codes they would read from the browser-collected dict.
- The store holds a draft that has NO saved Plan (the pre-Plan accumulation case) and is school-scoped: a draft created for one school is not loadable under another.
- The slice's behavioral tests are authored first, observed failing before IMPL and passing after; the whole-project gate stays green and `context-validate` is clean.

## Mechanism (decided — no open forks)

A new append-versioned model, NOT FK'd to Plan, NOT a OneToOne.

- New model in `plans/models/draft.py` (e.g. `PlanDraftState`): `draft_id` (UUID, groups the versions of one draft — generated at draft start, decided: a UUID is the clean pre-Plan key), `school` FK (`school.School`, `on_delete=PROTECT`, mirroring `plans/models/plan.py:88`), `created_by` FK (the author/`users` model used elsewhere), `version` (int, 1-based, per draft_id), `draft_state` (`JSONField`, the flat `{prefix-i-field: value}` dict — JSONField precedent `ai/models/llm.py`), optional `entry_mode`/`seed` meta, `created_at`. Append per save; latest = max(version) for a draft_id. Register in `plans/models/__init__.py`.
- A thin persistence API on the model's manager (or a small `plans/services.py` function): `save_version(draft_id, school, author, draft_state) -> row` (computes next version), `load_latest(draft_id, school) -> dict` (the flat draft_state), `versions(draft_id, school) -> queryset` (walkable history). School-scoped in the query, mirroring DEC-20's school-filtered service pattern.
- Migration in `plans/migrations/` (standard numbered file).
- The flat draft_state is stored VERBATIM (no transform) so the consumability contract with `build_grounding` + the `_draft_state_*` helpers (`specs.py:609,636`) holds by construction.

The accumulation seam (folding a parsed `AssistResult.prefill` into the flat draft_state — the server-side inverse of `applyFormsetPrefill`, ai-assist.js:200) is explicitly the NEXT slice, decomposed by prefill-shape class.

## Verification (gate-first, through the harness)

- Author `plans/tests/test_slice_draftstate.py` (selected by `make verify-slice SLICE=draftstate` → `pytest -k slice_draftstate`) FIRST, asserting each success criterion: append-versioning (no overwrite), round-trip equality, build_grounding consumability (assert `_draft_state_row_count`/`_draft_state_milestone_improvement_types` read the expected values from a loaded draft), pre-Plan + school-scoping. Observe RED before IMPL.
- Run the loop: fresh IMPL → `make verify-slice SLICE=draftstate` (orchestrator-observed exit 0) → separate adversarial audit → iterate to gate exit 0 AND audit findings 0 → cascade (VER + verification_verifies_item edge + complete-task + context-validate). Per-task branch off main; merge on validation (local main, per the recalibrated default).

## Critical files

- `plans/models/draft.py` (new) + `plans/models/__init__.py` (register) + `plans/migrations/####_plandraftstate.py` (new).
- `plans/models/plan.py:88` — the school-scoping FK pattern to mirror.
- `ai/services/grounding.py:385` (`build_grounding`) + `planner/specs.py:609,636` (`_draft_state_row_count`, `_draft_state_milestone_improvement_types`) — the consumability contract the stored flat dict must satisfy (assert against these in tests).
- `planner/static/wizard/js/ai-assist.js:143` (`collectDraftState`) — the authoritative flat-key grammar the stored dict mirrors.
- `plans/tests/test_slice_draftstate.py` (new) — the gate-first behavioral tests.
- `school-improvement-plans/bin/verify-slice.sh` — the harness gate (unchanged; consumed via `SLICE=draftstate`).

## Discipline

Per-task branch off main first; gate-first tests RED before IMPL; fresh-agent IMPL; separate adversarial audit; iterate to gate-exit-0 AND audit-findings-0; machine cascade (context-validate). No FK to Plan (pre-Plan accumulation). Store the flat draft_state verbatim (the consumability contract). This slice is the STORE only; the fold/seam is the next slice.

allowedPrompts.prompt: run the slice gate and project gate (make verify-slice, ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js, context-validate)
run Django makemigrations/migrate against the dev DB DSN
git branch/commit/merge for the per-task branch
append/upsert the context spine via state.mjs and pi-context

{
  "id": "FEAT-010",
  "title": "Production orchestration trio (US-LLM-23/24/25) — admin current+desired -> promotion-ready draft",
  "status": "in-progress",
  "layer": "L3",
  "description": "The production realization of DEC-29's north star (NORTH-STAR.md) per the DEC-57 architecture: a server-side loop drives the 14 dependency-ordered per-element assists against the school's grounding, accumulating a persisted flat draft_state across DEC-33 staged admin review, repairing per-spec failures (degrade-to-Flag, no abort), until the assembled draft passes the real _check_promotion_gates; the admin alone activates it. Built lean in planner/plans/ai reusing the 9 essential families, importing zero retired-workshop/pi-workflows/.workshopping code; every slice gated by the verify-gated harness (FEAT-009 / make verify-slice). Decomposed into slices: (1) the persisted versioned draft_state store [TASK-059, this]; (2) the accumulation seam (fold a parsed prefill into the flat draft_state, by prefill-shape class); (3) the per-spec server-side loop (ground->render->stream->parse->resolve->accumulate); (4) bounded-retry + degrade-to-Flag resilience; (5) the US-LLM-25 gate-closure loop on the saved Plan; (6) final save_nested + admin-promote wiring. Plus the 3 pending per-element assists US-LLM-7/10/15 (pattern-fill).",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "From an admin's current+desired-state input, the system produces a fully-populated proposed plan draft that passes _check_promotion_gates, with every value drawn from the school's catalogue (no fabrication) and the run completing without aborting (each spec contributes or degrades to a recorded Flag).",
    "The draft accumulates across staged admin review (it survives between stages) and the admin alone activates the final draft; the AI never persists the plan graph directly nor flips lifecycle.",
    "The trio reuses the existing production validators/grounding/promotion-gate and introduces no retired-workshop or new-orchestration-engine dependency.",

}

You are an IMPL agent building ONE slice (TASK-059): the persisted, versioned draft_state store for the production orchestration trio. GATE-FIRST. Self-contained; STOP and report if any step is genuinely ambiguous (mandate-008) rather than guessing.

REPO: /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan ; Django root school-improvement-plans/ (run uv/make from there). Branch trio-draftstate-store (checked out; commit here). Dev DB DSN: postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans (prefix uv/manage.py runs needing the DB).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (the contract — write tests asserting each, observe RED before impl, GREEN after):
1. Saving a draft_state persists a row scoped to author + school, identified by a draft id; saving the SAME draft again APPENDS a new version (prior version remains; history walkable-back), NOT overwrite.
2. Loading the latest version returns the EXACT flat draft_state dict saved (every key+value preserved, round-trip equal).
3. The stored flat draft_state, passed to build_grounding(school, draft_state=<loaded>, include=['draft_state']), is consumable unchanged: planner/specs.py `_draft_state_row_count(grounding, prefix)` and `_draft_state_milestone_improvement_types(grounding)` return the same values they would from the originally-saved dict.
4. The store holds a draft with NO saved Plan (pre-Plan accumulation) and is school-scoped (a draft under one school is not loadable under another).
5. The whole-project gate stays green; nothing pre-existing breaks.

DESIGN (decided — implement it; do not redesign):
- New model in school-improvement-plans/plans/models/draft.py, e.g. `PlanDraftState`: `draft_id` (UUIDField, groups the versions of one draft — NOT unique by itself), `school` FK to "school.School" on_delete=PROTECT (mirror plans/models/plan.py:88 incl. related_name + verbose_name), `created_by` FK to the user/author model (mirror however Plan references its author/user), `version` (PositiveIntegerField, 1-based per draft_id), `draft_state` (JSONField, the flat {prefix-i-field: value} dict — JSONField precedent ai/models/llm.py), optional `entry_mode`/`seed` (nullable text meta), `created_at` (auto_now_add). Append per save; latest = max(version) for (draft_id, school). NOT FK'd to Plan; NOT OneToOne. Add a unique constraint on (draft_id, version). Register in plans/models/__init__.py.
- A thin SCHOOL-SCOPED persistence API (a model manager method set, or functions in plans/services.py — pick the pattern that matches the codebase): `save_version(draft_id, school, author, draft_state, **meta) -> row` (computes next version atomically), `load_latest(draft_id, school) -> dict` (the flat draft_state, or None), `versions(draft_id, school) -> queryset` (ordered, walkable). Every query filters by school.
- Store the flat draft_state VERBATIM — no transform — so the build_grounding/_draft_state_* consumability contract (criterion 3) holds by construction.
- Migration: `uv run python manage.py makemigrations plans` then apply to the dev DB (`migrate`); commit the migration file.

GATE-FIRST PROOF: author school-improvement-plans/plans/tests/test_slice_draftstate.py (selected by `pytest -k slice_draftstate`, i.e. test functions/class named with `slice_draftstate`). Cover all 5 criteria — esp. criterion 3: build a fixture flat dict with e.g. `milestones-0-improvement_type` + `milestones-1-improvement_type` + some `criteria-0-...`/`steps-0-...` keys, save+load it, and assert `_draft_state_row_count` / `_draft_state_milestone_improvement_types` (imported from planner.specs) return the expected counts/codes from the loaded dict. Observe `make verify-slice SLICE=draftstate` RED before implementing, GREEN after.

VERIFY BEFORE COMMIT (observed-green, from school-improvement-plans/ under uv + DSN): `make verify-slice SLICE=draftstate` exit 0; and confirm the full project gate green (ruff check, ruff format --check, mypy ., pytest, make test-js, context-validate 0 errors). Commit on branch trio-draftstate-store (use `git commit -F` from a /tmp file if the message has backticks). Do NOT weaken the gate or gate-before-commit.sh.

REPORT BACK: the model + API + migration + test file (paths), the gate-first RED→GREEN evidence, the full observed-green gate output, and any STOP point. Do not say "done"/"verified"/"complete" — that is the orchestrator's separate-audit step.

input.content:
"""Gate-first acceptance contract for TASK-059: the persisted, versioned
`PlanDraftState` store for the production orchestration trio.

Selected by `pytest -k slice_draftstate` (file name + the
`slice_draftstate` token in every test function). Each test asserts one
of the five acceptance criteria; observed RED before the model/API/
migration land, GREEN after.

Criteria:
  1. Saving a draft persists a row scoped to author + school by a draft
     id; saving the SAME draft again APPENDS a new version (prior version
     remains; history walkable-back), never overwrite.
  2. Loading the latest version returns the EXACT flat draft_state dict
     saved (round-trip equal).
  3. The stored flat draft_state, passed to
     build_grounding(school, draft_state=<loaded>, include=['draft_state']),
     is consumable unchanged: planner.specs._draft_state_row_count and
     _draft_state_milestone_improvement_types return the same values they
     would from the originally-saved dict.
  4. The store holds a draft with NO saved Plan (pre-Plan accumulation)
     and is school-scoped (a draft under one school is not loadable under
     another).
  5. (Whole-project gate — asserted by `make verify-slice SLICE=draftstate`,
     not a single test here.)
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid

import pytest
from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model

from ai.services.grounding import build_grounding
from planner.specs import (
    _draft_state_milestone_improvement_types,
    _draft_state_row_count,
)
from plans.models import PlanDraftState
from school.models import School

User = get_user_model()


def _make_user(email: str = "drafter@example.test"):
    return User.objects.create_user(email=email, password="x")


def _make_school(slug: str = "draft-school", name: str = "Draft School"):
    return School.objects.create(name=name, slug=slug)


def _sample_flat_draft() -> dict:
    """A representative flat {prefix-i-field: value} draft_state.

    Carries two milestones (with improvement_type codes), two criteria
    rows, and one steps row so the criterion-3 consumability assertions
    against `_draft_state_row_count` / `_draft_state_milestone_improvement_types`
    are non-trivial.
    """
    return {
        "milestones-0-improvement_type": "INSTRUCTIONAL",
        "milestones-0-title": "Raise reading fluency",
        "milestones-1-improvement_type": "STRUCTURAL",
        "milestones-1-title": "Reorganize the timetable",
        "criteria-0-statement": "80% of cohort at benchmark",
        "criteria-1-statement": "Timetable approved by board",
        "steps-0-description": "Run a diagnostic assessment",
        "title": "Literacy and structure plan",
    }


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_draftstate_save_appends_versions_scoped_to_author_and_school():
    """Criterion 1: a save persists a row scoped to author + school by a
    draft id; re-saving the same draft id APPENDS version 2 (prior
    version remains), never overwrites."""
    school = _make_school()
    author = _make_user()
    draft_id = uuid.uuid4()
    state_v1 = {"title": "first"}
    state_v2 = {"title": "second", "milestones-0-improvement_type": "INSTRUCTIONAL"}

    row1 = PlanDraftState.objects.save_version(
        draft_id=draft_id, school=school, author=author, draft_state=state_v1
    )
    assert row1.version == 1
    assert row1.school_id == school.id
    assert row1.created_by_id == author.id
    assert row1.draft_id == draft_id

    row2 = PlanDraftState.objects.save_version(
        draft_id=draft_id, school=school, author=author, draft_state=state_v2
    )
    # APPEND, not overwrite: a new row at version 2, and version 1 still present.
    assert row2.version == 2
    assert row2.pk != row1.pk
    versions = PlanDraftState.objects.versions(draft_id=draft_id, school=school)
    assert [r.version for r in versions] == [1, 2]
    # The version-1 row is unchanged (walkable-back history).
    persisted_v1 = versions[0]
    assert persisted_v1.draft_state == state_v1


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_draftstate_load_latest_round_trips_exact_dict():
    """Criterion 2: load_latest returns the EXACT flat dict saved as the
    latest version (every key+value preserved, round-trip equal)."""
    school = _make_school()
    author = _make_user()
    draft_id = uuid.uuid4()
    flat = _sample_flat_draft()

    PlanDraftState.objects.save_version(
        draft_id=draft_id, school=school, author=author, draft_state={"title": "old"}
    )
    PlanDraftState.objects.save_version(
        draft_id=draft_id, school=school, author=author, draft_state=flat
    )

    loaded = PlanDraftState.objects.load_latest(draft_id=draft_id, school=school)
    assert loaded == flat
    # Independence of object identity: it is the stored content, not the input ref.
    assert loaded is not flat


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_draftstate_load_latest_none_when_absent():
    """Criterion 2 corollary: load_latest returns None when no version of
    the draft exists for that school."""
    school = _make_school()
    assert PlanDraftState.objects.load_latest(draft_id=uuid.uuid4(), school=school) is None


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_draftstate_loaded_state_consumable_by_build_grounding():
    """Criterion 3: the stored flat draft_state, loaded and passed to
    build_grounding(..., include=['draft_state']), yields the same
    _draft_state_row_count / _draft_state_milestone_improvement_types
    values as the originally-saved dict — consumable unchanged."""
    school = _make_school()
    author = _make_user()
    draft_id = uuid.uuid4()
    flat = _sample_flat_draft()

    # Baseline: the helpers' values computed directly from the saved dict.
    direct_grounding = {"draft_state": flat}
    expected_milestones = _draft_state_row_count(direct_grounding, "milestones")
    expected_criteria = _draft_state_row_count(direct_grounding, "criteria")
    expected_steps = _draft_state_row_count(direct_grounding, "steps")
    expected_types = _draft_state_milestone_improvement_types(direct_grounding)
    assert expected_milestones == 2
    assert expected_criteria == 2
    assert expected_steps == 1
    assert expected_types == ["INSTRUCTIONAL", "STRUCTURAL"]

    # Persist + load, then run through the production grounding assembler.
    PlanDraftState.objects.save_version(
        draft_id=draft_id, school=school, author=author, draft_state=flat
    )
    loaded = PlanDraftState.objects.load_latest(draft_id=draft_id, school=school)
    grounding = build_grounding(school, draft_state=loaded, include=["draft_state"])

    assert _draft_state_row_count(grounding, "milestones") == expected_milestones
    assert _draft_state_row_count(grounding, "criteria") == expected_criteria
    assert _draft_state_row_count(grounding, "steps") == expected_steps
    assert _draft_state_milestone_improvement_types(grounding) == expected_types


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_draftstate_pre_plan_accumulation_no_plan_required():
    """Criterion 4a: a draft is held with NO saved Plan — the store does
    not require (and is not FK'd to) a Plan to accumulate state."""
    school = _make_school()
    author = _make_user()
    draft_id = uuid.uuid4()

    row = PlanDraftState.objects.save_version(
        draft_id=draft_id, school=school, author=author, draft_state={"title": "wip"}
    )
    assert row.pk is not None
    # No `plan` attribute / relation: the model is not coupled to Plan.
    assert not hasattr(row, "plan")
    assert not hasattr(row, "plan_id")


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_draftstate_school_scoped_not_cross_loadable():
    """Criterion 4b: a draft saved under one school is not loadable under
    another — every query filters by school."""
    school_a = _make_school(slug="school-a", name="School A")
    school_b = _make_school(slug="school-b", name="School B")
    author = _make_user()
    draft_id = uuid.uuid4()
    flat = {"title": "A's draft"}

    PlanDraftState.objects.save_version(
        draft_id=draft_id, school=school_a, author=author, draft_state=flat
    )

    # Same draft_id, but under school B: not loadable.
    assert PlanDraftState.objects.load_latest(draft_id=draft_id, school=school_b) is None
    assert list(PlanDraftState.objects.versions(draft_id=draft_id, school=school_b)) == []
    # Still loadable under its own school.
    assert PlanDraftState.objects.load_latest(draft_id=draft_id, school=school_a) == flat
