input.prompt:
You are the IMPL agent for one atomic slice of the WASC school-improvement-plan Django app. Execute it gate-first. STOP and report (do not guess) on any spec ambiguity or if a stated fact does not match source. Work only within this slice's scope; touch no unrelated code. All `manage.py`/`uv`/`make` runs are from `/Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan/school-improvement-plans`. You are on git branch `trio-save-plan` (already created off main) — commit there.

# Slice 8a — save the accumulated trio draft_state into a saved proposed Plan

## What this is
The production "trio" (`planner/orchestration.py:run_trio_pass`) drives 14 dependency-ordered LLM specs against a school's catalogue, accumulating ONE flat `draft_state` dict (the form-POST key grammar `{prefix}-{i}-{field}`, nested `{prefix}-{i}-{segment}-{k}-{field}`, two-level `criteria-{i}-measurement-{j}-channel-{k}-...`). It returns `(draft_id, final_version, final_draft_state, degraded)`. Today nothing turns that accumulated `draft_state` into a saved `Plan`. THIS slice adds that: a server-side function that persists the accumulated flat `draft_state` into a saved `Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED)`, owned by the school+author, with the full child graph. This is the prerequisite for slice 8b (the promotion gate runs on a SAVED Plan).

OUT OF SCOPE (do not build, do not call): `_check_promotion_gates`, any gate-closure re-invoke loop, `advance_plan_status`/admin-promote, Flag-row persistence. Those are slices 8b/9.

## Success criteria (the contract — your tests must verify EACH; loop until all hold)
1. The accumulated flat `draft_state` from `run_trio_pass` is saved as a `Plan` in `lifecycle=PROPOSED`, owned by the originating school and author, with the full graph populated: plan scalars, every formset row, every nested child, and every cross-row binding the `draft_state` carried.
2. The save round-trips: re-reading the saved Plan reproduces the `draft_state`'s content (scalar values, formset row counts, nested children, and bindings) with nothing dropped, duplicated, or reordered into a different binding.
3. For a given `draft_state`, the saved Plan graph is identical to what the existing browser one-shot create flow persists from the same posted data (parity), and the save is atomic all-or-nothing: a `draft_state` whose forms/formsets fail validation persists no Plan and surfaces the validation failure rather than a partial or invalid Plan.
4. The whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates. (The promotion-gate check, the gate-closure re-invoke loop, admin-promote, and Flag-row persistence are out of scope — slices 8b/9.)

## Established mechanism facts (verified against current source — build on these; if any is false, STOP and report)
- The browser one-shot create path is `PlanCreateView.post` (`planner/views.py:264-303`): `data = request.POST` → `objects = self._build_context_objects(data=data)` (`views.py:128-143`, which builds every step's primary form/formset binding `data=` plus extra-formsets and attaches nested) → validate every object's `.is_valid()` (no short-circuit) AND `self._validate_nested(data, objects)` (`views.py:379+`) → inside ONE `transaction.atomic()`: `plan = self._save_plan(request, objects)` (`views.py:305-314`, which sets `plan.author`, `plan.school`, `plan.lifecycle = Lifecycle.PROPOSED`, saves, `save_m2m`) then `save_nested(plan, data, objects, school)` (`planner/formsets.py:470+`) then `self._save_flag_requests(plan, data)`. A `ValidationError` from inside the atomic block rolls everything back (no partial Plan).
- Forms/formsets bind `data=` as POST data; multi-row fields use `getlist`, so `data` must be a Django `QueryDict`, not a plain dict.
- `collectDraftState` (`planner/static/wizard/js/ai-assist.js:143`) stores ONE value per field name (`state[el.name] = el.value`). The planner models M2M relations as extra-FORMSETS of single-FK rows (e.g. the A3 domain-alignment relations `learner_outcomes_targeted`/`stakeholder_impact`/`areas_for_improvement` are extra-formsets registered on `basics`), NOT multi-selects. So the `draft_state` is genuinely single-valued-per-key, and a `QueryDict` built with one value per key is faithful to what the browser posts.
- THE ONE NEW PIECE: the accumulated `draft_state` from `run_trio_pass` carries NO formset management-form fields. The seam folds (`planner/services.py`) write only `{prefix}-{i}-{field}` row keys and deliberately never synthesize `{prefix}-TOTAL_FORMS`/`-INITIAL_FORMS` (`services.py:94,148-149`); `run_trio_pass` starts from `{}`. But Django formsets REFUSE to bind without a management form. So this slice must synthesize the management-form fields for EVERY formset prefix present in the `draft_state` before binding — at every level:
  - top-level step formsets + the extra-formsets (e.g. the A3 relation formsets on `basics`),
  - nested children: `criteria-{i}-measurement`, `criteria-{i}-measurement-{j}-channel`, `steps-{i}-{seg}` for each segment in `planner.formsets.ACTIONSTEP_CHILD_FORMSETS` (`formsets.py:423`), `steps-{i}-evidence` (`ACTIONSTEP_EVIDENCE`, `formsets.py:441`), `review_events-{i}-input` (`REVIEWEVENT_INPUT`, `formsets.py:436`).
  Derive each prefix's row count from the `draft_state` keys (the row indices present under `{prefix}-(\d+)-`), set `TOTAL_FORMS` = max-index+1 (i.e. row count), `INITIAL_FORMS` = 0 (all rows are new), and whatever else the formset's management form requires (`MIN_NUM_FORMS`/`MAX_NUM_FORMS` — read the formset class). The browser does this implicitly via the add-row DOM; server-side you synthesize it. `_validate_nested` (`views.py:379+`) shows the exact nested prefix construction to mirror.
- `Lifecycle.PROPOSED` is the value `_save_plan` stamps; import it where it already lives (used in `views.py`).

## How to build it
- Honor criterion 3 (parity, no divergent save logic): REUSE the existing view bind→validate→`_save_plan`+`save_nested`+`_save_flag_requests` sequence rather than reimplementing a parallel save. Choose the cleanest reuse (e.g. extract the view's POST save body into a shared module-level function that both `PlanCreateView.post` and the trio-save call, OR drive the existing view methods). If you refactor the view, the view's existing behavior must be byte-for-byte preserved (its tests stay green). The author identity comes from the trio's `author`; the school from the trio's `school`.
- Home: put the new save-from-draft_state function where `run_trio_pass` can call it (`planner/orchestration.py` or `planner/services.py` — your call; keep imports clean, avoid a circular import with views).
- The function signature should take the accumulated flat `draft_state`, the `school`, and the `author`, and return the saved `Plan` (raise/​surface validation failure atomically on invalid input). It must NOT touch the promotion gate.

## Gate-first verification (mandatory order)
1. FIRST author `planner/tests/test_slice_save_draft.py` (the harness selects it via `pytest -k slice_save_draft`, so the file + test function names must contain `slice_save_draft`). The tests must assert EACH success criterion with a REALISTIC draft_state — prefer driving `run_trio_pass` with the mocked LLM (there is a shared `trio_school` fixture in `planner/tests/conftest.py` and a `FakeLLMProvider`; prior slices `test_slice_trio_pass.py`/`test_slice_resilience.py` show the pattern) so the saved Plan reflects a genuine accumulated draft_state spanning scalars + formset rows + nested children + bindings. Include: (a) full-graph save + `lifecycle==PROPOSED` + owner; (b) round-trip (re-read the Plan, assert counts/values/bindings match the draft_state); (c) PARITY — POST the SAME data through `PlanCreateView` (the browser path) and assert the resulting Plan graph is identical to the trio-save graph; (d) atomic rejection — an invalid draft_state persists no Plan and surfaces the failure.
2. Observe the new tests RED before implementing (run `uv run pytest planner/tests/test_slice_save_draft.py` and confirm failure for the right reason).
3. Implement to GREEN.
4. Run the slice gate: `make verify-slice SLICE=save_draft` from `school-improvement-plans/`. It must exit 0 (BLOCKING class: your slice tests + ruff + mypy + full pytest + make test-js + context-validate). Do not weaken the gate. Do not commit on a red or unobserved gate.

## Commit + report
- Commit on branch `trio-save-plan` with a forensic message (speak to aims/intentions, not unwarranted certainty; no author-credit lines). Use `git commit -F` from a file in the SYSTEM `/tmp` (NOT the repo's tracked `tmp/`) if the message has shell-special chars. Working tree must be clean after (no stray files).
- Report back: the commit SHA, the new/changed files, the exact `make verify-slice SLICE=save_draft` result (the tail showing exit 0 + the ruff/mypy/pytest/test-js/context-validate lines), how you handled the management-form synthesis + the view-reuse, and confirmation that the gate was observed RED-before / GREEN-after. If you hit any ambiguity or a stated fact was false, STOP and report it instead of guessing.

input.content:
"""Slice 8a — SAVE the accumulated trio `draft_state` into a saved proposed `Plan`.

Covers the new server-side save-from-draft_state function (slice 8a). The trio
loop (`planner.orchestration.run_trio_pass`) accumulates ONE flat `draft_state`
across the 14 specs but persists no `Plan`; this slice turns that accumulated
flat `draft_state` into a saved `Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED)` owned by the
originating school + author, with the full child graph.

Gate-first: these tests assert EACH of the four success criteria with a REALISTIC
`draft_state` produced by driving `run_trio_pass` with the per-spec canned-LLM
dispatcher (reusing the `trio_school` fixture + the `_canned_outputs` /
`_SpecDispatchProvider` pattern from `test_slice_trio_pass`), so the saved Plan
reflects a genuine accumulation spanning scalars + formset rows + nested children
+ bindings.

  1. full-graph save + lifecycle==PROPOSED + owner (school + author);
  2. round-trip — re-read the saved Plan, assert counts/values/bindings match the
     draft_state;
  3. PARITY — drive the SAME synthesized POST data through `PlanCreateView` (the
     browser one-shot path) and assert the resulting Plan graph is identical to
     the trio-save graph; plus atomic rejection — an invalid draft_state persists
     no Plan and surfaces the failure.

Named `slice_save_draft` so `make verify-slice SLICE=save_draft` (→ `pytest -k
slice_save_draft`) selects this file.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from typing import Any

import pytest
from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError
from django.urls import reverse

from plans.choices import Lifecycle
from plans.models import (
    ActionStep,
    Assignment,
    Communication,
    FeedbackChannel,
    Measurement,
    MeasurementChannel,
    Milestone,
    Plan,
    PlanAccreditationStandard,
    PlanLearnerOutcome,
    RequiredResource,
    ReviewEvent,
    ReviewEventInput,
    SubStep,
    SuccessCriterion,
    Timeline,
)

# Reuse the per-spec canned-LLM dispatcher + canned outputs from the trio-pass
# slice so the saved draft_state is the genuine 14-spec accumulation.
from .test_slice_trio_pass import (  # noqa: F401 — fake_trio_llm is a fixture used by name
    TRIO_ORDER,
    _canned_outputs,
    fake_trio_llm,
)


def _run_trio(school: Any, author: Any, seed: str = "raise reading") -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Drive the trio loop and return its final accumulated flat draft_state."""
    from planner.orchestration import run_trio_pass

    draft_id = uuid.uuid4()
    _, _, final_state, degraded = run_trio_pass(
        school, author, draft_id, seed=seed, entry_mode="trio"
    )
    assert degraded == []  # happy-path canned outputs degrade nothing
    return final_state


def _graph_shape(plan: Plan) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """A structural fingerprint of a saved Plan's whole child graph.

    Used for the parity assertion (criterion 3): two Plans built from the same
    posted data must fingerprint identically. Captures plan scalars, per-relation
    row COUNTS, ordered child labels/descriptions, and the cross-row bindings the
    draft_state carries (measurement→channel, review-event→channel input)."""
    criteria = list(plan.successcriteria.order_by("pk"))
    steps = list(plan.actionsteps.order_by("order", "pk"))
    return {
        "title": plan.title,
        "current_state": plan.current_state,
        "desired_state": plan.desired_state,
        "rationale": plan.rationale,
        "student_impact_framing": plan.student_impact_framing,
        "lifecycle": plan.lifecycle,
        "n_criteria": len(criteria),
        "criteria_texts": sorted(c.text for c in criteria),
        "n_measurements": Measurement.objects.filter(
            success_criterion__plan=plan
        ).count(),
        "n_measurement_channels": MeasurementChannel.objects.filter(
            measurement__success_criterion__plan=plan
        ).count(),
        "n_feedback": plan.feedbackchannels.count(),
        "feedback_labels": sorted(f.label for f in plan.feedbackchannels.all()),
        "n_milestones": plan.milestones.count(),
        "milestone_labels": sorted(m.label for m in plan.milestones.all()),
        "n_steps": len(steps),
        "step_descriptions": [s.description for s in steps],
        "n_assignments": Assignment.objects.filter(step__plan=plan).count(),
        "n_timelines": Timeline.objects.filter(action_step__plan=plan).count(),
        "n_resources": RequiredResource.objects.filter(action_step__plan=plan).count(),
        "n_substeps": SubStep.objects.filter(action_step__plan=plan).count(),
        "n_evidence": plan.evidence_artifacts.count(),
        "n_standards": PlanAccreditationStandard.objects.filter(plan=plan).count(),
        "n_learner_outcomes": PlanLearnerOutcome.objects.filter(plan=plan).count(),
        "n_communications": plan.communications.count(),
        "communication_channels": sorted(c.channel for c in plan.communications.all()),
        "n_review_events": plan.reviewevents.count(),
        "n_review_event_inputs": ReviewEventInput.objects.filter(
            review_event__plan=plan
        ).count(),
        "n_revision_rules": plan.revisionrules.count(),
    }


# --- Criterion 1: full-graph save + lifecycle PROPOSED + owner. ----------------


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_save_draft_persists_full_graph_proposed_and_owned(
    trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm
):
    from planner.orchestration import save_trio_draft

    school = trio_school["school"]
    draft_state = _run_trio(school, author)

    plan = save_trio_draft(draft_state, school=school, author=author)

    # Saved, owned, PROPOSED.
    assert isinstance(plan, Plan)
    assert plan.pk is not None
    assert plan.school_id == school.pk
    assert plan.author_id == author.pk
    assert plan.lifecycle == Lifecycle.PROPOSED

    # Full graph populated across every fold-shape class.
    assert plan.successcriteria.count() == 1
    assert plan.feedbackchannels.count() == 1
    assert plan.milestones.count() == 2 - 1  # one milestone proposed by D1
    assert plan.actionsteps.count() == 2
    assert Assignment.objects.filter(step__plan=plan).count() == 2
    assert Timeline.objects.filter(action_step__plan=plan).count() == 2
    assert RequiredResource.objects.filter(action_step__plan=plan).count() == 2
    assert plan.evidence_artifacts.count() == 2
    assert PlanAccreditationStandard.objects.filter(plan=plan).count() == 1
    assert PlanLearnerOutcome.objects.filter(plan=plan).count() == 1
    assert plan.communications.count() == 1
    assert plan.reviewevents.count() == 1
    # Cross-row binding: the B2 measurement→channel landed.
    assert (
        MeasurementChannel.objects.filter(
            measurement__success_criterion__plan=plan
        ).count()
        == 1
    )


# --- Criterion 2: the save round-trips. ----------------------------------------


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_save_draft_round_trips_draft_state_content(trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm):
    """Re-reading the saved Plan reproduces the draft_state's content: scalar
    values, formset row counts, nested children, and the bindings — nothing
    dropped, duplicated, or re-bound."""
    from planner.orchestration import save_trio_draft

    school = trio_school["school"]
    draft_state = _run_trio(school, author)
    plan = save_trio_draft(draft_state, school=school, author=author)

    # Scalars round-trip from the A1 narrative fold.
    assert plan.current_state == draft_state["current_state"]
    assert plan.desired_state == draft_state["desired_state"]
    assert plan.rationale == draft_state["rationale"]

    # Formset row counts equal the draft_state's row indices.
    crit = plan.successcriteria.get()
    assert crit.text == draft_state["criteria-0-text"]

    steps = list(plan.actionsteps.order_by("order"))
    assert [s.description for s in steps] == [
        draft_state["steps-0-description"],
        draft_state["steps-1-description"],
    ]

    # Nested child round-trips (F2 assignment under step 0).
    step0 = steps[0]
    assert step0.assignments.count() == 1

    # The B2 binding round-trips to exactly one channel under one measurement,
    # bound to the single feedback channel — not duplicated nor re-bound.
    channel = plan.feedbackchannels.get()
    mc = MeasurementChannel.objects.get(measurement__success_criterion=crit)
    assert mc.feedback_channel_id == channel.pk

    # The G1 review-event input binds to the same feedback channel.
    rei = ReviewEventInput.objects.get(review_event__plan=plan)
    assert rei.feedback_channel_id == channel.pk


# --- Criterion 3a: PARITY with the browser one-shot create path. ---------------


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_save_draft_parity_with_browser_create_path(client, trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm):
    """For a given draft_state, the trio-save graph is identical to what the
    browser one-shot create flow persists from the SAME posted data.

    The trio-save builds a QueryDict (management forms synthesized) from the
    draft_state; this test extracts that exact POST mapping via the same public
    builder and posts it through `PlanCreateView`, then fingerprints both Plans.
    """
    from planner.orchestration import build_trio_post_data, save_trio_draft

    school = trio_school["school"]
    draft_state = _run_trio(school, author)

    # Path A: the trio save.
    trio_plan = save_trio_draft(draft_state, school=school, author=author)

    # Path B: the SAME synthesized POST data through the browser view.
    post_data = build_trio_post_data(draft_state)
    client.force_login(author)
    resp = client.post(reverse("planner:create"), data=dict(post_data.lists()))
    assert resp.status_code == 302, getattr(resp, "content", b"")[:2000]

    browser_plan = (
        Plan.objects.filter(author=author, school=school).exclude(pk=trio_plan.pk).get()
    )

    a = _graph_shape(trio_plan)
    b = _graph_shape(browser_plan)
    # Titles auto-derived may differ; compare everything else structurally.
    a_cmp = {k: v for k, v in a.items() if k != "title"}
    b_cmp = {k: v for k, v in b.items() if k != "title"}
    assert a_cmp == b_cmp


# --- Criterion 3b: atomic all-or-nothing rejection of an invalid draft_state. --


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_save_draft_invalid_draft_state_persists_nothing(trio_school, author):
    """A draft_state whose forms/formsets fail validation persists NO Plan and
    surfaces the validation failure (not a partial/invalid Plan)."""
    from planner.orchestration import save_trio_draft

    school = trio_school["school"]
    before = Plan.objects.count()

    # A criterion row missing its required `text` is a formset validation
    # failure (and carries no valid plan basics at all).
    bad_draft_state: dict[str, Any] = {
        "criteria-0-verification_kind": "judgment",
    }

    with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
        save_trio_draft(bad_draft_state, school=school, author=author)

    assert Plan.objects.count() == before

context: file DEC-59 + TASK-069 and re-scope slice 8a (trio save) after the IMPL's mandate-008 STOP

The slice-8a IMPL (save accumulated trio draft_state -> proposed Plan) STOPped per
mandate-008 on a real premise gap rather than guessing: the accumulated draft_state
never carries two REQUIRED Plan fields. Plan.title (CharField, not-null, no blank;
plans/models/plan.py:94) and Plan.cycle (FK school.Cycle, PROTECT, not-null; :101) are
produced by no spec -- A1 narrative-draft emits only its five narrative scalars, no fold
writes title/cycle, and PlanDraftState carries neither. The IMPL declined to fabricate a
title. This commit records the investigation's outcome; it changes no Django code.

Resolution (DEC-59), derived from NORTH-STAR.md ("the admin writes ONLY the current state
and the desired end state; the system proposes EVERY remaining element"), not invented:

- cycle: aims to derive via the existing ai.services.grounding._active_cycle(school) deriver
  (the same active cycle the trio's A1 grounding already uses) and inject it at save time --
  scoping context, not a proposed element; intended to surface a precondition failure when a
  school has no active cycle rather than fabricate one.
- title: system-proposed -- its intended home is A1 (the narrative spec; title is a narrative
  scalar of-a-kind with the four it already emits), to be added to NARRATIVE_FIELDS with the
  DB PromptTemplate('plan-narrative-draft') edited via a guarded ai/00NN migration. A
  mechanical/placeholder title was rejected as a fabrication inconsistent with the North Star.

Consequent re-scope: slice 8a splits into TASK-069 (8a-i, "A1 emits title") which PRECEDES
TASK-068 (8a-ii, the save, now amended to derive+inject cycle and synthesize the formset
management forms the folds never write). Filed TASK-069 + three edges (story_contains_task
under STORY-013, task_addresses_feature FEAT-010, task_depends_on_task TASK-069 -> TASK-068);
amended TASK-068's description; appended the STOP-resolution event to ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.

Records only: seed-round-plan.json (DEC-59), .context tasks/relations/objects (TASK-068
amend + TASK-069 + edges), ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.json (the re-plan event). context-validate: 0
errors. The premature save-test the IMPL had drafted was removed (8a-ii will author its
tests gate-first once A1-title lands).

3. THE GUARDED-MIGRATION PATTERN. Read a recent guarded-byte-match edit to THIS template (e.g. ai/migrations/0020_reinforce_voice_constraint.py and/or 0021) AND ai/migrations/0016_update_propose_domain_alignment_rationale.py. Establish the exact pattern: how it loads the template, byte-matches the expected current body (guard), replaces it, and how the reverse/backward migration restores it. What is the precise current body string a 0027 migration must match against? 4. THE FORM-STEP FOLD + BINDING. Confirm the A1 narrative fold (the "form-step fold", planner/services.py — slice 1) writes the narrative prefill as BARE keys (e.g. `current_state`, not `basics-0-current_state`) into draft_state, and that adding `title` to the prefill requires NO fold change. Which would BREAK when A1 emits a 6th field `title` (e.g. an assertion that the prefill has exactly the 5 keys, a canned A1 output used by the trio tests test_slice_trio_pass.py / conftest.py that would now need a title key, a template-body snapshot test)? 7. THE TRIO PATH. You are the IMPL agent for one atomic slice of the WASC school-improvement-plan Django app. Execute it gate-first. STOP and report (do not guess) on any spec ambiguity or if a stated fact does not match source. Work only within this slice's named scope; touch no unrelated code. # Slice 8a-i (TASK-069, DEC-59) — A1 narrative-draft emits a plan `title`

## Why
The production "trio" (`planner/orchestration.py:run_trio_pass`) accumulates one flat `draft_state` from 14 LLM specs and (in the next slice) saves it as a `Plan`. `Plan.title` is REQUIRED (CharField, not-null, no blank — `plans/models/plan.py:94`) but NO spec produces it today. Per NORTH-STAR.md the admin writes ONLY current+desired state and the system PROPOSES every remaining element — so the title is system-proposed, and its home is A1 (the narrative spec; `title` is a narrative scalar of-a-kind with the four prose scalars A1 already emits). This slice extends A1 to also emit a concise `title`. (The companion `cycle` value and the save itself are the NEXT slice, TASK-068 — out of scope here.)

## Success criteria (the contract — your tests must verify EACH; loop until all hold)
1. A trio run's accumulated `draft_state` carries a non-empty `title` (within the `Plan.title` 200-char limit) that binds to `PlanForm.title`, produced by A1's reasoning from its grounding rather than a fixed/placeholder string.
2. A1's existing four narrative scalars (`current_state`, `desired_state`, `rationale`, `student_impact_framing`) still parse and fold unchanged; an A1 response lacking a title degrades gracefully to a partial prefill exactly as a missing scalar does today.
3. The production prompt-template change applies only to the expected prior template body (a guarded change, not a blind overwrite) and is reversible.
4. The whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates.

## Established facts (verified against current source — build on these; if any is false, STOP and report)
- `NARRATIVE_FIELDS` (`planner/specs.py:403`) is currently the 5-tuple (`current_state`, `desired_state`, `rationale`, `student_impact_framing`, `provenance`). `parse_narrative` (`specs.py:711`) keeps exactly the keys in `NARRATIVE_FIELDS` whose value is a non-empty string, omits missing ones gracefully, and raises `ValueError` only when NONE are present. Its docstring says "five narrative keys" in several places.
- `planner/_freetext_audit.py:_select_a1` enumerates the SAME 5 field names in a hardcoded tuple (it builds the `(label, text)` pairs the free-text catalogue scan reads). `title` must be ADDED to this tuple so the title prose is scanned like the others. That scan attaches NON-BLOCKING flags (A3 / FGAP-004) — it does not hard-raise — so a title naming an improvement area is fine (flags, not errors). Confirm the non-raising behavior before relying on it.
- A1's prompt is the DB row `PromptTemplate(slug='plan-narrative-draft')`, seeded by `ai/migrations/0003_seed_plan_narrative_draft_template.py` and edited by guarded byte-match migrations since (0018/0019/0020/0021/0023/0025 all reference the slug). The CURRENT body is the cumulative result. The body states an output-key contract (the JSON keys the model must return); `title` must be added to that contract.
- The guarded-byte-match migration pattern: see `ai/migrations/0020_reinforce_voice_constraint.py` / `0021_extend_no_fabrication_directives.py` / `0016_update_propose_domain_alignment_rationale.py`. Each loads the template, asserts/guards the EXACT expected current body (so it no-ops or errors safely if the body is not the known state), replaces it, and the reverse migration restores the prior body. The latest `ai` migration is `0026`; your new migration is `0027`. To get the exact post-0026 body to guard against, apply migrations to a DB and read `PromptTemplate.objects.get(slug='plan-narrative-draft').template` (the dev DB already has them applied; or use the test DB / a scratch shell) — do NOT hand-guess the body.
- `PlanForm.Meta.fields` (`planner/forms.py`, ~line 293) already includes `title` (and a separate `title_zh_hans` modeltranslation twin). The basics step binds UNPREFIXED, so a bare `title` key binds to `PlanForm.title`. The A1 "form-step fold" (`planner/services.py`, seam slice 1) writes the narrative prefill as BARE keys — so adding `title` to the prefill needs NO fold change and NO `run_trio_pass`/orchestration change.
- `title_zh_hans` is an OPTIONAL modeltranslation twin (not a hard model field; runtime-registered). A1 is the active-language narrative — emit ONLY bare `title`, NOT `title_zh_hans` (the zh_hans twin is US-LLM-7, a different slice). A Plan with a filled `title` and blank `title_zh_hans` validates.

## The change (two production edits + their fixture/test consequences)
1. Add `"title"` to `NARRATIVE_FIELDS` (`specs.py:403`); update `parse_narrative`'s docstring count language ("five"→"six") accordingly.
2. Add `"title"` to the `_select_a1` tuple (`planner/_freetext_audit.py`).
3. NEW migration `ai/migrations/0027_*.py`: guarded edit of `PromptTemplate('plan-narrative-draft')` adding `title` to the output-key contract — instruct the model to also return a concise `title`: a short plan title (a handful of words, within 200 chars) naming the improvement thrust, derived from the desired end state, in the same JSON object, consistent with the existing no-fabrication / voice constraints. Guard against the exact current body; provide a reverse that restores it.

## Existing fixtures/tests that will need the title key (the gate runs FULL pytest — these must stay green)
- `planner/tests/test_a1_narrative.py` — the canned `A1_JSON` + any "five fields" assertions/comments.
- `planner/tests/test_slice_fold_form.py` (~:35-44) — the `A1_JSON` fixture.
- `planner/tests/test_slice_trio_pass.py` (~:95-103) — the canned `narrative-draft` output in `_canned_outputs()`.
Update each so its A1 output carries a `title` and any exact-key-set assertion includes it. (These are stated from an exploration pass — VERIFY each against source; if the line/shape differs, adapt and note it.)

## Gate-first verification (mandatory order)
1. FIRST author `planner/tests/test_slice_a1_title.py` (the harness selects it via `pytest -k slice_a1_title`, so the file + test names must contain `slice_a1_title`). Assert EACH success criterion: (a) `parse_narrative` keeps a `title` from a title-bearing A1 output and binds it (a bare `title` key reaches `PlanForm.title` — assert via PlanForm or the fold); (b) a full `run_trio_pass` (mocked LLM, the `trio_school` conftest fixture + the canned-dispatch pattern in `test_slice_trio_pass.py`) yields an accumulated `draft_state` carrying a non-empty bare `title`; (c) an A1 output WITHOUT a title still parses (partial prefill, no raise) and the other scalars are unaffected; (d) the `0027` migration is guarded — it applies forward (the template body gains the title contract) and its reverse restores the prior body (test via the migration's operations or a body-before/after assertion).
2. Observe the new tests (and the to-be-updated existing fixtures) RED for the right reason before implementing.
3. Implement to GREEN.
4. Run the slice gate: `make verify-slice SLICE=a1_title` from `school-improvement-plans/`. It must exit 0 (BLOCKING: your slice tests + ruff + mypy + full pytest + make test-js + context-validate). The full-pytest run applies the `0027` migration to the test DB. Do not weaken the gate; do not commit on a red or unobserved gate.

## Commit + report
- Commit on branch `trio-a1-title` with a forensic message (speak to aims/intentions, not unwarranted certainty; no author-credit lines). Use `git commit -F` from a file in the SYSTEM `/tmp` (NOT the repo's tracked `tmp/`) if the message has shell-special chars. Working tree must be clean after.
- Report: the commit SHA; the files changed (incl. the exact `0027` migration name + how you obtained the guarded current body); the title instruction wording you added to the template; the exact `make verify-slice SLICE=a1_title` tail (exit 0 + the ruff/mypy/pytest/test-js/context-validate lines); the list of existing fixtures you updated; and confirmation the gate was observed RED-before / GREEN-after. input.content:
"""TASK-069 / DEC-59 — A1 narrative-draft also emits a plan `title` (slice 8a-i).

The production trio (`planner.orchestration.run_trio_pass`) accumulates one
flat `draft_state` from the 14 specs; the NEXT slice (TASK-068) saves it as a
`Plan`. `Plan.title` is REQUIRED (CharField, not-null, no blank;
`plans/models/plan.py:94`) but no spec produced it. Per NORTH-STAR the admin
writes only current+desired state and the system PROPOSES the rest, so the
title is system-proposed; its home is A1 (a narrative scalar of-a-kind with the
four prose scalars A1 already emits). This slice extends A1 to also emit a
concise `title` that binds to `PlanForm.title`.

The four success criteria, each asserted below:
  1. A trio run's accumulated draft_state carries a non-empty `title` (within
     the 200-char Plan.title limit) that binds to PlanForm.title, reasoned by
     A1 from grounding (not a fixed placeholder).
  2. A1's existing four narrative scalars still parse and fold unchanged; an A1
     response lacking a title degrades to a partial prefill exactly as a
     missing scalar does today (no raise).
  3. The 0027 prompt-template migration is a GUARDED edit of the expected prior
     body (not a blind overwrite) and is reversible.
  4. (the whole-project gate + context-validate are run by the slice gate, not
     here.)

Named `slice_a1_title` so `make verify-slice SLICE=a1_title`
(→ `pytest -k slice_a1_title`) selects this file.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import importlib
import json
import uuid
from typing import Any

import pytest

from ai.models import PromptTemplate
from ai.services.llm.base import LLMResponse
from planner.forms import PlanForm
from planner.services import fold_form_step_prefill
from planner.specs import NARRATIVE_FIELDS, parse_narrative
from plans.models import PlanDraftState

# Reuse the trio slice's canned-output corpus + per-spec dispatch provider so
# the full-loop test exercises the SAME path the trio slice covers, differing
# only in the A1 output carrying a `title`.
from .test_slice_trio_pass import TRIO_ORDER, _canned_outputs

# A representative title-bearing A1 output: the five prior keys plus a concise
# title reasoned from the desired end state (a handful of words, well within
# the 200-char Plan.title limit).
A1_TITLE = "Schoolwide Reading Proficiency"
A1_JSON_WITH_TITLE = json.dumps(
    {
        "title": A1_TITLE,
        "current_state": "Reading proficiency sits below the cycle target.",
        "desired_state": "Every student reads at or above grade level.",
        "rationale": "Reading underpins access to the whole curriculum.",
        "student_impact_framing": "Improves outcomes for every student.",
        "provenance": "Drawn from the cycle self-study.",
    }
)


# --- Criterion 1: title parses, is a bare key, binds to PlanForm.title --------


def test_slice_a1_title_parse_keeps_title():
    """`parse_narrative` keeps a non-empty `title` from a title-bearing output."""
    result = parse_narrative(A1_JSON_WITH_TITLE)
    assert "title" in NARRATIVE_FIELDS
    assert result.prefill["title"] == A1_TITLE
    # Within the Plan.title 200-char limit.
    assert len(result.prefill["title"]) <= 200
    # The four prior prose scalars still parse unchanged (criterion 2).
    assert result.prefill["current_state"] == "Reading proficiency sits below the cycle target."
    assert result.prefill["desired_state"] == "Every student reads at or above grade level."
    assert result.prefill["rationale"] == "Reading underpins access to the whole curriculum."
    assert result.prefill["student_impact_framing"] == "Improves outcomes for every student."


def test_slice_a1_title_binds_to_planform_title():
    """A bare `title` prefill key binds to PlanForm.title (the basics step
    binds UNPREFIXED, so a bare key reaches the form field of the same name)."""
    prefill = parse_narrative(A1_JSON_WITH_TITLE).prefill
    folded = fold_form_step_prefill({}, prefill)
    assert folded["title"] == A1_TITLE
    # The form field exists and accepts the value (binding proof).
    assert "title" in PlanForm.base_fields
    form = PlanForm(data={"title": folded["title"]})
    form.is_valid()  # may be invalid for OTHER required fields; title must clean.
    assert "title" not in form.errors
    assert form.cleaned_data.get("title") == A1_TITLE


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_a1_title_in_trio_draft_state(trio_school, author, monkeypatch):
    """A full `run_trio_pass` (mocked LLM) yields an accumulated draft_state
    carrying a non-empty bare `title` (criterion 1, end-to-end)."""
    from planner import orchestration

    # The trio canned corpus with the A1 output swapped for the title-bearing
    # one; every other spec output is unchanged.
    canned = _canned_outputs()
    canned["narrative-draft"] = A1_JSON_WITH_TITLE
    outputs_in_order = [(key, canned[key]) for key in TRIO_ORDER]

    class _Dispatch:
        def __init__(self) -> None:
            self._idx = 0

        def complete(self, messages: list[dict], **kwargs: Any) -> LLMResponse:
            _key, content = outputs_in_order[self._idx]
            self._idx += 1
            return LLMResponse(
                content=content,
                model="fake-model",
                provider="anthropic",
                usage={"input_tokens": 4, "output_tokens": 6},
            )

    provider = _Dispatch()
    monkeypatch.setattr(orchestration, "get_llm_logged", lambda user=None, provider_name=None: provider)

    school = trio_school["school"]
    draft_id = uuid.uuid4()
    _, final_version, final_state, degraded = orchestration.run_trio_pass(
        school, author, draft_id, seed="raise reading", entry_mode="trio"
    )

    assert degraded == []
    assert final_version == 14
    # The accumulated draft_state carries a non-empty bare title within bounds.
    assert isinstance(final_state.get("title"), str)
    assert final_state["title"].strip()
    assert len(final_state["title"]) <= 200
    assert final_state["title"] == A1_TITLE


# --- Criterion 2: a title-less A1 output still parses (partial, no raise) ------


def test_slice_a1_title_absent_degrades_gracefully():
    """An A1 output WITHOUT a title still parses; the four prior scalars are
    unaffected and no key is fabricated for the missing title."""
    no_title = json.dumps(
        {
            "current_state": "Now.",
            "desired_state": "Later.",
            "rationale": "Because.",
            "student_impact_framing": "For students.",
            "provenance": "From data.",
        }
    )
    result = parse_narrative(no_title)
    # No raise; the four prior scalars present; no title key invented.
    assert "title" not in result.prefill
    assert result.prefill["current_state"] == "Now."
    assert result.prefill["desired_state"] == "Later."
    assert result.prefill["rationale"] == "Because."
    assert result.prefill["student_impact_framing"] == "For students."


def test_slice_a1_title_empty_title_omitted():
    """An empty-string title is omitted gracefully (a partial prefill), exactly
    as an empty prose scalar is today."""
    empty_title = json.dumps(
        {
            "title": "",
            "current_state": "Only this matters.",
        }
    )
    result = parse_narrative(empty_title)
    assert "title" not in result.prefill
    assert result.prefill == {"current_state": "Only this matters."}


# --- Criterion 3: the 0027 migration is a guarded, reversible edit ------------


def _load_0027():
    return importlib.import_module("ai.migrations.0027_add_title_to_plan_narrative_draft")


def test_slice_a1_title_migration_guard_is_byte_exact():
    """The 0027 forward guard targets the EXACT expected prior contract body,
    and the new body adds the title key + says six (not five)."""
    mod = _load_0027()
    # The guarded old/new contract substrings are module constants.
    assert "five" in mod.OLD_CONTRACT
    assert '"title"' not in mod.OLD_CONTRACT
    assert "six" in mod.NEW_CONTRACT
    assert '"title"' in mod.NEW_CONTRACT
    # The old name-line says five; the new says six.
    assert "five output keys" in mod.OLD_NAME_LINE
    assert "six output keys" in mod.NEW_NAME_LINE


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_a1_title_migration_forward_then_reverse_round_trips(db):
    """The migrated body carries the title contract; the reverse restores the
    pre-0027 body byte-for-byte (a reversible guarded edit)."""
    mod = _load_0027()
    from django.apps import apps as global_apps

    # The test DB has 0027 applied, so the live body already carries the title.
    tpl = PromptTemplate.objects.get(slug="plan-narrative-draft")
    applied_body = tpl.template
    assert '"title"' in applied_body
    assert mod.NEW_CONTRACT in applied_body
    assert mod.NEW_NAME_LINE in applied_body
    assert mod.OLD_CONTRACT not in applied_body

    # Reverse: restores the prior contract + name-line, removes the title key.
    mod.backwards(global_apps, None)
    reverted = PromptTemplate.objects.get(slug="plan-narrative-draft").template
    assert mod.OLD_CONTRACT in reverted
    assert mod.OLD_NAME_LINE in reverted
    assert mod.NEW_CONTRACT not in reverted

    # Forward again: re-applies the title contract (guarded, idempotent).
    mod.forwards(global_apps, None)
    reapplied = PromptTemplate.objects.get(slug="plan-narrative-draft").template
    assert reapplied == applied_body

input.content:
# Adds a `title` key to the `plan-narrative-draft` (A1) PromptTemplate output
# contract (DEC-59 / TASK-069, trio slice 8a-i).
#
# Aim: the production trio (`planner.orchestration.run_trio_pass`) accumulates
# one flat `draft_state` from the 14 specs and (next slice, TASK-068) saves it
# as a `Plan`. `Plan.title` is REQUIRED (CharField, not-null, no blank;
# `plans/models/plan.py:94`) but no spec produced it. Per NORTH-STAR the admin
# writes only current+desired state and the system PROPOSES the rest, so the
# title is system-proposed; its home is A1, a narrative scalar of-a-kind with
# the four prose scalars A1 already emits. This migration extends A1's prompt
# body so the model ALSO returns a concise `title`, derived from the desired
# end state, in the same JSON object — consistent with the existing
# no-fabrication / voice constraints (the body's preamble already carries
# those; this change touches only the OUTPUT CONTRACT block and the one
# name-line that enumerates the output keys).
#
# Pattern mirrors ai/0020 / ai/0021 / ai/0016: a GUARDED byte-match string
# replacement on the single `plan-narrative-draft` row. Forward replaces the
# EXACT post-0026 contract block + the matching name-line with the title-
# bearing variants, only when the OLD substrings are present AND the NEW ones
# are not yet present (so a re-run is a no-op and an admin-edited body that no
# longer byte-matches OLD is left untouched). Backward is the symmetric guard:
# revert only when NEW is present. The body's prior text is preserved
# everywhere except the two replaced regions.
#
# The contract previously asked for "EXACTLY these five string-valued keys";
# it now asks for six and lists `title` first. The single name-line that read
# "Every name in the five output keys ..." now reads "... six output keys ..."
# and includes the title in the same verbatim-from-catalogue rule.

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any

from django.db import migrations

TEMPLATE_SLUG = "plan-narrative-draft"

# The OUTPUT CONTRACT block as it stands in production after ai/0026.
# Byte-exact (verified by reading the migrated row off the dev DB).
OLD_CONTRACT = (
    "The object MUST have EXACTLY these five string-valued keys:\n"
    '- "current_state": where the school is today on this focus, in concrete terms.\n'
    '- "desired_state": the specific improved state the plan aims to reach.\n'
    '- "rationale": why this improvement matters now, grounded in the facts above.\n'
    '- "student_impact_framing": how this improvement is expected to affect students.\n'
    '- "provenance": what evidence, data, or prior work this plan draws on.\n'
    "\n"
    "Each value is a single plain-prose string (no nested objects, no lists, "
    "no markdown). Include all five keys."
)

# The title-bearing contract. `title` is listed first and described as a short
# plan title (a handful of words, within 200 chars) naming the improvement
# thrust, derived from the desired end state. The no-fabrication / voice
# constraints in the body preamble already bind every value, so the title
# carries the same "name only enumerated catalogue entities" rule the
# name-line (replaced below) makes explicit.
NEW_CONTRACT = (
    "The object MUST have EXACTLY these six string-valued keys:\n"
    '- "title": a concise plan title (a handful of words, at most 200 '
    "characters) naming the improvement thrust, derived from the desired end "
    "state. Not a sentence; no trailing period. Plain, factual, school-"
    "specific — the same dry report register as the other values.\n"
    '- "current_state": where the school is today on this focus, in concrete terms.\n'
    '- "desired_state": the specific improved state the plan aims to reach.\n'
    '- "rationale": why this improvement matters now, grounded in the facts above.\n'
    '- "student_impact_framing": how this improvement is expected to affect students.\n'
    '- "provenance": what evidence, data, or prior work this plan draws on.\n'
    "\n"
    "Each value is a single plain-prose string (no nested objects, no lists, "
    "no markdown). Include all six keys."
)

# The single line that enumerates the output keys for the verbatim-from-
# catalogue naming rule. It precedes the OUTPUT CONTRACT block.
OLD_NAME_LINE = (
    "Every name in the five output keys (a division, a position, a policy, a "
    "year-group, a guiding clause, an accreditation standard, a learner "
    "outcome, an area for improvement, a stakeholder group, a prior plan) MUST "
    "be drawn verbatim from the enumerated catalogues above; do not name an "
    "entity not present."
)

NEW_NAME_LINE = (
    "Every name in the six output keys (a division, a position, a policy, a "
    "year-group, a guiding clause, an accreditation standard, a learner "
    "outcome, an area for improvement, a stakeholder group, a prior plan) MUST "
    "be drawn verbatim from the enumerated catalogues above; do not name an "
    "entity not present."
)


def forwards(apps: Any, schema_editor: Any) -> None:
    """Add the `title` key to the A1 output contract — guarded byte-match.

    The row's body is updated only when both NEW substrings are absent and
    both OLD substrings are present (a row admin-edited away from OLD, or one
    already migrated, is left untouched). Each substring is replaced once;
    the body's other text is preserved. Saved with
    `update_fields=["template"]` so unrelated columns stay intact."""
    PromptTemplate = apps.get_model("ai", "PromptTemplate")
    tmpl = PromptTemplate.objects.filter(slug=TEMPLATE_SLUG).first()
    if tmpl is None:
        return
    body = tmpl.template
    if NEW_CONTRACT in body or NEW_NAME_LINE in body:
        return
    if OLD_CONTRACT not in body or OLD_NAME_LINE not in body:
        return
    body = body.replace(OLD_NAME_LINE, NEW_NAME_LINE)
    body = body.replace(OLD_CONTRACT, NEW_CONTRACT)
    tmpl.template = body
    tmpl.save(update_fields=["template"])


def backwards(apps: Any, schema_editor: Any) -> None:
    """Remove the `title` key from the A1 output contract — symmetric guard.

    Reverted only when both NEW substrings are present; a body re-edited away
    from NEW is left untouched. Restores the pre-0027 contract + name-line
    byte-for-byte."""
    PromptTemplate = apps.get_model("ai", "PromptTemplate")
    tmpl = PromptTemplate.objects.filter(slug=TEMPLATE_SLUG).first()
    if tmpl is None:
        return
    body = tmpl.template
    if NEW_CONTRACT not in body or NEW_NAME_LINE not in body:
        return
    body = body.replace(NEW_NAME_LINE, OLD_NAME_LINE)
    body = body.replace(NEW_CONTRACT, OLD_CONTRACT)
    tmpl.template = body
    tmpl.save(update_fields=["template"])

## The contract (the 4 success criteria — verify EACH adversarially, by id)
1. A trio run's accumulated `draft_state` carries a non-empty `title` (within the `Plan.title` 200-char limit) that binds to `PlanForm.title`, produced by A1's reasoning from its grounding — NOT a fixed/placeholder string.
2. A1's existing four narrative scalars (`current_state`, `desired_state`, `rationale`, `student_impact_framing`) still parse and fold unchanged; an A1 response lacking a title degrades gracefully to a partial prefill exactly as a missing scalar does today.
3. The production prompt-template change applies only to the expected prior template body (a GUARDED change, not a blind overwrite) AND is reversible (the reverse restores the prior body byte-for-byte).
4. The whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates.

## What the IMPL claims it did (verify, do not trust)
- Added `"title"` to `NARRATIVE_FIELDS` (`planner/specs.py:403`, now a 6-tuple, title first) + updated `parse_narrative` docstring/raise-message count language.

- New gate-first test `planner/tests/test_slice_a1_title.py`.
- Title instruction wording added to the template: a concise plan title (a handful of words, ≤200 chars) naming the improvement thrust, derived from the desired end state, no trailing period, same dry report register.

## Adversarial probes (do these, plus anything else you find)
- **Criterion 1 — non-vacuous flow.** Does a REAL `run_trio_pass` (mocked LLM) actually land a bare `title` key in the accumulated `draft_state`, and does it bind to `PlanForm.title`? Would the trio test FAIL if `title` were dropped from the fold/prefill (i.e. is it asserting the right thing, not a tautology over the canned input)? Is the 200-char limit actually respected/asserted, or just claimed? Is the title genuinely model-produced (the template instructs it) vs hardcoded anywhere in production code (grep for a literal default title)?
- **Criterion 2 — degrade + no regression.** Construct an A1 output with the 4 scalars but NO title — does `parse_narrative` keep the 4 and omit title without raising? Does an output with NONE of the 6 still raise as before? Did the count-language edits miss any spot (grep specs.py for "five"/"5" referring to narrative keys)? - **Criterion 3 — guard soundness (probe hard).** Apply `0027` forward then backward on a scratch/test DB and assert the body returns BYTE-FOR-BYTE to the pre-0027 state (`migrate ai 0026` → capture body → `migrate ai 0027` → `migrate ai 0026` → compare). Does forward refuse/no-op safely if the body is NOT the expected state (e.g. would it raise rather than silently corrupt)? Are the OLD/NEW guard substrings actually present/absent as claimed? Did the contract's key-COUNT language ("five"→"six") get updated everywhere in the body, or is there now an internal inconsistency (says "six" in one place, lists/says "five" elsewhere)?

context: cancel TASK-012 + re-home FGAP-024 (canonical reconciliation to DEC-57)

User-directed reconciliation of the workshop-runner .context neighborhood to DEC-57's
workshop retirement.

- TASK-012 (the workshop-era "per-spec quality evaluation toward a promotion-ready
  whole-plan draft" = the DISC-26 goal) was status=in-progress under the retired
  PHASE-PROMPTWORKSHOP-RUNNER / FEAT-004 -- a false statement of project state. Set to
  CANCELLED: not completed (the goal was never achieved by the workshop), not blocked
  (its approach is dead); DEC-57 retired the workshop runner while the trio
  (FEAT-010 / US-LLM-25 gate-closure) carries the goal. Keeping it as a "DISC-26 tracker"
  was rejected -- the trio's own FEAT-010 tasks track that goal.
- FGAP-024 is NOT a workshop-quality gap and is NOT closed by the cancellation: it is an
  OPEN production-security gap (the PromptSanitizer role-token injection check is evadable
  by Unicode glyph obfuscation on production PromptTemplate.render, which the trio render
  path uses). It stays identified/open as standalone debt awaiting its own
  canonical-pipeline pass. Its stale task_addresses_gap edge to the cancelled TASK-012
  could not be removed via remove-relation (a legacy refname-as-oid edge form); left in
  place rather than hand-editing relations.json -- logged as pi-context friction CTX-15
  in PI-WORKFLOWS-FINDINGS.md.

(state.mjs append does not mint one when absent) -- assigned seq 106/107 -- and re-scopes
the workshop-reconciliation item to the resolved/remaining split (remaining: TASK-037
planned, the phase/feature/story terminal status, and FGAP-024's own pass). Appends the
decision to ORCHESTRATOR-LOG. context-validate: 0 errors.

input.prompt:
You are the IMPL agent for one atomic slice of the WASC school-improvement-plan Django app. Execute it gate-first. STOP and report (do not guess) on any spec ambiguity or if a stated fact does not match source. Work only within this slice's scope; touch no unrelated code. All `manage.py`/`uv`/`make` runs are from `/Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan/school-improvement-plans`. You are on git branch `trio-save-draft` (already created off main) — commit there.

# Slice 8a-ii — save the accumulated trio draft_state → a saved proposed Plan

## What this is
The production "trio" (`planner/orchestration.py:run_trio_pass`) drives 14 dependency-ordered LLM specs against a school's catalogue, accumulating ONE flat `draft_state` dict (the form-POST key grammar `{prefix}-{i}-{field}`, nested `{prefix}-{i}-{segment}-{k}-{field}`, two-level `criteria-{i}-measurement-{j}-channel-{k}-...`). It returns `(draft_id, final_version, final_draft_state, degraded)`. THIS slice adds the server-side function that persists that accumulated flat `draft_state` into a saved `Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED)`, owned by the school+author, with the full child graph. It is the prerequisite for slice 8b (the promotion gate runs on a SAVED Plan).

OUT OF SCOPE (do not build, do not call): `_check_promotion_gates`, any gate-closure re-invoke loop, `advance_plan_status`/admin-promote, Flag-row persistence. Those are slices 8b/9.

## Success criteria (the contract — your tests must verify EACH; loop until all hold)
1. The accumulated flat `draft_state` from `run_trio_pass` is saved as a `Plan` in `lifecycle=PROPOSED`, owned by the originating school and author, with the full graph populated: plan scalars, every formset row, every nested child, and every cross-row binding the `draft_state` carried.
2. The save round-trips: re-reading the saved Plan reproduces the `draft_state`'s content (scalar values, formset row counts, nested children, and bindings) with nothing dropped, duplicated, or reordered into a different binding.
3. For a given `draft_state`, the saved Plan graph is identical to what the existing browser one-shot create flow persists from the same posted data (parity), and the save is atomic all-or-nothing: a `draft_state` whose forms/formsets fail validation persists no Plan and surfaces the validation failure rather than a partial or invalid Plan.
4. The whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates. (The promotion-gate check, the gate-closure re-invoke loop, admin-promote, and Flag-row persistence are out of scope — slices 8b/9.)

## Established mechanism facts (verified against current source — build on these; if any is false, STOP and report)
- The browser one-shot create path is `PlanCreateView.post` (`planner/views.py:264-303`): `data = request.POST` → `objects = self._build_context_objects(data=data)` (`views.py:128-143`) → validate every object's `.is_valid()` (no short-circuit) AND `self._validate_nested(data, objects)` (`views.py:379+`) → inside ONE `transaction.atomic()`: `plan = self._save_plan(request, objects)` (`views.py:305-314`, which sets `plan.author`, `plan.school`, `plan.lifecycle = Lifecycle.PROPOSED`, saves, `save_m2m`) then `save_nested(plan, data, objects, school)` (`planner/formsets.py:470+`) then `self._save_flag_requests(plan, data)`. A `ValidationError` from inside the atomic block rolls everything back (no partial Plan).
- Forms/formsets bind `data=` as POST data; multi-row fields use `getlist`, so `data` must be a Django `QueryDict`, not a plain dict. `collectDraftState` (`planner/static/wizard/js/ai-assist.js:143`) stores ONE value per field name, and the planner models M2M as extra-FORMSETS of single-FK rows — so the `draft_state` is single-valued-per-key and a `QueryDict` built with one value per key is faithful to what the browser posts.
- TWO required `Plan` fields are NOT in the accumulated `draft_state` and must be supplied by THIS save (per DEC-59):
  - `title` (CharField, not-null): as of slice 8a-i (merged), A1 narrative-draft NOW emits a bare `title` key into the draft_state (it binds to the unprefixed `PlanForm.title`). So a real `run_trio_pass` draft_state already carries `title` — your tests driving `run_trio_pass` will have it. Do NOT inject a title.
  - `cycle` (FK `school.Cycle`, PROTECT, not-null): NOT produced by any spec. DERIVE it via `ai.services.grounding._active_cycle(school)` (the same active-cycle deriver the trio's grounding uses) and inject it into the bind data as the bare `cycle` key (the value `PlanForm.cycle` expects — confirm whether that is the cycle PK; bind accordingly). If `_active_cycle(school)` returns None (a school with no active cycle), the save must SURFACE that as a clear precondition failure — never fabricate or silently skip. `PlanForm.Meta.fields` (`planner/forms.py:~293`) includes `title`, `title_zh_hans`, `cycle`, the prose scalars; the basics step binds UNPREFIXED so bare keys (`title`, `cycle`, `current_state`, …) bind to it.
- THE NEW PIECE — management-form synthesis: the accumulated `draft_state` carries NO formset management-form fields. The seam folds (`planner/services.py`) write only `{prefix}-{i}-{field}` row keys and never synthesize `{prefix}-TOTAL_FORMS`/`-INITIAL_FORMS` (`services.py:94,148-149`); `run_trio_pass` starts from `{}`. Django formsets REFUSE to bind without a management form. So this slice must synthesize the management-form fields for EVERY formset prefix present in the `draft_state` before binding — at every level:
  - top-level step formsets + the extra-formsets (e.g. the A3 relation formsets on `basics`),
  - nested children: `criteria-{i}-measurement`, `criteria-{i}-measurement-{j}-channel`, `steps-{i}-{seg}` for each segment in `planner.formsets.ACTIONSTEP_CHILD_FORMSETS` (`formsets.py:423`), `steps-{i}-evidence` (`ACTIONSTEP_EVIDENCE`, `formsets.py:441`), `review_events-{i}-input` (`REVIEWEVENT_INPUT`, `formsets.py:436`).
  Derive each prefix's row count from the `{prefix}-(\d+)-` row indices present in the `draft_state`; set `TOTAL_FORMS` = max-index+1, `INITIAL_FORMS` = 0 (all rows new), plus whatever else each formset's management form requires (`MIN_NUM_FORMS`/`MAX_NUM_FORMS` — read the formset class). `_validate_nested` (`views.py:379+`) shows the exact nested prefix construction to mirror.
- `Lifecycle.PROPOSED` is what `_save_plan` stamps; import it where `views.py` does.

## How to build it
- Honor criterion 3 (parity, no divergent save logic): REUSE the existing view bind→validate→`_save_plan`+`save_nested`+`_save_flag_requests` sequence rather than reimplementing a parallel save. Choose the cleanest reuse (e.g. extract the view's POST save body into a shared module-level function that both `PlanCreateView.post` and the trio-save call, OR drive the existing view methods). If you refactor the view, its existing behavior must be byte-for-byte preserved (its tests stay green). Author identity = the trio's `author`; school = the trio's `school`.
- Home: put the new save-from-draft_state function where `run_trio_pass` can call it (`planner/orchestration.py` or `planner/services.py` — your call; keep imports clean, avoid a circular import with views). Signature: takes the accumulated flat `draft_state`, the `school`, and the `author`; returns the saved `Plan`; raises/surfaces the validation failure atomically on invalid input. It must NOT touch the promotion gate.

## Gate-first verification (mandatory order)
1. FIRST author `planner/tests/test_slice_save_draft.py` (the harness selects it via `pytest -k slice_save_draft`, so the file + test function names must contain `slice_save_draft`). Assert EACH success criterion with a REALISTIC draft_state — prefer driving `run_trio_pass` with the mocked LLM (shared `trio_school` fixture in `planner/tests/conftest.py`; the canned-dispatch pattern in `test_slice_trio_pass.py`/`test_slice_resilience.py`) so the saved Plan reflects a genuine accumulated draft_state spanning scalars + formset rows + nested children + bindings. Include: (a) full-graph save + `lifecycle==PROPOSED` + owner + derived cycle; (b) round-trip (re-read the Plan; counts/values/bindings match the draft_state); (c) PARITY — POST the SAME data (the draft_state + synthesized management forms + injected cycle) through `PlanCreateView` and assert the resulting Plan graph is identical to the trio-save graph; (d) atomic rejection — an invalid draft_state persists no Plan and surfaces the failure; (e) the no-active-cycle precondition surfaces a clear failure, not a fabricated/None cycle.
2. Observe the new tests RED before implementing (run them, confirm failure for the right reason).
3. Implement to GREEN.
4. Run the slice gate: `make verify-slice SLICE=save_draft` from `school-improvement-plans/`. It must exit 0 (BLOCKING: your slice tests + ruff + mypy + full pytest + make test-js + context-validate). Do not weaken the gate. Do not commit on a red or unobserved gate.

## Commit + report
- Commit on branch `trio-save-draft` with a forensic message (speak to aims/intentions, not unwarranted certainty; no author-credit lines). Use `git commit -F` from a file in the SYSTEM `/tmp` (NOT the repo's tracked `tmp/`) if the message has shell-special chars. Working tree clean after.
- Report: the commit SHA; files changed; how you handled management-form synthesis, the cycle derivation/injection, and the view-reuse (parity); the exact `make verify-slice SLICE=save_draft` tail (exit 0 + the ruff/mypy/pytest/test-js/context-validate lines); confirmation the gate was observed RED-before / GREEN-after. If any stated fact was false or anything is ambiguous, STOP and report instead of guessing.

input.content:
"""TASK (FEAT-010 slice 8a-ii) — save the accumulated trio draft_state → a saved Plan.

Covers `planner.orchestration.save_draft_state_as_plan`: the server-side function
that persists the flat `draft_state` accumulated by `run_trio_pass` into a saved
`Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED)`, owned by the originating school + author, with the
full child graph — synthesizing the formset management forms the folds never
write, deriving + injecting the active `cycle` (DEC-59), and reusing the EXISTING
`PlanCreateView` bind→validate→save path so the saved graph is byte-for-byte the
browser one-shot create's graph (parity).

OUT OF SCOPE (slices 8b/9): the promotion gate, the gate-closure re-invoke loop,
admin-promote, Flag-row persistence.

Named `slice_save_draft` so `make verify-slice SLICE=save_draft` (→ `pytest -k
slice_save_draft`) selects this file.

The realistic `draft_state` under test is produced by driving `run_trio_pass`
against the enriched `trio_school` with the per-spec canned LLM dispatcher (the
SAME pattern + canned outputs as `test_slice_trio_pass.py`), so the saved Plan
reflects a genuine accumulation spanning scalars + formset rows + nested children
+ cross-row bindings.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid

import pytest
from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError

from plans.choices import Lifecycle
from plans.models import (
    ActionStep,
    FeedbackChannel,
    Measurement,
    MeasurementChannel,
    Milestone,
    Plan,
    PlanAccreditationStandard,
    ReviewEvent,
    SuccessCriterion,
)
from school.models import Cycle

# Reuse the per-spec canned dispatcher + the 14 canned outputs from the trio_pass
# slice so the draft_state under test is a real accumulation, not a hand-built
# stub. `fake_trio_llm` is defined in that module's fixture; re-declare an
# equivalent fixture here patching the same symbol.
from .test_slice_trio_pass import TRIO_ORDER, _canned_outputs, _SpecDispatchProvider


@pytest.fixture
def fake_trio_llm(monkeypatch):
    """Patch `planner.orchestration.get_llm_logged` to the per-spec dispatcher."""
    canned = _canned_outputs()
    outputs_in_order = [(key, canned[key]) for key in TRIO_ORDER]
    provider = _SpecDispatchProvider(outputs_in_order)

    def _factory(user=None, provider_name=None):
        return provider

    monkeypatch.setattr("planner.orchestration.get_llm_logged", _factory)
    return provider


def _accumulated_draft_state(school, author):
    """Drive a full trio pass and return its final accumulated draft_state."""
    from planner.orchestration import run_trio_pass

    _, final_version, final_state, degraded = run_trio_pass(
        school, author, uuid.uuid4(), seed="raise reading", entry_mode="trio"
    )
    assert degraded == [], degraded
    assert final_version == 14
    return final_state


# --- Criterion 1 + (cycle/owner/lifecycle) ------------------------------------


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_save_draft_persists_full_graph_proposed_owned_with_cycle(
    trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm
):
    from planner.orchestration import save_draft_state_as_plan

    school = trio_school["school"]
    draft_state = _accumulated_draft_state(school, author)

    plan = save_draft_state_as_plan(draft_state, school=school, author=author)

    # Saved, owned, PROPOSED, cycle derived (the active cycle of the school).
    assert plan.pk is not None
    assert plan.lifecycle == Lifecycle.PROPOSED
    assert plan.school == school
    assert plan.author == author
    expected_cycle = Cycle.objects.filter(school=school).order_by("-starts_on").first()
    assert plan.cycle == expected_cycle

    # Plan scalars came from the A1 narrative draft.
    assert plan.title == "Schoolwide Reading Proficiency"
    assert plan.current_state == "Reading proficiency sits below the cycle target."

    # The full child graph is populated (every formset class the draft_state carried).
    assert SuccessCriterion.objects.filter(plan=plan).count() == 1
    assert FeedbackChannel.objects.filter(plan=plan).count() == 1
    assert Milestone.objects.filter(plan=plan).count() == 1
    assert ActionStep.objects.filter(plan=plan).count() == 2
    assert PlanAccreditationStandard.objects.filter(plan=plan).count() == 1
    assert ReviewEvent.objects.filter(plan=plan).count() == 1
    # Cross-row binding: the criterion's measurement → channel resolved to the
    # saved feedback channel (B2 binding round-tripped through save).
    measurement = Measurement.objects.get(criterion__plan=plan)
    channel = MeasurementChannel.objects.get(measurement=measurement)
    fb = FeedbackChannel.objects.get(plan=plan)
    assert channel.channel_id == fb.pk


# --- Criterion 2: round-trip --------------------------------------------------


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_save_draft_round_trips_draft_state_content(trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm):
    """Re-reading the saved Plan reproduces the draft_state's content: scalar
    values, formset row counts, nested children, and bindings — nothing dropped,
    duplicated, or reordered into a different binding."""
    from planner.orchestration import save_draft_state_as_plan

    school = trio_school["school"]
    draft_state = _accumulated_draft_state(school, author)
    plan = save_draft_state_as_plan(draft_state, school=school, author=author)

    # Scalars.
    assert plan.desired_state == draft_state["desired_state"]
    assert plan.rationale == draft_state["rationale"]

    # Formset row counts == the draft_state's per-prefix row counts.
    steps = list(ActionStep.objects.filter(plan=plan).order_by("order"))
    assert len(steps) == 2
    assert steps[0].description == draft_state["steps-0-description"]
    assert steps[1].description == draft_state["steps-1-description"]

    # Nested children of step 0: one assignment, one timeline, one resource, one
    # substep, one evidence — exactly the draft_state's per-step child rows.
    step0 = steps[0]
    assert step0.assignments.count() == 1
    assert step0.timelines.count() == 1
    assert step0.required_resources.count() == 1
    assert step0.substeps.count() == 1
    assert step0.substeps.first().description == draft_state["steps-0-substep-0-description"]

    # Standard binding.
    std_row = PlanAccreditationStandard.objects.get(plan=plan)
    assert std_row.rationale == draft_state["standards-0-rationale"]


# --- Criterion 3a: parity with the browser one-shot create --------------------


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_save_draft_parity_with_browser_create(
    trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm, client
):
    """POSTing the SAME data (the draft_state + synthesized management forms +
    injected cycle) through `PlanCreateView` yields a Plan graph IDENTICAL to the
    trio-save graph."""
    from planner.orchestration import (
        build_create_post_data,
        save_draft_state_as_plan,
    )

    school = trio_school["school"]
    draft_state = _accumulated_draft_state(school, author)

    # Trio-save graph.
    trio_plan = save_draft_state_as_plan(draft_state, school=school, author=author)

    # Browser-create graph from the SAME bind data.
    post_data = build_create_post_data(draft_state, school=school)
    client.force_login(author)
    resp = client.post("/planner/create/", data=dict(post_data.lists()))
    assert resp.status_code == 302, getattr(resp, "content", b"")[:2000]
    browser_plan = Plan.objects.exclude(pk=trio_plan.pk).get(author=author)

    # Identical graph shape: same scalars, same per-formset counts, same bindings.
    assert browser_plan.title == trio_plan.title
    assert browser_plan.current_state == trio_plan.current_state
    assert browser_plan.cycle_id == trio_plan.cycle_id
    assert browser_plan.lifecycle == trio_plan.lifecycle == Lifecycle.PROPOSED

    for model, related in (
        (SuccessCriterion, "plan"),
        (FeedbackChannel, "plan"),
        (Milestone, "plan"),
        (ActionStep, "plan"),
        (PlanAccreditationStandard, "plan"),
        (ReviewEvent, "plan"),
    ):
        assert model.objects.filter(**{related: trio_plan}).count() == model.objects.filter(
            **{related: browser_plan}
        ).count()

    # The B2 binding resolved to the same create-order channel in both graphs.
    for p in (trio_plan, browser_plan):
        m = Measurement.objects.get(criterion__plan=p)
        ch = MeasurementChannel.objects.get(measurement=m)
        assert ch.channel_id == FeedbackChannel.objects.get(plan=p).pk


# --- Criterion 3b: atomic all-or-nothing on invalid draft_state ---------------


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_save_draft_invalid_draft_state_persists_nothing(trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm):
    """A draft_state whose forms/formsets fail validation persists NO Plan and
    surfaces the validation failure (not a partial/invalid Plan)."""
    from planner.orchestration import save_draft_state_as_plan

    school = trio_school["school"]
    draft_state = dict(_accumulated_draft_state(school, author))

    # Corrupt a required scalar (blank the title the A1 draft supplied).
    draft_state["title"] = ""

    before = Plan.objects.count()
    with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
        save_draft_state_as_plan(draft_state, school=school, author=author)
    assert Plan.objects.count() == before


# --- Criterion 3c: no-active-cycle precondition surfaces a clear failure -------


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_save_draft_no_active_cycle_surfaces_precondition_failure(
    trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm
):
    """A school with no cycle surfaces a clear precondition failure — never a
    fabricated or None cycle, never a partial Plan."""
    from planner.orchestration import save_draft_state_as_plan

    school = trio_school["school"]
    draft_state = _accumulated_draft_state(school, author)

    # Remove the school's only cycle (must clear the draft_state's bare `cycle`
    # too if present, but the trio never emits one — the save derives it).
    Cycle.objects.filter(school=school).delete()
    assert Cycle.objects.filter(school=school).count() == 0

    before = Plan.objects.count()
    with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
        save_draft_state_as_plan(draft_state, school=school, author=author)
    assert Plan.objects.count() == before

def post(self, request: HttpRequest, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> HttpResponse:
        data = request.POST
        try:
            plan = self._bind_validate_and_save(data, author=request.user)
        except _BindValidationError as exc:
            # A form/formset / nested-shape failure, OR a `ValidationError`
            # raised from inside the atomic save (e.g. an unresolvable
            # milestone-timed carrier, US-UI-5). The bound `objects` carry the
            # field errors (and, for the atomic case, a flattened non-field
            # error on `basics`); re-render at HTTP 200 — never a 500 / DB
            # `IntegrityError`. The transaction (if any) already rolled back.
            return self._render(exc.objects)

        return HttpResponseRedirect(reverse("planner:draft_detail", kwargs={"pk": plan.pk}))

    def _bind_validate_and_save(self, data: Any, *, author: Any) -> Plan:
        """Build, validate, and atomically save the whole Plan graph from `data`.

        The shared create body behind BOTH the browser one-shot POST
        (`PlanCreateView.post`) and the trio draft_state save
        (`planner.orchestration.save_draft_state_as_plan`), so the two paths
        persist a byte-for-byte identical graph (parity). Steps, in order:

          1. build every step's primary + extra + nested objects bound to `data`,
          2. validate every primary form/formset (no short-circuit — all errors
             surface at once) AND the deeply-nested child formsets,
          3. inside ONE `transaction.atomic()`: save the Plan row
             (`author`/`school`/`lifecycle=PROPOSED` stamped), then the child
             graph (`save_nested`), then the flag requests.

        On a validation failure (parent/nested invalid, or a `ValidationError`
        from inside the atomic block) it raises `_BindValidationError` carrying
        the bound `objects` so the caller decides how to surface it: the view
        re-renders them; the trio save re-raises a plain `ValidationError`. The
        transaction rolls back on the atomic-block failure, so a partial Plan is
        never persisted."""
        objects = self._build_context_objects(data=data)

        # Validate every primary form/formset. `is_valid()` is called on
        # all of them (no short-circuit) so the re-render shows every
        # section's errors at once.
        all_valid = True
        for obj in objects.values():
            if not obj.is_valid():
                all_valid = False

        # Validate the deeply-nested child formsets, bound to placeholder
        # parents purely for shape/clean validation. They are rebuilt
        # against the real saved parents inside `save_nested`.
        nested_valid = self._validate_nested(data, objects)
        if not (all_valid and nested_valid):
            raise _BindValidationError(objects)

        # `save_nested` resolves same-POST parent links (US-UI-5) by
        # create-order index AFTER the parents save. A milestone-timed
        # carrier whose `milestone_index` does not resolve raises a
        # `ValidationError` from inside the atomic block: catch it, roll the
        # transaction back (nothing is written), surface the message as a
        # non-field error on `basics`, and re-raise as a bind failure.
        try:
            with transaction.atomic():
                plan = self._save_plan_for(objects, author=author)
                save_nested(plan, data, objects, self._school())
                self._save_flag_requests(plan, data)
        except ValidationError as exc:
            # Flatten to plain messages so a field-keyed `ValidationError`
            # (e.g. `{"milestone_index": ...}`) attaches as a top-level
            # non-field error on the basics form — its keys are not fields
            # on `PlanForm`, so they cannot be assigned by name.
            objects["basics"].add_error(None, ValidationError(exc.messages))
            raise _BindValidationError(objects) from exc

        return plan

def post(self, request: HttpRequest, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> HttpResponse:
        data = request.POST
        objects = self._build_context_objects(data=data)

        # Validate every primary form/formset. `is_valid()` is called on
        # all of them (no short-circuit) so the re-render shows every
        # section's errors at once.
        all_valid = True
        for obj in objects.values():
            if not obj.is_valid():
                all_valid = False

        # Validate the deeply-nested child formsets, bound to placeholder
        # parents purely for shape/clean validation. They are rebuilt
        # against the real saved parents inside `save_nested`.
        nested_valid = self._validate_nested(data, objects)
        if not (all_valid and nested_valid):
            return self._render(objects)

        # `save_nested` resolves same-POST parent links (US-UI-5) by
        # create-order index AFTER the parents save. A milestone-timed
        # carrier whose `milestone_index` does not resolve raises a
        # `ValidationError` from inside the atomic block: catch it, roll the
        # transaction back (nothing is written), surface the message as a
        # non-field error, and re-render at HTTP 200 — never a 500 / DB
        # `IntegrityError`.
        try:
            with transaction.atomic():
                plan = self._save_plan(request, objects)
                save_nested(plan, data, objects, self._school())
                self._save_flag_requests(plan, data)
        except ValidationError as exc:
            # Flatten to plain messages so a field-keyed `ValidationError`
            # (e.g. `{"milestone_index": ...}`) attaches as a top-level
            # non-field error on the basics form — its keys are not fields
            # on `PlanForm`, so they cannot be assigned by name.
            objects["basics"].add_error(None, ValidationError(exc.messages))
            return self._render(objects)

        return HttpResponseRedirect(reverse("planner:draft_detail", kwargs={"pk": plan.pk}))

def _save_plan_for(self, objects: dict[str, Any], *, author: Any) -> Plan:
        """Save the Plan row itself, stamping author / school / lifecycle.

        `author` is passed explicitly (the browser POST threads `request.user`;
        the trio save threads its run author) so the one save body serves both
        entry paths. `school` stays `self._school()` — the school the view's
        objects were built + scoped against."""
        plan_form = objects["basics"]
        plan: Plan = plan_form.save(commit=False)
        plan.author = author
        plan.school = self._school()
        plan.lifecycle = Lifecycle.PROPOSED
        plan.save()
        plan_form.save_m2m()
        return plan
