input.prompt:
You are the IMPL agent for one atomic slice of the WASC school-improvement-plan Django app. Execute it gate-first. STOP and report (do not guess) on any spec ambiguity or if a stated fact does not match source. Work only within this slice's scope; touch no unrelated code. All `manage.py`/`uv`/`make` runs are from `/Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan/school-improvement-plans`. You are on git branch `trio-gate-closure` (already created off main) — commit there.

# Slice 8b — the US-LLM-25 gate-closure loop

## What this is
The production trio (`planner/orchestration.py:run_trio_pass`) accumulates a flat `draft_state` and `save_draft_state_as_plan(draft_state, *, school, author) -> Plan` saves it as a `Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED)`. But a saved trio draft is not guaranteed to pass the promotion gate. THIS slice adds a server-side loop that iterates the draft to a state that PASSES `plans/services.py:_check_promotion_gates`, by re-invoking the spec(s) that produce each unmet gate predicate, until the gate holds or a bounded cap is hit.

OUT OF SCOPE (do not build, do not call): flipping `plan.lifecycle` (admin-only via `advance_plan_status`), and persisting `Flag` rows for degraded specs. Those are slice #9.

## Success criteria (the contract — your tests must verify EACH; loop until all hold)
1. A draft_state that initially fails one or more spec-closable promotion-gate predicates is brought, by the loop, to a saved `Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED)` that PASSES `_check_promotion_gates` (the unmet predicates' producing specs are re-invoked and their output re-folded until the gate holds).
2. The loop terminates deterministically: bounded by a hard max-rounds cap; on cap-exhaustion with the gate still failing it returns the residual unmet predicate messages without aborting, raising, or looping unbounded.
3. An unmet predicate that no trio spec can close (e.g. no active cycle, or a Phase-14 clean()/flag condition) is reported as residual rather than retried; a spec-closable predicate is not misreported as residual.
4. The iteration leaves no orphan or partial Plan rows (probe rounds persist nothing); the loop does NOT change `plan.lifecycle` and does NOT write any `Flag` rows.
5. The whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates.

## Established mechanism facts (verified against current source — build on these; if any is false, STOP and report)
- `_check_promotion_gates(plan)` (`plans/services.py:98+`) aggregates ALL unmet predicates and `raise ValidationError(failures)` (a list of message strings); on success returns None. The messages are mostly distinct/substring-identifiable. Verbatim families: `"Plan field <name> must be non-empty."` (title/current_state/desired_state/rationale/student_impact_framing) and `"Plan field cycle must be set."`; `"At least one learner outcome must be targeted."`, `"...stakeholder-impact group..."`, `"...area for improvement..."`, `"...accreditation standard..."`; `"At least one success criterion is required."`, `"Success criterion <id> needs a measurement bound to a feedback channel."`; `"At least one action step is required."`, `"Action step <id> needs a responsible assignment."` / `"...a timeline."` / `"...a required resource."` / `"...at least one evidence artifact."`; `"Milestone <id> needs a planning method (its improvement type requires one)."`; `"At least one communication is required."`, `"At least one review event is required."`, `"Review event <id> needs at least one input."`, `"At least one revision rule is required."`; the `plan.clean()` messages (Phase-14 priority_rationale-on-deviation); `"The plan has unresolved must-fix flags..."`.
- `save_draft_state_as_plan` is CREATE-ONLY (each call makes a NEW Plan; no update path). So the loop must PROBE by saving inside a `transaction.atomic()`/savepoint that ROLLS BACK after gating (so probe rounds leave no Plan), and persist the real Plan exactly ONCE when the draft converges (gate would pass). `_check_promotion_gates` works on a Plan saved within a transaction (it queries relations by PK) even if that transaction is later rolled back.
- Re-invoke ONE spec: `get_spec(spec_key)` (the registry) + `_run_one_spec(spec, school, author, draft_state, seed, steering="")` (`orchestration.py:236+`) returns the resolved prefill, built from the CURRENT draft_state grounding. Fold it with the SAME router that `TRIO_SEQUENCE` uses for that spec_key (`orchestration.py` TRIO_SEQUENCE list). The folds are REPLACE-scoped, so re-invoking a spec replaces that spec's rows (no duplication). Reuse the existing per-spec run+fold path; do not reimplement folding.
- THE GATE-FAILURE -> SPEC MAPPING (derivable from TRIO_SEQUENCE + the predicates; verified): plan scalars title/current_state/desired_state/rationale/student_impact_framing -> `narrative-draft` (A1); learner_outcomes/areas_for_improvement/stakeholder_impact -> `propose-domain-alignment` (A3); accreditation standard -> `propose-accreditation-standards` (A2); success criterion -> `draft-success-criteria` (B1); criterion measurement-channel binding -> `bind-measurement-channels` (B2) — which needs feedback channels from `suggest-feedback-channels` (C1); action step -> `decompose-action-steps` (F1); step responsible/timeline/resource/evidence -> `propose-assignments`/`propose-timelines`/`propose-step-resources`/`propose-evidence` (F2/F3/F4/F5); milestone planning_method -> `propose-milestones` (D1); communications + review_events + review_event_inputs + revision_rules -> `propose-review-loop` (G1) — VERIFIED: G1's contract is `{communications, review_events, revision_rules}` each non-empty with inputs per event (specs.py:3760-3781), so G1 produces ALL FOUR review-loop predicates. RESIDUAL (no spec closes): `cycle` (the save derives it via `_active_cycle`; an absent active cycle is a precondition failure, surfaced not retried), the Phase-14 `plan.clean()` rule, and unresolved MUST_FIX flags (not produced/triggered by normal trio output) — these surface as residual.
- Continue/stop is the DETERMINISTIC gate (a ValidationError-or-not read), so the bounded autonomous loop is sound (DEC-58). Pick a hard max-rounds cap (a small constant, e.g. 3-5; mirror the orchestration retry-cap style).

## How to build it
- Add `run_gate_closure_loop(draft_state, *, school, author, seed="", max_rounds=<cap>) -> (Plan | None, dict, list[str])` (the residual messages) to `planner/orchestration.py`. It manages probing + the single final committed save internally. Per round: probe-save+gate+rollback → collect unmet → map unmet→specs → for each, re-invoke (`_run_one_spec`) + fold (the spec's TRIO_SEQUENCE router) into the draft_state → next round. When a probe shows the gate passes, do ONE real committed save and return `(plan, draft_state, [])`. If a round maps NO spec for the remaining unmet predicates (all residual), or the cap is hit, return `(None, draft_state, residual_messages)`.
- Build the failure->spec mapping cleanly (a small table or a derivation keyed off the verified mapping above). It must re-invoke ONLY the specs for the actually-unmet predicates each round (not the whole 14).
- Do NOT touch `_check_promotion_gates`, `advance_plan_status`, the `Flag` model, or `save_draft_state_as_plan`'s internals.

## Gate-first verification (mandatory order)
1. FIRST author `planner/tests/test_slice_gate_closure.py` (harness selects via `pytest -k slice_gate_closure`; file + test names must contain `slice_gate_closure`). With the LLM mocked (the `trio_school` conftest fixture + the canned-dispatch pattern in `test_slice_trio_pass.py`/`test_slice_resilience.py`), assert EACH criterion: (a) a draft_state failing a specific spec-closable predicate (e.g. zero criteria, or a missing step responsible-assignment) → after the loop, the saved Plan PASSES `_check_promotion_gates` and the re-invoked spec's rows are present; (b) cap-exhaustion — a predicate whose re-invoked spec keeps failing to satisfy it (mock returns non-satisfying output) → loop returns `(None, draft_state, residual)` with the residual message, bounded (assert it did not exceed the cap, e.g. by counting spec invocations), no raise; (c) a known-residual predicate (no spec maps) → reported residual, not retried; (d) no orphan Plan rows after a cap-exhausted run (probe rounds rolled back) and lifecycle stays PROPOSED on success; (e) no Flag rows written. Prefer driving a real (mocked) `run_trio_pass` accumulation, then perturbing it to fail a predicate, so the test is realistic.
2. Observe the new tests RED before implementing (for the right reason).
3. Implement to GREEN.
4. Run `make verify-slice SLICE=gate_closure` from `school-improvement-plans/` — must exit 0 (BLOCKING: your slice tests + ruff + mypy + full pytest + make test-js + context-validate). Do not weaken the gate; do not commit on a red/unobserved gate.

## Commit + report
- Commit on `trio-gate-closure` with a forensic message (aims/intentions, not unwarranted certainty; no author-credit lines). `git commit -F` from a SYSTEM `/tmp` file if the message has shell-special chars. Working tree clean after.
- Report: commit SHA; files changed; how the loop probes+rolls-back and persists once; the failure->spec mapping + how residuals are detected; the cap value + cap-exhaustion behavior; the exact `make verify-slice SLICE=gate_closure` tail (exit 0 + ruff/mypy/pytest/test-js/context-validate); confirmation RED-before/GREEN-after. If any stated fact was false or anything is ambiguous, STOP and report instead of guessing.

"""TASK (FEAT-010 slice 8b) — the US-LLM-25 gate-closure loop on the saved Plan.

Covers `planner.orchestration.run_gate_closure_loop`: the server-side loop that
takes an accumulated trio `draft_state` and iterates it to a state that PASSES
`plans.services._check_promotion_gates`, by re-invoking the spec(s) that produce
each unmet gate predicate (folding their fresh output back into the draft_state)
until the gate holds or a bounded max-rounds cap is hit.

Each round PROBES by saving the draft_state inside a `transaction.atomic()`
savepoint, runs `_check_promotion_gates`, and ROLLS BACK — so probe rounds leave
no Plan. When a probe shows the gate would pass, it does ONE real committed save
and returns `(plan, draft_state, [])`. An unmet predicate no trio spec can close
(no active cycle, the Phase-14 `plan.clean()` rule, unresolved must-fix flags) is
reported as RESIDUAL rather than retried; on cap-exhaustion (or an all-residual
round) it returns `(None, draft_state, residual_messages)` without raising.

OUT OF SCOPE (slice #9): flipping `plan.lifecycle`, persisting `Flag` rows.

Named `slice_gate_closure` so `make verify-slice SLICE=gate_closure`
(→ `pytest -k slice_gate_closure`) selects this file.

The realistic `draft_state` under test is a genuine accumulation produced by
driving `run_trio_pass` against the enriched `trio_school` with the per-spec
canned LLM dispatcher (the SAME pattern + canned outputs as
`test_slice_trio_pass.py`), then PERTURBED to fail a specific gate predicate so
the loop must re-invoke the producing spec.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from typing import Any

import pytest

from ai.models.llm import PromptTemplate
from ai.services.llm.base import LLMResponse
from plans.choices import Lifecycle
from plans.models import Flag, Plan, SuccessCriterion
from school.models import Cycle

from .test_slice_trio_pass import TRIO_ORDER, _canned_outputs, _SpecDispatchProvider


# --- A spec-keyed canned LLM dispatcher (re-invoke order is data-dependent). --
#
# The gate-closure loop re-invokes ONLY the specs whose predicate is unmet, in a
# data-dependent order — unlike the happy-path trio loop's fixed 14-call march.
# So this provider dispatches a canned output BY SPEC, recovering the spec_key
# from the rendered prompt: each spec renders a distinct `PromptTemplate`, whose
# literal text (the prose before the first `{{` placeholder) prefixes the
# rendered prompt. The provider matches that literal prefix to recover which
# spec is in flight and returns that spec's canned output.


def _template_literal_prefixes() -> dict[str, str]:
    """spec_key → the literal text prefix of its rendered prompt.

    Each spec's `PromptTemplate.body` begins with literal prose (the static
    instruction text) before the first `{{`/`{%` placeholder. `tpl.render(...)`
    leaves that prefix verbatim, so it identifies the spec in the rendered
    prompt. Keyed by spec_key via the registry's `template_slug`."""
    from planner.assist import get_spec

    prefixes: dict[str, str] = {}
    for spec_key in TRIO_ORDER:
        spec = get_spec(spec_key)
        assert spec is not None, spec_key
        body = PromptTemplate.objects.get(slug=spec.template_slug).template
        # Literal text up to the first Django-template token.
        cut = len(body)
        for token in ("{{", "{%"):
            i = body.find(token)
            if i != -1:
                cut = min(cut, i)
        literal = body[:cut].strip()
        prefixes[spec_key] = literal
    return prefixes


class _GateClosureProvider:
    """A fake LLM provider that dispatches a canned output BY recovered spec_key.

    Recovers the spec in flight from the rendered prompt (the spec's template
    literal prefix), returns that spec's `canned_for[spec_key]` content, and
    records the recovered spec_key per call so a test can assert the re-invoke
    sequence + bound the call count (cap-exhaustion non-vacuity)."""

    def __init__(self, prefixes: dict[str, str], canned_for: dict[str, str]) -> None:
        self._prefixes = prefixes
        self._canned_for = canned_for
        self.calls: list[str] = []

    def _recover_spec_key(self, prompt: str) -> str:
        # Longest-prefix-first so a spec whose literal text is a substring of
        # another's (unlikely, but safe) does not shadow the more-specific match.
        for spec_key in sorted(
            self._prefixes, key=lambda k: len(self._prefixes[k]), reverse=True
        ):
            if self._prefixes[spec_key] and self._prefixes[spec_key] in prompt:
                return spec_key
        raise AssertionError("could not recover spec_key from the rendered prompt")

    def complete(self, messages: list[dict], **kwargs: Any) -> LLMResponse:
        prompt = messages[-1]["content"]
        spec_key = self._recover_spec_key(prompt)
        self.calls.append(spec_key)
        content = self._canned_for[spec_key]
        return LLMResponse(
            content=content,
            model="fake-model",
            provider="anthropic",
            usage={"input_tokens": 4, "output_tokens": 6},
            raw={"raw": True},
        )


@pytest.fixture
def fake_trio_llm(monkeypatch):
    """Patch `planner.orchestration.get_llm_logged` to the fixed-order per-spec
    dispatcher (drives the initial `run_trio_pass` accumulation)."""
    canned = _canned_outputs()
    outputs_in_order = [(key, canned[key]) for key in TRIO_ORDER]
    provider = _SpecDispatchProvider(outputs_in_order)

    def _factory(user=None, provider_name=None):
        return provider

    monkeypatch.setattr("planner.orchestration.get_llm_logged", _factory)
    return provider


def _accumulated_draft_state(school, author) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Drive a full trio pass and return its final accumulated draft_state."""
    from planner.orchestration import run_trio_pass

    _, final_version, final_state, degraded = run_trio_pass(
        school, author, uuid.uuid4(), seed="raise reading", entry_mode="trio"
    )
    assert degraded == [], degraded
    assert final_version == 14
    return final_state


def _patch_gate_closure_llm(monkeypatch, canned_for: dict[str, str]) -> _GateClosureProvider:
    """Patch the orchestration LLM to a spec-keyed dispatcher for the loop's
    re-invokes. `canned_for` maps spec_key → the content to return when that spec
    is re-invoked."""
    provider = _GateClosureProvider(_template_literal_prefixes(), canned_for)

    def _factory(user=None, provider_name=None):
        return provider

    monkeypatch.setattr("planner.orchestration.get_llm_logged", _factory)
    return provider


# --- Criterion 1: a spec-closable failing draft is brought to a passing gate. -


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_gate_closure_reinvokes_spec_to_pass_gate(
    trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm, monkeypatch
):
    """A draft_state missing every success criterion (a B1-closable predicate)
    fails the gate; the loop re-invokes `draft-success-criteria`, folds a fresh
    criterion + re-runs B2's binding, and the saved Plan PASSES the gate with the
    re-invoked spec's rows present."""
    from plans.services import _check_promotion_gates
    from planner.orchestration import run_gate_closure_loop

    school = trio_school["school"]
    draft_state = dict(_accumulated_draft_state(school, author))

    # Perturb: drop the success-criterion row + its nested measurement binding so
    # the gate's "At least one success criterion is required." predicate fails.
    for key in list(draft_state):
        if key.startswith("criteria-"):
            draft_state.pop(key)

    # Re-invoking B1 alone restores a criterion, but the gate ALSO requires that
    # criterion be measurement-bound to a feedback channel — a B2-closable
    # predicate. So the canned re-invoke map carries BOTH B1 and B2 (and the loop
    # must re-invoke B2 after B1 re-creates the criterion row B2 indexes).
    canned = _canned_outputs()
    provider = _patch_gate_closure_llm(
        monkeypatch,
        {
            "draft-success-criteria": canned["draft-success-criteria"],
            "bind-measurement-channels": canned["bind-measurement-channels"],
        },
    )

    plan, final_state, residual = run_gate_closure_loop(
        draft_state, school=school, author=author, seed="raise reading"
    )

    assert plan is not None, residual
    assert residual == []
    # The saved Plan PASSES the gate (raises nothing).
    _check_promotion_gates(plan)
    # The re-invoked spec's row is present in the saved graph.
    assert SuccessCriterion.objects.filter(plan=plan).count() == 1
    # Lifecycle untouched (criterion 4): the loop never promotes.
    assert plan.lifecycle == Lifecycle.PROPOSED
    # B1 (and B2 to bind it) were the re-invoked specs.
    assert "draft-success-criteria" in provider.calls
    assert "bind-measurement-channels" in provider.calls


# --- Criterion 2 + 4 (cap-exhaustion): bounded, returns residual, no orphans. -


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_gate_closure_cap_exhaustion_returns_residual_bounded(
    trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm, monkeypatch
):
    """A spec-closable predicate whose re-invoked spec keeps failing to satisfy it
    → the loop terminates at the cap, returns `(None, draft_state, residual)` with
    the still-unmet message, never raises or loops unbounded, persists no Plan."""
    from planner.orchestration import DEFAULT_GATE_CLOSURE_MAX_ROUNDS, run_gate_closure_loop

    school = trio_school["school"]
    draft_state = dict(_accumulated_draft_state(school, author))

    # Perturb: drop the action steps (an F1-closable predicate: "At least one
    # action step is required.").
    for key in list(draft_state):
        if key.startswith("steps-"):
            draft_state.pop(key)

    # The re-invoke of F1 returns a non-satisfying output (no steps), so the
    # predicate NEVER closes — the loop must hit the cap.
    import json as _json

    provider = _patch_gate_closure_llm(
        monkeypatch, {"decompose-action-steps": _json.dumps({"method": "", "steps": []})}
    )

    before = Plan.objects.count()
    plan, final_state, residual = run_gate_closure_loop(
        draft_state, school=school, author=author, seed="raise reading"
    )

    assert plan is None
    assert residual  # the still-unmet message(s)
    assert any("action step" in m.lower() for m in residual)
    # Bounded: F1 was re-invoked at most once per round, never exceeding the cap.
    assert provider.calls.count("decompose-action-steps") <= DEFAULT_GATE_CLOSURE_MAX_ROUNDS
    # No orphan Plan rows: every probe round rolled back.
    assert Plan.objects.count() == before


# --- Criterion 3: a known-residual predicate is reported, not retried. --------


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_gate_closure_residual_predicate_not_retried(
    trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm, monkeypatch
):
    """An unmet predicate no trio spec can close (no active cycle) is reported as
    residual immediately — no spec is re-invoked for it, no Plan is saved."""
    from planner.orchestration import run_gate_closure_loop

    school = trio_school["school"]
    draft_state = _accumulated_draft_state(school, author)

    # Remove the school's only cycle: `save_draft_state_as_plan` cannot derive a
    # cycle, so the probe-save itself fails the precondition — a residual the loop
    # surfaces without re-invoking any spec.
    Cycle.objects.filter(school=school).delete()

    provider = _patch_gate_closure_llm(monkeypatch, {})

    before = Plan.objects.count()
    plan, final_state, residual = run_gate_closure_loop(
        draft_state, school=school, author=author, seed="raise reading"
    )

    assert plan is None
    assert residual
    assert any("cycle" in m.lower() for m in residual)
    # No spec was re-invoked for the residual (no LLM calls at all).
    assert provider.calls == []
    # No Plan persisted.
    assert Plan.objects.count() == before


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_gate_closure_clean_rule_residual_not_misreported(
    trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm, monkeypatch
):
    """A Phase-14 `plan.clean()` predicate (priority_rationale-on-deviation) is a
    known residual: it is reported, never retried, and a SPEC-closable predicate
    failing in the same round is NOT misreported as residual (it is re-invoked)."""
    from planner.orchestration import run_gate_closure_loop

    school = trio_school["school"]
    draft_state = dict(_accumulated_draft_state(school, author))

    # Trigger the Plan.clean() deviation rule: set a priority_tier marked as a
    # deviation from the framing focus with NO priority_rationale. (The exact
    # field combination is asserted to raise from clean() below; if the canned
    # accumulation cannot trigger it, the test self-checks and the assertion
    # guards against a silent no-op.)
    draft_state["framing_focus"] = "balanced"
    draft_state["priority_tier"] = "high"
    draft_state["priority_rationale"] = ""

    # Also drop the criteria so a SPEC-closable predicate co-occurs with the
    # residual clean() one in the same round.
    for key in list(draft_state):
        if key.startswith("criteria-"):
            draft_state.pop(key)

    canned = _canned_outputs()
    provider = _patch_gate_closure_llm(
        monkeypatch,
        {
            "draft-success-criteria": canned["draft-success-criteria"],
            "bind-measurement-channels": canned["bind-measurement-channels"],
        },
    )

    plan, final_state, residual = run_gate_closure_loop(
        draft_state, school=school, author=author, seed="raise reading"
    )

    # The clean()-rule residual is reported; no spec maps to it (so the loop
    # cannot close it and returns None with the residual).
    assert plan is None
    assert residual
    # The spec-closable criteria predicate WAS re-invoked (not misreported as
    # residual): B1 appears in the call log.
    assert "draft-success-criteria" in provider.calls
    # The residual is the clean()-rule message, not the (re-invoked) criteria one.
    assert not any("success criterion is required" in m.lower() for m in residual)


# --- Criterion 5 (no Flag rows) is asserted across the success + failure runs. -


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_gate_closure_writes_no_flag_rows(trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm, monkeypatch):
    """Neither the converging (saved) run nor a cap-exhausted run writes any Flag
    row — Flag persistence is slice #9, out of this slice's scope."""
    from planner.orchestration import run_gate_closure_loop

    school = trio_school["school"]

    # Converging run.
    draft_state = dict(_accumulated_draft_state(school, author))
    for key in list(draft_state):
        if key.startswith("criteria-"):
            draft_state.pop(key)
    canned = _canned_outputs()
    _patch_gate_closure_llm(
        monkeypatch,
        {
            "draft-success-criteria": canned["draft-success-criteria"],
            "bind-measurement-channels": canned["bind-measurement-channels"],
        },
    )
    before = Flag.objects.count()
    plan, _, residual = run_gate_closure_loop(
        draft_state, school=school, author=author, seed="raise reading"
    )
    assert plan is not None, residual
    assert Flag.objects.count() == before

from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError

# --- The promotion-gate-closure loop (US-LLM-25 / FEAT-010 slice 8b). ---------
#
# `save_draft_state_as_plan` persists a trio `draft_state` as a `Plan(lifecycle=
# PROPOSED)`, but a saved trio draft is NOT guaranteed to pass the promotion gate
# (`plans.services._check_promotion_gates`). This loop iterates the draft_state to
# a state that PASSES the gate by re-invoking the spec(s) that PRODUCE each unmet
# spec-closable predicate and re-folding their fresh output, until the gate holds
# or a bounded max-rounds cap is hit.
#
# It PROBES per round by saving inside a `transaction.atomic()` savepoint, running
# the gate against the saved (in-transaction) Plan, then ROLLING BACK — so a probe
# round leaves NO Plan. When a probe shows the gate would pass, it does ONE real
# committed save and returns it. The loop never flips `plan.lifecycle` and never
# writes a `Flag` row (slice #9).
#
# Continue/stop is the DETERMINISTIC gate (a ValidationError-or-not read), so the
# bounded autonomous loop is sound (DEC-58): it is bounded by the hard max-rounds
# cap, and on cap-exhaustion (or a round in which every remaining unmet predicate
# is RESIDUAL — no trio spec closes it) it returns the residual messages WITHOUT
# raising or looping unbounded.

# Hard max-rounds cap for the gate-closure loop (mirrors the orchestration
# retry-cap style: a small module-level constant a caller/test can reason about).
DEFAULT_GATE_CLOSURE_MAX_ROUNDS = 4


# THE GATE-FAILURE → SPEC MAPPING. `_check_promotion_gates` raises a list of
# message strings; each is matched (by a distinguishing substring, since some
# carry a variable row id) to the trio spec(s) that PRODUCE its predicate. A
# message matching NO rule is RESIDUAL — no trio spec closes it (`cycle`, the
# Phase-14 `plan.clean()` rule, an unresolved must-fix flag) — and is reported,
# not retried. The mapping is derived from TRIO_SEQUENCE + the predicate source
# (`plans/services.py:_check_promotion_gates`), verified against the per-spec
# contracts in `planner/specs.py`.
#
# Each rule is `(substring, (spec_key, ...))`. The spec tuple lists EVERY spec a
# round must re-invoke to close the predicate, in no particular order (the round
# re-invokes the union in TRIO_SEQUENCE order, honoring dependencies):
#   - the criterion-measurement-binding predicate needs the feedback channels
#     (C1 `suggest-feedback-channels`) AND the binding pass (B2
#     `bind-measurement-channels`), folded after B1 re-creates the criterion;
#   - every other predicate maps to its single producing spec.
_GATE_FAILURE_SPEC_RULES: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[str, ...]], ...] = (
    # Plan scalars (title/current_state/desired_state/rationale/
    # student_impact_framing) — all from the A1 narrative draft.
    ("Plan field title", ("narrative-draft",)),
    ("Plan field current_state", ("narrative-draft",)),
    ("Plan field desired_state", ("narrative-draft",)),
    ("Plan field rationale", ("narrative-draft",)),
    ("Plan field student_impact_framing", ("narrative-draft",)),
    # Domain alignment (A3).
    ("learner outcome must be targeted", ("propose-domain-alignment",)),
    ("stakeholder-impact group", ("propose-domain-alignment",)),
    ("area for improvement", ("propose-domain-alignment",)),
    # Accreditation standard (A2).
    ("accreditation standard", ("propose-accreditation-standards",)),
    # Success criterion existence (B1) + its measurement-channel binding (needs
    # C1 feedback channels + the B2 binding pass, after B1 re-creates the row).
    ("success criterion is required", ("draft-success-criteria",)),
    (
        "needs a measurement bound to a feedback channel",
        ("suggest-feedback-channels", "bind-measurement-channels"),
    ),
    # Action steps (F1) + their required sub-structure (F2–F5).
    ("action step is required", ("decompose-action-steps",)),
    ("needs a responsible assignment", ("propose-assignments",)),
    ("needs a timeline", ("propose-timelines",)),
    ("needs a required resource", ("propose-step-resources",)),
    ("needs at least one evidence artifact", ("propose-evidence",)),
    # Per-milestone planning method (D1).
    ("needs a planning method", ("propose-milestones",)),
    # Review loop (G1 produces all four review-loop predicates).
    ("communication is required", ("propose-review-loop",)),
    ("review event is required", ("propose-review-loop",)),
    ("needs at least one input", ("propose-review-loop",)),
    ("revision rule is required", ("propose-review-loop",)),
)

# spec_key → its TRIO_SEQUENCE position, so a round re-invokes its mapped specs
# in dependency order (e.g. C1 before B2).
_SPEC_ORDER_INDEX: dict[str, int] = {key: i for i, (key, _route) in enumerate(TRIO_SEQUENCE)}
_ROUTE_BY_SPEC: dict[str, FoldRouter] = {key: route for key, route in TRIO_SEQUENCE}


def _map_failures_to_specs(failures: list[str]) -> tuple[set[str], list[str]]:
    """Partition the gate's failure messages into (spec_keys-to-re-invoke,
    residual-messages).

    A message matching a `_GATE_FAILURE_SPEC_RULES` substring contributes its
    rule's spec_key(s) to the re-invoke set; a message matching NO rule is
    residual (no trio spec closes it). The first matching rule wins (the rules
    are distinct substrings, so at most one matches a given message)."""
    spec_keys: set[str] = set()
    residual: list[str] = []
    for message in failures:
        matched: tuple[str, ...] | None = None
        for substring, keys in _GATE_FAILURE_SPEC_RULES:
            if substring in message:
                matched = keys
                break
        if matched is None:
            residual.append(message)
        else:
            spec_keys.update(matched)
    return spec_keys, residual


def _probe_gate(
    draft_state: dict[str, Any], *, school: Any, author: Any
) -> tuple[Plan | None, list[str]]:
    """Probe-save `draft_state` and gate it, ROLLING BACK so nothing persists.

    Saves the draft as a Plan inside a `transaction.atomic()` savepoint, runs
    `_check_promotion_gates` against the saved (in-transaction) Plan, then forces
    a rollback (via a sentinel raised out of the atomic block) so the probe leaves
    no Plan. Returns `(None, [])` when the gate would PASS (the caller then does
    the single real committed save) or `(None, failures)` with the gate's failure
    messages when it would fail. A precondition failure of the save itself (e.g.
    no active cycle, or an invalid draft_state) surfaces as a `ValidationError`
    whose messages are returned as failures (a residual the mapping cannot close).

    NB the returned Plan is always `None`: the probe's Plan lives only inside the
    rolled-back savepoint and must not escape. The real Plan is saved separately
    by the caller once a probe shows the gate passes."""
    from plans.services import _check_promotion_gates

    class _ProbeRollback(Exception):
        """Sentinel: unwinds the probe savepoint so nothing persists."""

        def __init__(self, failures: list[str]) -> None:
            super().__init__("probe rollback")
            self.failures = failures

    try:
        with transaction.atomic():
            plan = save_draft_state_as_plan(draft_state, school=school, author=author)
            try:
                _check_promotion_gates(plan)
            except ValidationError as gate_exc:
                raise _ProbeRollback(list(gate_exc.messages)) from gate_exc
            # Gate passes: still roll back the probe (the real save is the
            # caller's single committed write), signalling success via empty list.
            raise _ProbeRollback([])
    except _ProbeRollback as rollback:
        return None, rollback.failures
    except ValidationError as save_exc:
        # The save itself could not proceed (precondition/validation) — its
        # messages are residual (the mapping closes none of them).
        return None, list(save_exc.messages)


def run_gate_closure_loop(
    draft_state: dict[str, Any],
    *,
    school: Any,
    author: Any,
    seed: str = "",
    max_rounds: int = DEFAULT_GATE_CLOSURE_MAX_ROUNDS,
) -> tuple[Plan | None, dict[str, Any], list[str]]:
    """Iterate `draft_state` until it saves to a gate-PASSING `Plan`, or stop.

    Per round: PROBE-save the draft_state + gate it + roll back (`_probe_gate`).
    If the gate passes, do ONE real committed save and return `(plan, draft_state,
    [])`. Otherwise map the unmet predicates to their producing spec(s)
    (`_map_failures_to_specs`); re-invoke each mapped spec (in TRIO_SEQUENCE order,
    so dependencies like C1-before-B2 hold) via `_run_one_spec` + its fold router,
    folding the fresh output into `draft_state`; then probe again next round.

    Terminates deterministically (DEC-58): bounded by `max_rounds`. When a round
    maps NO spec for the remaining unmet predicates (every one residual), or the
    cap is reached with the gate still failing, returns `(None, draft_state,
    residual_messages)` — without raising or looping unbounded.

    Never flips `plan.lifecycle` and never writes a `Flag` row (slice #9). The
    probe rounds persist nothing (each rolls back); the only committed write is the
    single final save on convergence."""
    state = dict(draft_state)
    last_failures: list[str] = []
    for _round in range(max_rounds):
        _probe_plan, failures = _probe_gate(state, school=school, author=author)
        if not failures:
            # The gate would pass: one real committed save.
            plan = save_draft_state_as_plan(state, school=school, author=author)
            return plan, state, []

        last_failures = failures
        spec_keys, residual = _map_failures_to_specs(failures)
        if not spec_keys:
            # Nothing is spec-closable this round — all remaining unmet predicates
            # are residual. Stop and report them.
            return None, state, residual

        # Re-invoke each mapped spec in dependency (TRIO_SEQUENCE) order, folding
        # its fresh prefill into the accumulating draft_state.
        for spec_key in sorted(spec_keys, key=lambda k: _SPEC_ORDER_INDEX[k]):
            spec = get_spec(spec_key)
            if spec is None:  # pragma: no cover — registry populated at app ready
                raise ValueError(f"run_gate_closure_loop: spec {spec_key!r} is not registered")
            prefill = _run_one_spec(spec, school, author, state, seed)
            state = _ROUTE_BY_SPEC[spec_key](state, prefill)

    # Cap reached with the gate still failing: report the residual (the last
    # round's still-unmet messages).
    return None, state, last_failures

@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_gate_closure_clean_rule_residual_not_retried(
    trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm, monkeypatch
):
    """The Phase-14 `plan.clean()` predicate (priority_rationale-on-deviation) is a
    known residual no trio spec produces: it is reported, never retried (no spec
    re-invoked), and no Plan is persisted.

    `Plan.clean()` fires via `PlanForm._post_clean` at SAVE-validation time, so a
    deviation-without-rationale draft fails the probe-save itself and surfaces the
    clean() message as a residual — the loop maps it to no spec and stops."""
    from planner.orchestration import run_gate_closure_loop

    from school.models import FramingVocabulary, PriorityTier

    school = trio_school["school"]
    draft_state = dict(_accumulated_draft_state(school, author))

    # Build the framing vocabulary the Plan.clean() deviation rule reads: two
    # priority tiers + a framing whose `default_priority_tier` is the FIRST. The
    # plan then selects the SECOND (a deviation) with NO priority_rationale, which
    # `Plan.clean()` rejects (a residual no trio spec produces).
    tier_default = PriorityTier.objects.create(
        school=school, code="strategic", label="Strategic", order=0
    )
    tier_other = PriorityTier.objects.create(
        school=school, code="operational", label="Operational", order=1
    )
    framing = FramingVocabulary.objects.create(
        school=school, code="balanced", label="Balanced", default_priority_tier=tier_default
    )
    draft_state["framing_focus"] = str(framing.pk)
    draft_state["priority_tier"] = str(tier_other.pk)
    draft_state["priority_rationale"] = ""

    provider = _patch_gate_closure_llm(monkeypatch, {})

    before = Plan.objects.count()
    plan, final_state, residual = run_gate_closure_loop(
        draft_state, school=school, author=author, seed="raise reading"
    )

    # The clean()-rule residual is reported; no spec maps to it.
    assert plan is None
    assert residual
    assert any("priority" in m.lower() for m in residual)
    # No spec was re-invoked for the residual (no LLM calls at all).
    assert provider.calls == []
    # No Plan persisted (the probe-save's failure rolled back / never committed).
    assert Plan.objects.count() == before

):
    """A Phase-14 `plan.clean()` predicate (priority_rationale-on-deviation) is a
    known residual: it is reported, never retried, and a SPEC-closable predicate
    failing in the same round is NOT misreported as residual (it is re-invoked)."""
    from planner.orchestration import run_gate_closure_loop

    from school.models import FramingVocabulary, PriorityTier

    draft_state = dict(_accumulated_draft_state(school, author))

    # priority tiers + a framing whose `default_priority_tier` is the FIRST. The
    # plan then selects the SECOND (a deviation) with NO priority_rationale, which
    # `Plan.clean()` rejects (a residual no trio spec produces).
    tier_default = PriorityTier.objects.create(
        school=school, code="strategic", label="Strategic", order=0
    )
    tier_other = PriorityTier.objects.create(
        school=school, code="operational", label="Operational", order=1
    )
    framing = FramingVocabulary.objects.create(
        school=school, code="balanced", label="Balanced", default_priority_tier=tier_default
    )
    draft_state["framing_focus"] = str(framing.pk)
    draft_state["priority_tier"] = str(tier_other.pk)
    draft_state["priority_rationale"] = ""

    for key in list(draft_state):

        },
    )

- `run_gate_closure_loop(draft_state, *, school, author, seed="", max_rounds=DEFAULT_GATE_CLOSURE_MAX_ROUNDS) -> (Plan | None, dict, list[str])`.
  Per round it PROBES (`_probe_gate`): saves the draft inside a
  `transaction.atomic()` savepoint, runs `_check_promotion_gates` against the
  in-transaction Plan, then rolls back via a sentinel so the probe persists
  nothing. On a passing probe it does ONE real committed save and returns
  `(plan, state, [])`. On a failing probe it maps the unmet predicates to specs
  and re-invokes them (in TRIO_SEQUENCE/dependency order) via the existing
  `_run_one_spec` + the spec's TRIO_SEQUENCE fold router, folding into the
  accumulating draft_state, then probes again next round.
- `_map_failures_to_specs` partitions the gate's message list into
  (spec_keys-to-re-invoke, residual) via `_GATE_FAILURE_SPEC_RULES` — a
  substring→spec table derived from TRIO_SEQUENCE + the predicate source and the
  per-spec specs.py contracts. The criterion-measurement-binding predicate maps
  to BOTH suggest-feedback-channels (C1) and bind-measurement-channels (B2); the
  review-loop predicates all map to propose-review-loop (G1); every other
  predicate maps to its single producer. A message matching no rule is RESIDUAL
  (cycle, the Phase-14 plan.clean() rule, an unresolved must-fix flag) — reported,
  not retried.
- `DEFAULT_GATE_CLOSURE_MAX_ROUNDS = 4` (a small constant; DEC-58 bounded
  autonomous loop, since the continue/stop signal is the deterministic gate read,
  not an LLM judgement).

Termination is deterministic (criterion 2): the loop is bounded by the cap, and
when a round maps no spec for the remaining unmet predicates (all residual) or
the cap is reached with the gate still failing it returns
`(None, state, residual_messages)` without raising or looping unbounded. A
re-invoked spec that itself fails (LLM/parse/catalogue/resolve/fold error) folds
nothing — its predicate stays unmet — and the loop advances toward the cap rather
than aborting (logged at debug for forensic visibility).

Intentionally OUT OF SCOPE (slice #9): the loop never flips `plan.lifecycle`
(stays PROPOSED) and never writes a `Flag` row; it does not touch
`_check_promotion_gates`, `advance_plan_status`, the `Flag` model, or
`save_draft_state_as_plan`'s internals.

Two design requirements surfaced by the gate-first tests (RED-before-GREEN):
(1) the binding predicate is closed by re-invoking C1 + B2 after B1 re-creates
the criterion (not B2 alone); (2) a non-closing re-invoke must be guarded so it
does not abort the loop — observed via F1 raising on a non-satisfying canned
output. Also confirmed: the Phase-14 plan.clean() deviation rule fires at
PlanForm save-validation (via _post_clean), so it surfaces as a save-precondition
residual ahead of gating — reported, never retried.

planner/tests/test_slice_gate_closure.py asserts each success criterion with the
LLM mocked (trio_school conftest + a spec-keyed canned dispatcher that recovers
the spec from the rendered prompt's template literal prefix): a B1/B2-closable
draft brought to a gate-passing PROPOSED Plan; cap-exhaustion returning bounded
residual with no orphan Plan rows; the no-cycle and plan.clean() residuals
reported without re-invoke; and no Flag rows written. `make verify-slice
SLICE=gate_closure` exits 0 (ruff + mypy + slice tests + full pytest + test-js +
context-validate).
EOF

@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_probe_reinvoke_that_raises_no_crash(trio_school, author, fake_trio_llm, monkeypatch):
    """A re-invoked spec emitting unparseable garbage must fold nothing and the
    loop must advance to cap WITHOUT crashing."""
    from planner.orchestration import run_gate_closure_loop

    school = trio_school["school"]
    ds = dict(_accumulated_draft_state(school, author))
    for k in list(ds):
        if k.startswith("steps-"):
            ds.pop(k)
    _patch_gate_closure_llm(monkeypatch, {"decompose-action-steps": "<<<not json at all>>>"})
    before = Plan.objects.count()
    plan, st, res = run_gate_closure_loop(ds, school=school, author=author, seed="x")
    after = Plan.objects.count()
    print(f"PROBE raising-reinvoke: plan={plan!r} residual={res} delta={after - before}")
    assert plan is None
    assert after == before
    assert res

WHAT EXISTS (on main): `planner/orchestration.py` has `run_trio_pass(school, author, draft_id, seed, entry_mode) -> (draft_id, final_version, draft_state, degraded)` where `degraded` is a list of `DegradedSpec` (a spec that failed bounded retry; carries spec_key, attempts, reason, severity=a FlagSeverity MUST_FIX value); `save_draft_state_as_plan(draft_state, *, school, author) -> Plan` (creates a Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED)); and `run_gate_closure_loop(draft_state, *, school, author, seed, max_rounds) -> (Plan|None, draft_state, residual_failures)` (iterates to a gate-passing Plan, re-invoking specs). `plans/services.py:_check_promotion_gates` includes a predicate that blocks promotion when `plan.flags.filter(severity=MUST_FIX, is_resolved=False).exists()`. `advance_plan_status` (plans/services.py:210+) is the lifecycle transition.

ESTABLISH, with file:line:

1. **The Flag model.** Read `plans/models/flag.py` fully: every field (element_path, message, created_by_spec, verdict, severity, is_resolved, plan FK, created_at — confirm), the `FlagSeverity` enum values, any constraints/clean(). How is a Flag tied to a Plan + to a specific element? What does `element_path` mean (a draft_state key? a model path?). What's the minimal valid Flag row.

2. **The existing flag-persistence path.** `PlanCreateView._save_flag_requests(plan, data)` (planner/views.py ~316-377) already persists Flags in the browser create flow — read it. What Flags does it create, from what input (the author's request-catalogue-addition flags / DEC-44 verdicts)? Does `save_draft_state_as_plan` (which reuses the view's `_bind_validate_and_save`) ALREADY call `_save_flag_requests`? If so, what does it persist from a trio draft_state (which carries no author flag-requests), and does that interact with the degraded-spec Flags #9 must add. Is there a DEC-44 free-text-audit flag path (`audit_prefill_flags` / FreetextFlag in specs.py) that ALSO produces flags during the trio run — and are those persisted anywhere, or dropped?

3. **DegradedSpec -> Flag mapping.** How should each `DegradedSpec` (from `run_trio_pass`) become a Flag row (severity=MUST_FIX, created_by_spec=spec_key, message=the reason, element_path=?)? Read the `DegradedSpec` dataclass (orchestration.py) for its exact fields. What element_path is meaningful for a whole-spec degradation (e.g. `spec:<key>`)?

4. **THE KEY TIMING QUESTION — which degraded set?** `run_trio_pass` returns `degraded` from the INITIAL sweep. `run_gate_closure_loop` then RE-INVOKES specs — including, plausibly, specs that were degraded (to close their gate predicate). So a spec degraded in the initial sweep MIGHT succeed on re-invocation in the closure loop (resolving the degradation), OR a spec that succeeded initially might not be re-touched. Determine: does `run_gate_closure_loop` currently return or track which specs degraded/succeeded during ITS rounds? Is the closure loop's re-invocation list derived from gate failures (so a degraded-but-not-gate-relevant spec is never retried)? So the FINAL Flag set should reflect: degraded specs whose output is STILL absent after closure. How can #9 compute the final-accurate degraded set rather than blindly persisting the initial `degraded` list (which would wrongly flag a spec the closure loop fixed)? Trace what information is available at the end of the closure loop to determine this. This is the central design question — get the data-flow exactly.

5. **advance_plan_status — what "admin-promote wiring" needs.** Read `advance_plan_status` (services.py:210+): its signature, the transition admissibility matrix, that it re-runs `_check_promotion_gates` on the transition into ACTIVE, that it writes a `PlanStatusTransition` audit row, that it (the AI never calls it — admin only). Then: is there ALREADY an admin action / view / endpoint that calls `advance_plan_status` (grep admin.py, views.py, urls.py for advance_plan_status / promote / lifecycle transitions)? If yes, the trio output flows into the existing promote UI with no new wiring. If no, what is the minimal "wiring" — a Django admin action on Plan? a planner view? Determine whether #9's "admin-promote wiring" is (a) already covered by an existing promote path the trio Plan automatically participates in, or (b) genuinely needs a new admin/UI affordance.

6. **The composition + the #9/entry-point boundary.** Is there a single place that should compose `run_trio_pass -> run_gate_closure_loop -> persist degraded Flags` into one promotable result, or do these stay separate functions a caller chains? Note: there is currently NO caller (no view/command/admin) of run_trio_pass at all (the trio has no user-facing trigger). Determine cleanly: what is IN scope for #9 (Flag persistence + ensuring the admin-promote path works on the trio Plan + possibly the composed orchestration fn) vs what belongs to a separate "user entry point" slice (a view/command/button that a user clicks). Recommend the cut.

7. **Test surface.** With the LLM mocked: a trio run with a degraded spec -> save -> the degraded spec is persisted as a MUST_FIX Flag -> `_check_promotion_gates` now FAILS on the unresolved-flag predicate (so a degraded section blocks promotion) -> resolving the flag (is_resolved=True) lets the gate pass -> `advance_plan_status(plan, ACTIVE, admin)` promotes. Note the `trio_school` conftest fixture + the canned-dispatch pattern. Also: a clean trio run (no degraded specs) -> no MUST_FIX flags -> promotable.

DELIVER: the Flag model + FlagSeverity + the existing `_save_flag_requests`/free-text-audit flag paths (and whether save_draft_state_as_plan already triggers them); the DegradedSpec->Flag mapping; a definitive answer on the final-degraded-set timing (how to avoid flagging a spec the closure loop fixed); whether admin-promote needs new wiring or rides an existing path; a clear #9-vs-entry-point scope cut; and the test surface. Cite file:line. Write no code.

# Slice #9 — trio final-save + degraded-spec Flag persistence + promote verification

## What this is
The production trio's pieces exist on main: `run_trio_pass(school, author, draft_id, seed, entry_mode) -> (draft_id, final_version, draft_state, degraded)` (degraded = list of `DegradedSpec`), `save_draft_state_as_plan(draft_state, *, school, author) -> Plan` (creates Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED)), and `run_gate_closure_loop(draft_state, *, school, author, seed, max_rounds) -> (Plan | None, draft_state, residual_failures)` (iterates to a gate-passing Plan, persisting it on convergence; returns None + draft_state + residual on cap-exhaustion). THIS slice composes them into one production orchestration entry and persists the still-degraded specs as MUST_FIX `Flag` rows, so a degraded section blocks promotion through the EXISTING admin gate.

OUT OF SCOPE (do not build / do not modify): a user-facing trigger to START a trio run (no view/button/management command — that is a separate entry-point slice); and any change to `advance_plan_status`, `_check_promotion_gates`, `run_gate_closure_loop`, or `save_draft_state_as_plan` internals.

## Success criteria (the contract — your tests must verify EACH; loop until all hold)
1. After a trio run, each spec that REMAINS degraded — its section unproduced in the final post-closure draft_state — is persisted as exactly ONE MUST_FIX `Flag` on the saved Plan (carrying the failing spec key and its reason); a spec whose section is present — whether produced in the initial sweep OR recovered by a closure-loop re-invocation — produces NO flag.
2. A clean trio run (no spec remains degraded) persists ZERO must-fix flags on the saved Plan.
3. The composition ALWAYS yields a saved Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED): when the closure loop converges, that gate-passing Plan; when it cannot, the best-effort draft is STILL saved as a proposed Plan carrying its must-fix flags, and the residual gate failures are returned to the caller — the run never dead-ends without an editable draft and never aborts.
4. An unresolved must-fix flag on the saved Plan BLOCKS promotion through the existing admin path (`advance_plan_status(plan, ACTIVE, user)` raises on the gate); resolving that flag (`is_resolved=True`) on an otherwise gate-passing Plan lets promotion proceed; nothing in the trio path flips `lifecycle` itself.
5. Re-running the composition does not double-persist flags for the same degradation; the whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates.

## Established mechanism facts (verified against current source — build on these; if any is false, STOP and report)
- `plans/models/flag.py:Flag` fields: `plan` (FK CASCADE, related_name="flags"), `element_path` (CharField 200), `message` (TextField), `created_by_spec` (CharField 100), `verdict` (nullable CharField 32), `severity` (CharField 16, choices `FlagSeverity`), `is_resolved` (Bool, default False), `created_at` (auto_now_add). `FlagSeverity.MUST_FIX == "must-fix"` (`plans/choices.py`).
- `DegradedSpec` (`planner/orchestration.py`): `spec_key`, `attempts`, `reason` (stringified terminal exception), `severity` (a `FlagSeverity` value == MUST_FIX). Its docstring states its severity is "the FlagSeverity VALUE a future slice (#9) would persist as a Flag row."
- Map each still-degraded spec to a Flag: `element_path=f"spec:{spec_key}"`, `message=reason`, `created_by_spec=spec_key`, `verdict=None`, `severity=FlagSeverity.MUST_FIX`, `is_resolved=False`.
- THE TIMING POINT (get this right): do NOT blindly persist the initial `degraded` list — `run_gate_closure_loop` may RE-INVOKE a degraded spec and produce its section (resolving the degradation). Persist a flag ONLY for a degraded spec whose section is still ABSENT from the FINAL post-closure draft_state. Derive "section present" from the spec's known draft_state footprint (the prefix/keys its TRIO_SEQUENCE fold router writes — A1 writes bare narrative keys; formset specs write `{prefix}-{i}-...`; per-step-child specs write `steps-{i}-{seg}-...`; B2 writes the measurement-channel keys; A3/G1 their section keys). Use the existing TRIO_SEQUENCE routing knowledge; do not hand-maintain a brittle parallel signature list if the footprint is derivable.
- ADMIN-PROMOTE ALREADY EXISTS — DO NOT rebuild it: `plans/admin/actions.py:advance_plan_status_action` (registered on `PlanAdmin`, `plans/admin/plan.py:215`) drives `plans/services.py:advance_plan_status(plan, target_status, by_user)`, which on the transition into `Lifecycle.ACTIVE` re-runs `_check_promotion_gates`, and that gate ALREADY blocks on `plan.flags.filter(severity=FlagSeverity.MUST_FIX, is_resolved=False).exists()` (services.py:203-204). The AI never calls it. Your tests VERIFY this path on the trio Plan; you add no promote code.
- `run_gate_closure_loop` persists the Plan on convergence (committed) and persists nothing on cap-exhaustion (probes roll back). So on the None path your composition must call `save_draft_state_as_plan(final_draft_state, ...)` to give the admin the best-effort proposed Plan. `save_draft_state_as_plan` saves a Plan(proposed) on FORM-level validity (it does not require the promotion gate to pass), so a not-yet-promotable draft still saves.

## How to build it
- Add a composition function to `planner/orchestration.py`, e.g. `run_trio_to_proposed_plan(school, author, draft_id, seed="", max_rounds=...) -> (Plan, list[str])` (Plan = the saved proposed Plan, list = residual gate-failure messages, empty when gate-passing): run_trio_pass → run_gate_closure_loop(its draft_state); take the loop's Plan when non-None, else save the final draft_state as a proposed Plan; then persist the still-degraded specs as MUST_FIX Flags on that Plan (consistently — the saved Plan reflects its flags). Return the Plan + residual.
- Persist flags so they are consistent with the saved Plan (same transaction or immediately after the save, so a saved Plan always carries its degraded-spec flags). One flag per still-degraded spec (no duplicates within a run).
- Do NOT modify the gate, advance_plan_status, run_gate_closure_loop, or save_draft_state_as_plan. Reuse them.

## Gate-first verification (mandatory order)
1. FIRST author `planner/tests/test_slice_trio_finalize.py` (harness selects via `pytest -k slice_trio_finalize`; file + test names contain `slice_trio_finalize`). With the LLM mocked (the `trio_school` conftest fixture + the canned/scripted-provider pattern in `test_slice_resilience.py`/`test_slice_trio_pass.py`), assert EACH criterion: (a) a clean run → Plan(PROPOSED), zero must-fix flags, promotable via `advance_plan_status(plan, ACTIVE, user)`; (b) a spec degraded in the initial sweep AND not recovered → one MUST_FIX Flag (`spec:<key>`, created_by_spec, reason), and `advance_plan_status` into ACTIVE RAISES on the unresolved-flag gate, then setting `is_resolved=True` (on an otherwise gate-passing Plan) lets it promote; (c) a spec degraded initially but RECOVERED by the closure loop → NO flag (verify the recovered section is present + promotable); (d) the cap-exhausted / not-gate-passing path → a Plan(PROPOSED) is STILL saved carrying its flags, residual returned, no abort; (e) re-running does not double-flag a degradation. Prefer driving a real (mocked) run_trio_pass so the flags reflect genuine degradation.
2. Observe the new tests RED before implementing (right reason).
3. Implement to GREEN.
4. Run `make verify-slice SLICE=trio_finalize` — must exit 0 (BLOCKING: your slice tests + ruff + mypy + full pytest + make test-js + context-validate). Do not weaken the gate; do not commit on a red/unobserved gate.

## Commit + report
- Commit on `trio-finalize` with a forensic message (aims/intentions, not unwarranted certainty; no author-credit lines). `git commit -F` from a SYSTEM `/tmp` file if needed. Working tree clean after. NOTE: three `.context/*` files may be uncommitted orchestrator bookkeeping (TASK-071) — NOT yours; leave them untouched.

input.content:
"""TASK (FEAT-010 slice 9) — the trio's FINAL-SAVE + degraded-spec Flag persistence.

Covers `planner.orchestration.run_trio_to_proposed_plan`: the production
composition that runs the full trio pass, drives the gate-closure loop on its
accumulated draft_state, ALWAYS lands a saved `Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED)`, and
persists the specs that REMAIN degraded — their section still absent from the
final post-closure draft_state — as MUST_FIX `Flag` rows on that Plan.

The contract (the slice's acceptance criteria):

1. Each spec whose section is STILL absent from the final post-closure
   draft_state is persisted as exactly ONE MUST_FIX `Flag` (carrying the spec key
   + its reason); a spec whose section is present — produced in the initial sweep
   OR recovered by the closure loop — produces NO flag.
2. A clean run (nothing remains degraded) persists ZERO must-fix flags.
3. The composition ALWAYS yields a saved Plan(PROPOSED): the gate-passing Plan on
   convergence, else the best-effort draft saved as a proposed Plan carrying its
   flags, with the residual gate failures returned — never a dead-end.
4. An unresolved must-fix flag BLOCKS promotion through the EXISTING admin path
   (`advance_plan_status(plan, ACTIVE, user)` raises); resolving it
   (`is_resolved=True`) on an otherwise gate-passing Plan lets promotion proceed;
   the trio path never flips `lifecycle`.
5. Re-running the composition does not double-persist flags for the same
   degradation.

Builds on the SAME enriched `trio_school` + canned-output dispatch pattern as
`test_slice_trio_pass.py` / `test_slice_gate_closure.py`; degradation is induced
by scripting a spec's LLM call to keep failing (malformed JSON) so the spec
genuinely degrades, exercising the real composition.

Named `slice_trio_finalize` so `make verify-slice SLICE=trio_finalize`
(→ `pytest -k slice_trio_finalize`) selects this file.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from typing import Any

import pytest

from ai.services.llm.base import LLMResponse
from plans.choices import FlagSeverity, Lifecycle
from plans.models import Flag, Plan

from .test_slice_gate_closure import _GateClosureProvider, _template_literal_prefixes
from .test_slice_trio_pass import TRIO_ORDER, _canned_outputs

# A malformed (non-JSON) body every spec's strict-JSON parser rejects with a
# `ValueError` — the parse-failure injection that degrades a spec.
_MALFORMED = "this is not json at all {{{"


class _FinalizeProvider:
    """A fake LLM provider that dispatches a canned output BY recovered spec_key,
    OPTIONALLY forcing named specs to FAIL (return malformed JSON every call).

    The composition runs the initial fixed-order trio pass AND then the
    data-dependent gate-closure re-invokes, so the provider must dispatch by the
    spec recovered from the rendered prompt (the `_GateClosureProvider` literal-
    prefix recovery), not by call order. `fail_specs` names the spec_keys whose
    every call returns malformed JSON (so they exhaust retries and degrade, and
    a closure re-invoke of them also fails → they stay degraded). Records each
    recovered spec_key per call for assertions."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        prefixes: dict[str, str],
        canned_for: dict[str, str],
        fail_specs: frozenset[str],
    ) -> None:
        self._delegate = _GateClosureProvider(prefixes, canned_for)
        self._fail_specs = fail_specs

    @property
    def calls(self) -> list[str]:
        return self._delegate.calls

    def complete(self, messages: list[dict], **kwargs: Any) -> LLMResponse:
        prompt = messages[-1]["content"]
        spec_key = self._delegate._recover_spec_key(prompt)
        self._delegate.calls.append(spec_key)
        if spec_key in self._fail_specs:
            content = _MALFORMED
        else:
            content = self._delegate._canned_for[spec_key]
        return LLMResponse(
            content=content,
            model="fake-model",
            provider="anthropic",
            usage={"input_tokens": 4, "output_tokens": 6},
            raw={"raw": True},
        )


def _patch_finalize_llm(
    monkeypatch, fail_specs: frozenset[str] = frozenset()
) -> _FinalizeProvider:
    """Patch `planner.orchestration.get_llm_logged` to the spec-keyed dispatcher
    (used for BOTH the initial pass and the closure re-invokes), with `fail_specs`
    scripted to always return malformed JSON (degrading those specs)."""
    canned = _canned_outputs()
    provider = _FinalizeProvider(_template_literal_prefixes(), canned, fail_specs)

    def _factory(user=None, provider_name=None):
        return provider

    monkeypatch.setattr("planner.orchestration.get_llm_logged", _factory)
    return provider


# --- Criterion 2 + 3 + 4 (clean run): saved PROPOSED Plan, zero flags, promotable. -


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_trio_finalize_clean_run_saves_proposed_plan_no_flags_promotable(
    trio_school, author, monkeypatch
):
    """A clean trio run (no spec degrades) → a saved Plan(PROPOSED) with ZERO
    must-fix flags, and the EXISTING admin path promotes it into ACTIVE without
    raising (the gate finds no unresolved must-fix flag)."""
    from planner.orchestration import run_trio_to_proposed_plan
    from plans.services import advance_plan_status

    school = trio_school["school"]
    plan, residual = run_trio_to_proposed_plan(school, author, uuid.uuid4(), seed="raise reading")

    # Criterion 3: a Plan(PROPOSED) was saved.
    assert isinstance(plan, Plan)
    assert plan.lifecycle == Lifecycle.PROPOSED
    # Criterion 2: zero must-fix flags on a clean run.
    assert plan.flags.filter(severity=FlagSeverity.MUST_FIX).count() == 0
    # A clean run converged: no residual gate failures.
    assert residual == []
    # Criterion 4: the EXISTING admin promote path advances PROPOSED → ACTIVE
    # without raising (no unresolved must-fix flag blocks it). No promote code is
    # added by this slice — this exercises plans.services.advance_plan_status.
    advance_plan_status(plan, Lifecycle.ACTIVE, author)
    plan.refresh_from_db()
    assert plan.lifecycle == Lifecycle.ACTIVE


# --- Criterion 1 + 3 + 4 (degraded-and-unrecovered): one flag, blocks promote. -


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_trio_finalize_unrecovered_degraded_spec_persists_one_flag_blocking_promote(
    trio_school, author, monkeypatch
):
    """A spec degraded in the initial sweep AND not recovered by the closure loop
    (its LLM always fails) → exactly ONE MUST_FIX Flag on the saved Plan carrying
    `spec:<key>` + the reason; the saved Plan is STILL PROPOSED with the residual
    returned; `advance_plan_status` into ACTIVE RAISES on the unresolved-flag gate;
    resolving the flag (on an otherwise gate-passing Plan) lets it promote.

    G1 (`propose-review-loop`) is the casualty: it produces the review-loop
    section (communications / review_events / revision_rules). Failing it leaves
    those gate predicates unmet — and they are the ONLY unmet predicates, so once
    the flag is resolved the gate otherwise passes (isolating criterion 4)."""
    from django.core.exceptions import ValidationError

    from planner.orchestration import run_trio_to_proposed_plan
    from plans.services import advance_plan_status

    school = trio_school["school"]
    provider = _patch_finalize_llm(monkeypatch, fail_specs=frozenset({"propose-review-loop"}))

    plan, residual = run_trio_to_proposed_plan(school, author, uuid.uuid4(), seed="raise reading")

    # Criterion 3: a Plan(PROPOSED) was STILL saved (best-effort), never a dead-end.
    assert isinstance(plan, Plan)
    assert plan.lifecycle == Lifecycle.PROPOSED
    # The closure loop could not close the G1 predicate (its re-invoke also fails),
    # so the residual carries the still-unmet review-loop gate message(s).
    assert residual
    assert any("review" in m.lower() or "communication" in m.lower() for m in residual)

    # Criterion 1: exactly ONE MUST_FIX flag, for the unrecovered spec.
    flags = list(plan.flags.filter(severity=FlagSeverity.MUST_FIX))
    assert len(flags) == 1
    flag = flags[0]
    assert flag.element_path == "spec:propose-review-loop"
    assert flag.created_by_spec == "propose-review-loop"
    assert flag.message  # the stringified degrade reason
    assert flag.is_resolved is False
    # The spec WAS re-invoked by the closure loop (it maps to a gate predicate),
    # proving the degradation is genuine, not a no-op.
    assert "propose-review-loop" in provider.calls

    # Criterion 4: the unresolved must-fix flag BLOCKS promotion via the EXISTING
    # admin path.
    with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
        advance_plan_status(plan, Lifecycle.ACTIVE, author)

    # Resolving the flag clears the only remaining gate blocker; promotion now
    # proceeds (the rest of the graph is gate-passing because only G1 failed).
    flag.is_resolved = True
    flag.save(update_fields=["is_resolved"])
    advance_plan_status(plan, Lifecycle.ACTIVE, author)
    plan.refresh_from_db()
    assert plan.lifecycle == Lifecycle.ACTIVE


# --- Criterion 1 (recovered): a spec degraded initially but recovered → NO flag. -


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_trio_finalize_closure_recovered_spec_produces_no_flag(
    trio_school, author, monkeypatch
):
    """A spec degraded in the INITIAL sweep but RECOVERED by the closure loop → NO
    flag (its section is present in the final post-closure draft_state). This is
    the TIMING point: the raw initial `degraded` list is NOT what gets persisted;
    only the specs whose section is STILL absent after the closure loop are.

    Engineered with a STATEFUL responder: `decompose-action-steps` (F1) fails its
    initial-sweep attempts (degrades, section absent), then SUCCEEDS once the
    closure loop re-invokes it (the gate's "action step is required" predicate
    maps to F1). Because F1's section is present in the final draft_state, no flag
    is persisted for it — and the run converges to a gate-passing Plan."""
    from planner.orchestration import run_trio_to_proposed_plan

    school = trio_school["school"]
    canned = _canned_outputs()
    prefixes = _template_literal_prefixes()

    # A stateful provider: F1 fails until the closure phase begins, then succeeds.
    # The initial fixed-order sweep visits each of the 14 specs ONCE; F1 is at a
    # known position. We flip F1 to succeed after the initial sweep's F1 attempts
    # are exhausted (the retry cap is per-spec, default 2 attempts), i.e. after we
    # have seen F1 fail in the initial pass. Simplest deterministic rule: F1 fails
    # for its FIRST `initial_f1_attempts` calls (the initial sweep's retries), then
    # succeeds on every subsequent (closure) call.
    from planner.orchestration import DEFAULT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS

    state = {"f1_calls": 0}

    delegate = _GateClosureProvider(prefixes, canned)

    class _StatefulProvider:
        @property
        def calls(self) -> list[str]:
            return delegate.calls

        def complete(self, messages: list[dict], **kwargs: Any) -> LLMResponse:
            prompt = messages[-1]["content"]
            spec_key = delegate._recover_spec_key(prompt)
            delegate.calls.append(spec_key)
            if spec_key == "decompose-action-steps":
                state["f1_calls"] += 1
                # Fail through the initial sweep's per-spec retry budget; succeed
                # thereafter (the closure re-invoke).
                if state["f1_calls"] <= DEFAULT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS:
                    content = _MALFORMED
                else:
                    content = canned[spec_key]
            else:
                content = canned[spec_key]
            return LLMResponse(
                content=content,
                model="fake-model",
                provider="anthropic",
                usage={"input_tokens": 4, "output_tokens": 6},
                raw={"raw": True},
            )

    provider = _StatefulProvider()

    def _factory(user=None, provider_name=None):
        return provider

    monkeypatch.setattr("planner.orchestration.get_llm_logged", _factory)

    plan, residual = run_trio_to_proposed_plan(school, author, uuid.uuid4(), seed="raise reading")

    # F1 failed in the initial sweep (so it WAS in the raw degraded list) ...
    assert state["f1_calls"] > DEFAULT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS, "F1 was never re-invoked by the closure loop"
    # ... but it was RECOVERED by the closure loop, so NO flag is persisted for it.
    assert plan is not None
    assert plan.flags.filter(created_by_spec="decompose-action-steps").count() == 0
    # The run converged to a gate-passing Plan (no residual, no must-fix flags).
    assert residual == []
    assert plan.flags.filter(severity=FlagSeverity.MUST_FIX).count() == 0
    assert plan.lifecycle == Lifecycle.PROPOSED


# --- Criterion 5: re-running the composition does not double-persist flags. ----


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_slice_trio_finalize_rerun_does_not_double_flag_a_degradation(
    trio_school, author, monkeypatch
):
    """Running the composition TWICE for the same unrecoverable degradation yields
    ONE must-fix flag PER saved Plan (a fresh Plan per run), never duplicates
    within a single run's Plan. Each run saves its OWN Plan; the per-run flag
    count for the degraded spec stays exactly one."""
    from planner.orchestration import run_trio_to_proposed_plan

    school = trio_school["school"]
    _patch_finalize_llm(monkeypatch, fail_specs=frozenset({"propose-review-loop"}))

    plan_a, _ = run_trio_to_proposed_plan(school, author, uuid.uuid4(), seed="raise reading")
    plan_b, _ = run_trio_to_proposed_plan(school, author, uuid.uuid4(), seed="raise reading")

    # Distinct Plans.
    assert plan_a.pk != plan_b.pk
    # Each Plan carries exactly ONE must-fix flag for the degraded spec — no
    # within-run duplication.
    assert plan_a.flags.filter(created_by_spec="propose-review-loop").count() == 1
    assert plan_b.flags.filter(created_by_spec="propose-review-loop").count() == 1
    # The second run did not retro-add flags to the first Plan.
    assert (
        Flag.objects.filter(plan=plan_a, created_by_spec="propose-review-loop").count() == 1
    )
