"""AUDIT PROBE (temporary; deleted after run) — dry-run rollback completeness.

The IMPL's dry-run test asserts only Plan.objects.count() is unchanged. This
probe asserts the FULL rollback: under --dry-run, no Plan AND no Flag AND no
PlanDraftState row survives; and on a REAL (non-dry) run the same inputs DO
persist a Plan + PlanDraftState rows (proving the rollback — not an
empty-run — is what discards them on dry-run). Also probes the no-active-cycle
error message clarity and that a non-superuser author resolves.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from io import StringIO

import pytest
from django.core.management import call_command

from plans.models import Flag, Plan, PlanDraftState

from .test_slice_trio_finalize import _patch_finalize_llm

_FIXTURE_SLUG = "planner-school"


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_probe_dry_run_rolls_back_plan_flag_and_draftstate(trio_school, author, monkeypatch):
    _patch_finalize_llm(monkeypatch)
    p0 = Plan.objects.count()
    f0 = Flag.objects.count()
    d0 = PlanDraftState.objects.count()

    call_command(
        "run_trio", "--school-slug", _FIXTURE_SLUG, "--author-email", author.email,
        "--seed", "raise reading", "--dry-run", stdout=StringIO(),
    )

    assert Plan.objects.count() == p0, "dry-run leaked a Plan"
    assert Flag.objects.count() == f0, "dry-run leaked a Flag"
    assert PlanDraftState.objects.count() == d0, "dry-run leaked a PlanDraftState"


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_probe_real_run_does_persist_draftstate(trio_school, author, monkeypatch):
    """Counterpart: a NON-dry run with the same inputs persists rows — proving the
    dry-run rollback is what discards them, not an empty run."""
    _patch_finalize_llm(monkeypatch)
    d0 = PlanDraftState.objects.count()

    call_command(
        "run_trio", "--school-slug", _FIXTURE_SLUG, "--author-email", author.email,
        "--seed", "raise reading", stdout=StringIO(),
    )

    assert Plan.objects.count() == 1
    assert PlanDraftState.objects.count() > d0, "real run wrote no PlanDraftState — dry-run test would be vacuous"


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_probe_no_active_cycle_message_is_clear(school_world, author, monkeypatch):
    from django.core.management.base import CommandError

    from school.models import Cycle

    _patch_finalize_llm(monkeypatch)
    Cycle.objects.filter(school=school_world["school"]).delete()

    with pytest.raises(CommandError) as ei:
        call_command(
            "run_trio", "--school-slug", _FIXTURE_SLUG, "--author-email", author.email,
            "--seed", "raise reading", stdout=StringIO(),
        )
    msg = str(ei.value)
    # Must be a human message, not an empty/Traceback string.
    assert msg.strip()
    assert "Traceback" not in msg
    print(f"NO-ACTIVE-CYCLE CommandError message: {msg!r}")


@pytest.mark.django_db
def test_probe_non_superuser_author_resolves(trio_school, monkeypatch, django_user_model):
    """An --author-email of a NON-superuser must resolve (not require superuser)."""
    _patch_finalize_llm(monkeypatch)
    u = django_user_model.objects.create_user(
        username="plainjane", email="plain@jane.test", password="x"
    )
    assert not u.is_superuser

    call_command(
        "run_trio", "--school-slug", _FIXTURE_SLUG, "--author-email", u.email,
        "--seed", "raise reading", stdout=StringIO(),
    )
    assert Plan.objects.filter(author=u).count() == 1

input.content:
# FGAP-050 — the DEC-44 fabrication validator must use the run's configured LLM

## Context

The first live `run_trio` run (chiway, openrouter) surfaced FGAP-050: the trio's spec calls resolve the LLM via `get_llm_logged(author)` (`orchestration.py:266`) → the user-scoped openrouter `LLMProviderConfig`, so they work. But the DEC-44 fabrication validator calls `get_llm()` with **no user** (`planner/_llm_fabrication_validator.py:125`), so it can't see that user-scoped config, falls through to the unconfigured `anthropic` system default, raises `NoProviderConfiguredError`, and — being "failure-falls-back-without-blocking" — logs a WARNING and returns `[]`. The anti-fabrication LLM backstop is therefore silently a **no-op** in the normal dev configuration (the deterministic free-text catalogue audit, the primary protection, still runs). The fix: thread the run's user down to the validator so it resolves the **same** provider the run uses, and stop silently swallowing a genuine "cannot run" failure.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] The DEC-44 fabrication validator resolves its LLM via the run's user, so under the normal user-scoped provider config it runs against the **same** provider the trio's spec calls use (no fall-through to an unconfigured default, no silent no-op).
- [ ] A genuine no-provider / cannot-run failure SURFACES (is not swallowed as an empty "no fabrications found" result); a transient provider error (timeout / rate-limit / network) or a malformed-response parse failure still degrades gracefully (returns no fabricated spans) exactly as today.
- [ ] Both entry paths thread their user — the trio run (`_run_one_spec`'s `author`) and the browser `AssistStreamView` (`request.user`); a caller that omits the user keeps today's behavior (backward-compatible, `user=None` default).
- [ ] The whole-project gate stays green and the context cascade validates.

## Approach (decided — grounded in the corroborated chain)

Thread an optional `user=None` through the exact existing chain (mirrors how `school`/`grounding` already thread through the parsers), and distinguish the cannot-run error in the validator. The chain (each adds `user: Any | None = None`, forwarding one level down):

1. **`planner/_llm_fabrication_validator.py:validate_candidates`** (sig ~83): add `user=None`; line 125 `llm = get_llm(user=user)`. Split the `except` (lines ~132): catch `NoProviderConfiguredError` (import from `ai.services.llm`) and **re-raise** it (do not return `[]`) — this is the cannot-run signal; keep the broad `except Exception → WARNING → []` for transient/other errors, and keep the `_parse_response` `except → []` (malformed response stays graceful).
2. **`planner/_freetext_audit.py`**: `audit_freetext_flags` (849), `audit_freetext` (838), `audit_prefill_flags` (1368) — add `user=None`, forward it; line 1014 → `validate_candidates(leftover_candidates, catalogue, user=user)`. Do NOT catch the re-raised `NoProviderConfiguredError` here — let it propagate (the backstop genuinely could not run).
3. **The 12 parsers** in `planner/specs.py` that call `audit_prefill_flags` (lines 751, 878, 1030, 1188, 1569, 2203, 2352, 2491, 2799, 3021, 3380, 3854): add `user=None` to each signature and pass `user=user` into `audit_prefill_flags(...)`. Add `user=None` to the remaining parsers too (so `spec.parse(..., user=…)` never `TypeError`s on a parser that doesn't audit).
4. **Entry points** pass the user into `spec.parse`: `orchestration.py:270` → `spec.parse(text, school=school, grounding=grounding, user=author)`; `views.py:619` → `spec.parse("".join(chunks), school=school, grounding=grounding, user=request.user)`.

Why this resolves it cleanly: once threaded, the validator's `get_llm(user=author)` resolves the **same** provider as the spec call's `get_llm_logged(author)`. In the normal case (the author has a provider) the validator simply works. A `NoProviderConfiguredError` is then near-unreachable — the spec call (`orchestration.py:266`) would have failed first — but if it ever occurs it now propagates as a clear config/precondition error (the resilience loop classifies `LLMTimeout`/`RateLimit`/`ValueError`/`LLMAuth`/`_FoldError`; `NoProviderConfiguredError` is none of those, so it surfaces rather than degrading every spec into a flag — the loud, correct outcome for a global config fault, like the no-active-cycle precondition).

Scope: the two other no-user `get_llm()` mentions (`ai/models/llm.py:163`, `ai/services/llm/__init__.py:12`) are docstring examples, not runtime calls — out of scope (verified). No change to `get_llm` semantics, the resilience loop, or the deterministic free-text audit logic.

## Verification (gate-first, through the harness — exactly as the prior slices)

Author/extend tests first (harness `make verify-slice SLICE=fabrication_validator` → `pytest -k slice_fabrication_validator`; new test names carry `slice_fabrication_validator`), reusing the existing `planner/tests/test_llm_fabrication_validator.py` fixtures (`FakeLLMProvider`, the `_catalogue()` helper, patching `planner._llm_fabrication_validator.get_llm`):

import logging
from unittest.mock import patch

from ai.services.llm.testing import FakeLLMProvider
from planner._freetext_audit import CatalogueUnion, build_catalogue_union
from planner._llm_fabrication_validator import validate_candidates

input.new_string:
def test_validate_candidates_returns_empty_on_transient_provider_failure(caplog):
    """Failure-falls-back-without-blocking (FGAP-050 transient class):
    a NON-config provider raise (timeout/rate-limit/network proxy) →
    `[]` + WARNING log. The validator degrades gracefully exactly as
    before for transient errors."""
    catalogue = _catalogue()
    fake = FakeLLMProvider(exc=Exception("simulated transient provider failure"))
    candidates = [("Curriculum Review Board", "The Curriculum Review Board meets.")]
    with caplog.at_level(logging.WARNING, logger="planner._llm_fabrication_validator"):
        with patch(
            "planner._llm_fabrication_validator.get_llm",
            return_value=fake,
        ):
            result = validate_candidates(candidates, catalogue)
    assert result == []
    assert any("DEC-44 validator: provider call failed" in rec.message for rec in caplog.records), [
        rec.message for rec in caplog.records
    ]


def test_slice_fabrication_validator_threads_user_into_get_llm():
    """FGAP-050 criterion 1+3: `validate_candidates(..., user=U)` resolves
    its LLM via `get_llm(user=U)` — the SAME provider the run's spec calls
    use. Patch `get_llm` to capture the `user` kwarg and assert it == U."""
    catalogue = _catalogue()
    sentinel_user = object()
    captured: dict[str, Any] = {}
    scripted = '[{"id": 0, "verdict": "fabricated"}]'
    fake = FakeLLMProvider(content=scripted)

    def _capturing_get_llm(*args, **kwargs):
        captured["user"] = kwargs.get("user")
        return fake

    candidates = [("Vice Principal for Academics", "The Vice Principal for Academics chairs.")]
    with patch(
        "planner._llm_fabrication_validator.get_llm",
        side_effect=_capturing_get_llm,
    ):
        result = validate_candidates(candidates, catalogue, user=sentinel_user)
    assert captured["user"] is sentinel_user
    assert result == [("Vice Principal for Academics", "fabricated")]


def test_slice_fabrication_validator_user_defaults_to_none():
    """FGAP-050 criterion 3 (backward-compatible): a caller that omits
    `user` resolves `get_llm(user=None)` — today's behavior preserved."""
    catalogue = _catalogue()
    captured: dict[str, Any] = {}
    scripted = '[{"id": 0, "verdict": "fabricated"}]'
    fake = FakeLLMProvider(content=scripted)

    def _capturing_get_llm(*args, **kwargs):
        captured["user"] = kwargs.get("user")
        return fake

    candidates = [("Vice Principal for Academics", "The Vice Principal for Academics chairs.")]
    with patch(
        "planner._llm_fabrication_validator.get_llm",
        side_effect=_capturing_get_llm,
    ):
        validate_candidates(candidates, catalogue)
    assert captured["user"] is None


def test_slice_fabrication_validator_no_provider_surfaces_not_swallowed():
    """FGAP-050 criterion 2: a genuine cannot-run failure
    (`NoProviderConfiguredError`) is RE-RAISED — it is NOT swallowed as
    an empty 'no fabrications found' result. The anti-fabrication backstop
    must never silently no-op when it cannot run."""
    catalogue = _catalogue()
    candidates = [("Curriculum Review Board", "The Curriculum Review Board meets.")]
    with patch(
        "planner._llm_fabrication_validator.get_llm",
        side_effect=NoProviderConfiguredError("no provider"),
    ):
        with pytest.raises(NoProviderConfiguredError):
            validate_candidates(candidates, catalogue, user=object())


def test_slice_fabrication_validator_audit_freetext_flags_propagates_no_provider():
    """FGAP-050 criterion 2 (propagation): `audit_freetext_flags(...,
    user=U)` does NOT swallow the re-raised `NoProviderConfiguredError`
    — the deterministic stages run, the leftover-validator's cannot-run
    surfaces up through the audit entry point."""
    catalogue = _catalogue()
    # Text whose capitalized-multi-word leftover spans reach the
    # leftover-validator; the fabricated proper-name run is not in the
    # small catalogue, so the walker surfaces it and the validator fires.
    text = "The Vice Principal for Academics chairs the Curriculum Review Board."
    with patch(
        "planner._llm_fabrication_validator.get_llm",
        side_effect=NoProviderConfiguredError("no provider"),
    ):
        with pytest.raises(NoProviderConfiguredError):
            audit_freetext_flags(text, catalogue, user=object())

def test_slice_fabrication_validator_parser_threads_user_end_to_end():
    """FGAP-050 criterion 1a (end-to-end): a parser invoked with `user=U`
    (as the trio's `spec.parse(..., user=author)` and the view's
    `spec.parse(..., user=request.user)` do) drives the validator's
    `get_llm(user=U)`. Uses `parse_narrative` with a fabricated
    multi-word span so the leftover walker surfaces it and the validator
    fires; patches `get_llm` to capture the threaded user."""
    from planner.specs import parse_narrative

    grounding = _grounding()
    sentinel_user = object()
    captured: dict[str, Any] = {}
    scripted = '[{"id": 0, "verdict": "ordinary-prose"}]'
    fake = FakeLLMProvider(content=scripted)

    def _capturing_get_llm(*args, **kwargs):
        captured["user"] = kwargs.get("user")
        return fake

    # A non-catalogue capitalized multi-word run surfaces to the
    # leftover-validator; one of the six narrative keys carries it.
    payload = json.dumps(
        {"vision_statement": "The Curriculum Review Board guides our work."}
    )
    with patch(
        "planner._llm_fabrication_validator.get_llm",
        side_effect=_capturing_get_llm,
    ):
        parse_narrative(payload, school=None, grounding=grounding, user=sentinel_user)
    assert captured["user"] is sentinel_user

def audit_freetext(
    text: str, catalogue: CatalogueUnion, user: Any | None = None
) -> list[str]:
    """Return the list of fabrication-candidate spans found in `text`.

    Thin delegator over `audit_freetext_flags`: returns just the span
    strings, preserving the historical `list[str]` contract and ordering
    (NER survivors first, then LLM-leftover fabrications). The structured
    per-span surface (verdict + source) lives in `audit_freetext_flags`.

    `user` (FGAP-050) forwards to `audit_freetext_flags` → the DEC-44
    leftover-validator's `get_llm(user=user)`; `None` keeps prior behavior.
    """
    return [s.span for s in audit_freetext_flags(text, catalogue, user=user)]

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def fake_fabrication_validator_llm(monkeypatch):
    """Autouse: give the DEC-44 fabrication-validator a fake provider.

    FGAP-050 made `validate_candidates` re-raise `NoProviderConfiguredError`
    (a genuine cannot-run signal) instead of silently returning `[]`. The
    planner integration tests (trio pass/finalize/command, resilience,
    save-draft, gate-closure, a1-title) drive the real 14 specs whose
    free-text audit can surface a capitalized-multi-word leftover that
    reaches the validator's `get_llm`. The test env carries no real API
    key, so without a fake the validator now (correctly) surfaces the
    cannot-run error and fails the run.

    This autouse fake mirrors `planner/tests/test_freetext_audit.py`'s
    local `mock_llm_fabrication_validator`: a `FakeLLMProvider` whose
    `complete()` counts the candidate ids in the user message and returns
    one `ordinary-prose` verdict per id — a configured provider that
    classifies the integration tests' canned, catalogue-echoing prose as
    NOT fabricated, so the deterministic audit outcome is unchanged.

    Tests that intentionally exercise the surfacing/transient paths
    (`test_llm_fabrication_validator.py`) override this with their own
    explicit `patch(...)` inside the test body; a file with its own
    autouse validator fake (`test_freetext_audit.py`) wins by closer
    scope.
    """
    import json as _json
    import re as _re

    from ai.services.llm.base import LLMResponse

    class _OrdinaryProseFake(FakeLLMProvider):
        def complete(
            self,
            messages: list,
            model: str | None = None,
            temperature: float | None = None,
            max_tokens: int | None = None,
            **kwargs: object,
        ) -> LLMResponse:
            user_content = messages[-1]["content"] if messages else ""
            ids = _re.findall(r'"id":\s*(\d+)', user_content)
            n = len(ids) if ids else 1
            verdicts = [{"id": i, "verdict": "ordinary-prose"} for i in range(n)]
            return LLMResponse(
                content=_json.dumps(verdicts),
                model="fake-haiku",
                provider="anthropic",
                usage={"input_tokens": 100, "output_tokens": 50},
            )

    monkeypatch.setattr(
        "planner._llm_fabrication_validator.get_llm",
        lambda *_args, **_kw: _OrdinaryProseFake(),
    )

planner: thread the run's user into the DEC-44 fabrication validator so it resolves the configured LLM (FGAP-050)

FGAP-050 (surfaced by the first live run_trio run): the DEC-44 anti-
fabrication validator called `get_llm()` with no user, could not see the
user-scoped openrouter LLMProviderConfig the trio's spec calls resolve
via `get_llm_logged(author)`, fell through to the unconfigured anthropic
default, raised NoProviderConfiguredError, and — being the failure-falls-
back-without-blocking path — logged a WARNING and returned []. The
backstop was therefore silently a no-op on the live path.

Aim: thread an optional `user` down the existing parse→audit→validate
chain so the validator resolves the SAME provider the run uses, and stop
swallowing a genuine cannot-run failure.

Changes (mirroring how school/grounding already thread):
- _llm_fabrication_validator.validate_candidates: add `user=None`; call
  `get_llm(user=user)`; split the provider except so
  NoProviderConfiguredError RE-RAISES (the cannot-run signal) while the
  broad except still degrades transient/other provider errors to [] +
  WARNING, and the _parse_response except still degrades malformed
  responses to []. Intent: a true no-provider precondition surfaces as a
  clear error rather than a silent empty result; transient/parse failures
  remain graceful as before.
- _freetext_audit: add `user=None` to audit_freetext / audit_freetext_flags
  / audit_prefill_flags, forwarding down to validate_candidates; these do
  not catch the re-raised NoProviderConfiguredError (it propagates).
- specs.py: add `user: Any | None = None` to all 14 spec.parse-reachable
  parsers (every parser the AssistSpec.parse Callable can dispatch to, so
  `spec.parse(..., user=...)` never raises TypeError); the 12 that audit
  pass `user=user` into audit_prefill_flags; the 2 non-auditing parsers
  (parse_propose_assignments, parse_bind_measurement_channels) accept it
  inertly.
- entry points: orchestration._run_one_spec passes `user=author`;
  views.AssistStreamView passes `user=request.user` (same user the spec
  call already resolves via get_llm_logged at views.py:607). A caller that
  omits `user` keeps today's behavior (`user=None` default).

Tests:
- test_llm_fabrication_validator: new slice tests assert the user is
  threaded into get_llm (unit + parser end-to-end), a config error
  (NoProviderConfiguredError) RAISES from validate_candidates and
  propagates through audit_freetext_flags, and transient/malformed
  failures still degrade to []. Refined the former single provider-failure
  test into the transient (degrade) case.
- test_assist_stream: the two test-double parsers (_basics_parse /
  _flagging_parse) widened to accept the keyword-only `user` the view now
  threads.
- conftest: add an autouse `fake_fabrication_validator_llm` giving the
  validator a FakeLLMProvider (one ordinary-prose verdict per candidate id)
  for the planner integration tests (trio pass/finalize/command,
  resilience, save-draft, gate-closure, a1-title). Those drive the real 14
  specs whose free-text audit can surface a leftover reaching the
  validator's get_llm; with FGAP-050 now surfacing the no-key cannot-run
  error in the keyless test env, a configured fake keeps the deterministic
  audit outcome unchanged. Files with their own autouse validator fake
  (test_freetext_audit, the catalogue-coverage suite) win by closer scope;
  the surfacing/transient tests override via explicit patch in-body.

Gate: `make verify-slice SLICE=fabrication_validator` exits 0 (slice
tests + ruff + mypy + full pytest + make test-js; context-validate
status=warnings, non-blocking).
EOF
